### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-26T12:55:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-153883>

> [rgp888 said:](/goto/post?id=153878)
>
> I answered "yes" to all the questions from the blog for how to know you are Leptin Sensitive again.
>
> The change in how I'm feeling is fantastic! It's like a miracle! It's a very small change in the "what" I am eating - a BIG change in "how" - breakfast !!!!!, less veges (I only ate green veges anyway), more fat, and three meals a day. I feel really good on three meals a day (they are pretty small) so I'm going to keep doing that! The other big change is in sleep - going to bed at 10pm and getting up at 6am. Very easy now - first week was pretty interesting! And only waking up about three times. Mentally great! Menopause symptoms greatly reduced.
>
> I have started HIIT, and feel better in myself for doing it already.

If you're Leptin Sensitive, then you don't need the Leptin Reset . Your sleep and wake times look resonable, so you're decently yoked to the light-dark cycle of your locale.

I would say breakfast is optional for you. This is relevant in your case since your stated goal is to lose weight, and skipping break to do some Intermittent Fasting can be a good way to re-structure your gut flora and give yourself a more favourable energetic environment.

Also note that summer time doesn't exactly give the best signal to shed weight. Evolutionarily speaking, we're designed to store some fat for winter, and technically speaking, we will be living with a more coupled (as opposed to uncoupled) metabolism that is not very good at getting rid of protons (body fat is a proton store). You doing CT will give yourself a good uncoupling stimulus, but it still pales in comparison to a true winter.

Sidenote: Brisbane and surrounds does get down to the low teens in winter time. While that isn't a snowy winter like some of our friends in the US are experiencing, it nonetheless is a constant cold stimulus way in excess of what you're getting now (provided that you don't turn on the heater of course)

I also think that hacking insulin action through timed refeeds makes it possible to transiently uncouple the metabolism and reduce body fat. That's going to be a topic for another day though, and I encourage caution when threading into this territory. But essentially I'm going to endorse experimentation with experimenting with fast-acting protein or carbohydrates with a reduction in dietary fat during the last 2-hours of sunshine, specifically for those people wanting to lose weight, and specifically for those who have good enough Leptin Sensitivity to make this work.

See this post in my log for some details -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-39#post-153868](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-39#post-153868) . (this is nothing new BTW, and John Kiefer and Borge Fagerli have been advocating similar strategies mainly for body composition reasons. I think refinements can be made to the protocol to support optimal health as well, but I'm still processing my thoughts )​

I will remind you that tracking calorie content is a good way of quantifying how much food you're eating, but that isn't a primary metric in figuring out how much energy your body is getting, and the signalling required from the fat mass.

As usual, the only way you're going to find out is to try it out, and stop the Intermittent Fasting if it starts giving you issues

...

But essentially I'm going to endorse experimentation with experimenting with fast-acting protein or carbohydrates with a reduction in dietary fat during the last 2-hours of sunshine, specifically for those people wanting to lose weight, and specifically for those who have good enough Leptin Sensitivity to make this work.
>
> See this post in my log for some details -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-39#post-153868](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-39#post-153868) . (this is nothing new BTW, and John Kiefer and Borge Fagerli have been advocating similar strategies mainly for body composition reasons. I think refinements can be made to the protocol to support optimal health as well, but I'm still processing my thoughts )​
>
>
> I will remind you that tracking calorie content is a good way of quantifying how much food you're eating, but that isn't a primary metric in figuring out how much energy your body is getting, and the signalling required from the fat mass.
>
> As usual, the only way you're going to find out is to try it out, and stop the Intermittent Fasting if it starts giving you issues
>
> ...

You are such a source of information Yew! Love your work!

In 2013 I lost 8kg in 5 weeks with IF. It was really easy for me to do. I followed the workout plan to a "T". Didn't do any endurance training at all. Did a 7 hour outrigger "changeover" (paddlers swap in and out of the canoe - 3.5 hours in the canoe for me) from Molokai to Oahu and paddled really well, recovered really well, even with what my team mates would've considered "no training" (if they knew!!!).

After a while I stopped losing weight, then started to slide back up, and then had trouble holding a full day fast, began losing motivation (energy) to workout....

Then I got shingles last year and fell in a big hole. I was running a massive event pretty much single handed and was quite stressed (I don't worry so much as work myself into a hole). After that the weight came back on, no energy, heaps more menopause symptoms, low mental energy, foggy mind, etc. My desperate search for some kind of relief is what brought me here!

When I finished my elite sports career (20 years of 5 hours a day training! I retired from the national team at 42!) I had adrenal fatigue - repaired myself while I was studying to be a naturopath (yes, I am qualified but not registered and I don't practice). Cortisol is my Achilles heal. While IF works for me to lose weight, I think I have to manage it better than I did previously or it just taps into my cortisol history.

I think what Inger said about electrons is really important to me! As mentioned before - I think I am very efficient metabolism wise (in a good way) - it just means that if I'm not training 5 hours a day I need to eat less than the average person. Swapping over to electron dense food has made a really huge difference to how I feel energy wise - affects motivation and desire to workout. (I naturally tend towards veges having grown up a vegan (oh and grain was mostly off my menu due to childhood issues) - and I'm mentally more of a vegan than a carnivore - I have to shop carefully for properly farmed and killed animals!).

Yew, I was plugging my food intake into Myfitnesspal for a week - around 900 - 1100 calories a day. 50-60g fat, 45-50g protein and 5 - 15g carbs (incl fibre). So, even though most calories are coming from fat, its still not a lot of food. At this stage, while I don't always feel like eating breakfast when I get up, I think its making a difference. I'm going to bring my exercise up first before trying IF again. And I think then I'll cut lunch out - make it a 12 hour/12 hour routine and maybe on every second day. What do you think of that plan?

Inger, Yew - I really appreciate your support!

Thanks
L

### Penny — 2015-02-03T17:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-154623>

Greetings I have an idea on why you're not quite electron dense enough to lose weight - and I think having shingles is a huge indicator - you still have some major gut issues - I would take a couple days - since you are one of the most disciplined people I have ever read - and eat bone broths with coconut oil and cabbage/sauerkraut - some kind of probiotic food - put seaweed in the broths - having done a few triathalons myself, trust me to say you are inflamed is the understatement of the year - even if you keep eating the way you are and just add a cup of bone broth with coconunt oil before the meal, it would have a huge benefit for you me thinks...

This is why glutamine is so big in the body building world - it helps seal the gut and cools inflammation - heck, you could even take some glutamine before your meals if you weren't into cooking broth or even use grass fed gelatin - and of course, the trick is to not exercise to the point of inflaming your gut some more - this is why you probably are prone to arthritis - you also might want to add epsom salts for the magnesium and sulfate - especially soaking any parts of you prone to arthritis -

You are doing great and I find you quite inspiring

Another thing is in the morning, cortisol is highest so it is my understanding that might not be such a hot time to exercise - I have found doing deep yoga breaths to be helpful - practicing the relaxation response - you also might want to look up Emotional Freedom Tecnique - tapping - great for lowering cortisol - the lower cortisol is, the more of everything else you make...

It only takes 36 hours to rebuild the gut, which is why I suggested taking a couple of days to work on it... I know someone who was ill and ate nothing but bone broths for 2 days and got rid of 30 years of GERD... she is 77 with 3 autoimmune diseases so I doubt it would take you long

### rgp888 — 2015-02-04T01:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-154663>

>

>

>
> It only takes 36 hours to rebuild the gut, which is why I suggested taking a couple of days to work on it... I know someone who was ill and ate nothing but bone broths for 2 days and got rid of 30 years of GERD... she is 77 with 3 autoimmune diseases so I doubt it would take you long

Wow! That's HUGE! Thank you so much for dropping by Penny and taking the time to give me such a powerful suggestion. You might have missed it because it was only one line - but - my serious childhood illness - from birth to 4 years old before I was out of trouble, and about 8 years old until I was well, and then about 18 years old before I could eat anything (almost) without consequences - I was diagnosed with cystic fibrosis - since I got better most people say I never actually had it - except I couldn't digest anything and couldn't breath - spent the first 3 months of my life in an oxygen tent. (a family friend - self taught natural hygienist - put me on a very simple diet that included a gazillion Brazil nuts, no meat (I liked the bottom of the roasting pan though!), green veges, kelp, mineral salts, no nightshades, etc. Basically cured me....I was dying from the doctors pancreatic stimulating drugs & mucous suppressing drugs)....

I have always eaten very clean because I simply can't digest garbage! And I began swimming at age four to build up my lungs (it worked - I have 5.5L lung capacity!) - it began a lifetime of big training volumes - I think being really fit helped me in a way that minimized/masked symptoms - same with always eating clean - I would never have said I have gut issues - BUT - your post made me think about it - **of course I do**! Always have had - I just look after myself - naturally without thinking about it - and minimize the impact!

Off to the shops today to buy bones, sauerkraut, some glutamine, epsom salts, more cabbage (might explain why I LOVE brussel sprouts at present!), will dump epsom salts into my CT tub as well and use the magnesium spray I have. Already got a huge jar of coconut oil (which I don't enjoy except on my skin), and of course, eating a ton of seaweed already!

I'm also going to move my office from the back room (all artificial lighting) into the front/reception area - wire the computers into the internet and shield the wi-fi router - the reception area has big windows and softer lighting which I can leave off for most of the day. I'm going to try wearing the blue blocker glasses on the nights I teach martial arts as well - see how it goes!

Thanks again Penny!!!

### rgp888 — 2015-02-05T00:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-154749>

So bone broth it is for me!

Not sure if I am doing it right?

Got a bunch of bony offcuts, and some beef bones (the shop didn't have marrow bones). Put them in the pressure cooker on slow cook for 8 hours with water, cabbage, parsley, seaweed, garlic and onion. The bones weren't very fatty so I just put them in whole.

This morning the broth was done. Put a couple of spoonfuls of coconut oil on top and a touch of salt and pepper. Nice!

Now - am I on the right track putting the cabbage, etc in the broth to cook? When I want to add sauerkraut do I just plop some on top of the bone broth? Do I eat the meaty bits that fall off the bones? Or should I just use those bare marrow bones?

Today and tomorrow on broth (and cabbage and sauerkraut and seaweed and glutamine and coconut oil) - will report on how it goes!

### rgp888 — 2015-02-10T01:58:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-155169>

Monday Routine:

Weight 86.3kg (BMI is 33.1 and BF by waist and neck measurement is 41%, and on scales, 43% - but these measurements don't work for athletes - I am very muscular - BMI was 27 when my hydro-statically weighed body fat was around 14% - blood vessels visible when relaxed on arms and legs - always had skin to spare on my tummy and only slightly defined 6 pack - need DEX to measure for real - but - I am 20kg heavier than my best adult weight, and 14 kg heavier than my race weight - I don't need it!)
Up at 6:30
Spent nearly an hour looking for my old cat - couldn't find her - somewhat distressing - she is not far off passing and I like to keep her close!
7:30 - breakfast
100g bacon, two eggs, weak black coffee
Ate outside in morning sunshine
Work - 8:00 - computer based - moved my office from the middle office (artificial lighting - fluoros) to front reception area - natural light, hardwired computers to the internet - will be getting a shield for the wi-fi router later today...
during morning more weak black coffee, chili and licorice tea, 1.5 L cold water
12:00 noon - half hour relaxing in sun
12:30 - lunch - out outdoors (shade)
cup of bone broth with added glutamine and coconut oil
fillet smoked cod with tumeric, seaweed and butter
1:30 - back to work - all in office
5:30 - HIIT
2x (3x (6 x 30"/10" rest) battle ropes, kettle bells, club bells, pin loaded machines, TRX, dumbells and body weight) 3 time through 6 exercises, and different exercises for the second set.
followed by 10 minutes easy on EWOT - blood O2 - 99, HR 110 (resting HR 50, max HR around 190).
6:15 - filled up ice bath with fresh water, epsom salts and 10 kg more ice that previously (close to 40kg of ice now)
6:30 CT - in and out a bit - not enough water - not deep enough - had to add more water a couple of times - and VERY cold!!! 15 minutes once settled - brightest red I've been so far! Shower and dress in fluffy dressing gown.
7:00 - dinner (on skype with husband who is in Japan)
lamb offcuts (the bits off the bones that are going into the next bone broth) gristle and sinewy meat with tumeric, garlic, onions, fresh cabbage and sauerkraut. (really delicious!)
Lights off - watch a little bit of big screen, get stuck on JackKruse forum...... computer has f.lux screen, deep orange by the time I finally finish
11:00pm - bed - at bit late, thanks forum!

Nice sleep - woke up twice very hot, back to sleep quickly, no aircon last night.

Let me know if you see anything that might help!!!!!

Thanks
L

### rgp888 — 2015-02-11T02:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-155307>

Tuesday
weight 86.7kg

Up at 7:00
7:30 breakfast
150g bacon 2 eggs
weak black coffee
Ate at computer - straight into work
1:00pm lunch
2 lamb chops with brocolli, rocket and sauerkraut topped with coconut oil
2:00pm - out to do some shopping (had a piccolo - single shot espresso with a touch of milk foam) - fair bit of driving around as well
5:00pm - bought some ingredients so made a chocolate treat:
3tbs coconut oil, 1 tbs cacao butter, 1 tbs butter, 2 tbs finely chopped walnuts, 1 tbs almond meal, 1 teaspoon raw organic cacao powder, 1/2 tsp organic vanilla essence and a few drops of stevia to take the edge off the bitter chocolate. Put in fridge to set.
5:30 - ate my treat - all of it (about the size of a freddo frog and a half) with a cup of licorice and cacao chili tea.
5:45 workout
did 1x(3x45"on/15"rest) 18 min total - all battle rope, trx and body weight PLUS 2 minutes on high O2 EWOT
6:30 shower and get ready for martial arts training
Didn't feel like eating (I think my coconut oil treat killed my appetite) - so didn't eat
7:30 - 10:00pm - martial arts training - I am the teacher so not as hard work for me....
10:00 pm - licorice and cacao chili tea (no "tea" in it, no caffeine) and relax
11:00pm bed
about 4L of water plus herbal teas and coffees.

good energy and mental clarity all day.

very good sleep - aircon on - I don't think I woke up at all, but awake naturally at 5am - I began thinking out how to make a slip trailed dragon decoration on the top of hand thrown vase - how to handle the decoration, what kind of fabric to do the slip trailing on - whether the fabric side should go on the outside of the vase or the inside and how to wrap the dragon design around so that it appears to be flying up out of the vase, how to get the right amount of dryness before handling it, then maybe playing with the thixotropophy of the slip mix so it can be dryer but still malleable without cracking, and how to make the dragon many colours..... so didn't get up til 6:30am....

### rgp888 — 2015-02-13T02:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-155502>

Here's what I think is going on with me.......

I have spent SO long being a totally efficient glycogen sparing/producing machine that no matter what fuel I give my body it turns it into glycogen. So I stay a sugar burner no matter what I eat. As a sugar burner it is simply calorie in/calorie out (which is why only high amounts of exercise and very low calories has ever worked for me to lose weight). While I like to be fit and strong, I don't have the time or motivation to train for 2 - 4 hours a day! One hour a day will do me just fine, when I sort myself out and turn into a fat burner!

I searched all over the internet and JackKruse.com and the forum yesterday - the best answers I could come up with are:
\- I need to eat a lot less protein - way to easy for me to convert it into glycogen
\- Need to eat a LOT more fat - Currently getting about 60-70% fat - need to up that to at least 80%.
\- In spite of my desire to keep fit - I think I have to limit exercise to the EWOT machine for the time being - just doing 4 or 5 short sprints on low O2 and the rest (15 minutes) easier with high O2. And maybe just a little heavy lifting across a broad range of movements to keep flexible and strong. I think even HIIT encourages my body to make lots of yummy glycogen!!!!
\- Carbs are currently mostly around 10 - 15g a day - to keep them under 10g means cutting out the green veges - just seaweed.

Here's what I'm planning:
Breakfast (because I am feeling a lot better from sleeping better and eating breakfast close to rising - who woulda thunk!)
Small piece of fish, seaweed, one egg, lots of butter, coconut oil and black tahini or other seed/nut butter (maybe Brazil nut butter for the selenium)
Lunch
Bone broth - seaweed, cabbage/sauerkraut, cinnamon, coconut oil, L-glutamine, and marrow and fat from the bones - hungry? - fatty meat as well - add coconut oil and nut butters
Dinner
1 - have a coconut oil, cacao butter and butter slab with cacao powder and vanilla essence
2 - only if still looking for more after the fat slab above - fatty meat or seafood with lots of butter and coconut oil, both with seaweed.

I'm also going to cut out coffee
I am drinking non-tea herbals as present - licorice, cinnamon, cacao/chili, etc - can anyone see a problem with these teas?
Currently drinking about 4L of water a day - will try taking this up to 5L
CT is only 3 times a week (due to freezing enough ice taking 48 hours)
Getting sun almost everyday (never seen my tummy brown before - I tan easily and don't burn) - been a bit rainy the past week though!
Most nights I am in bed by 10:30 (trying for 10....) and up at 6:00am - this is working well - use blue blockers after dark on nights I'm not training, and between finishing training and going to bed the other nights. Have slept through the night several times now!
I have moved my office to the front room - natural light - and hard wired my computers - shielded the wi-fi router - my phone is on but I don't use it much - it reads a few other wi-fi signals around but the signals are weak. Turn router off at night. I work with computers all day though and unfortunately that's not going to change any time soon....
Sleep at ground level instead of second story - have to sleep with the aircon on most nights or I don't sleep cos its too hot.

I "know" I am not in ketosis in spite of 6 weeks of carbs under 15g a day. My daily calorie intake is about 1000 calories a day (I usually need to get down around 800 if I want to lose weight). 70% from fat. I attribute feeling better to better quality of sleep and eating breakfast upon rising (plus CT and minimising my EMF load as much as is easily possible). I think if I changed these things without changing my diet I would feel the same - in other words I don't think the diet side of things is helping me at all! (BTW - All I have really done nutritionally speaking, is cut down on the amount of green veges, swapped chicken for fish, swapped the olive oil I put on everything for coconut oil, and not had the odd chocolate or potato chip here and there). The lifestyle changes are great! But I need something else going on with nutrition!!!!

Any suggestions?

### shah78 — 2015-02-13T02:56:22+0200

The less important food/electron/light leg has been tapped out for now. Good luck.

### rgp888 — 2015-02-26T07:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-156675>

Well - a slight change in battle plan this past week and a half - I was SO OVER gaining weight, I had couple of days introspective and thought about how I feel - and this new battle plan is working great for me!

I feel like the time spent adjusting my sleep has been very beneficial - I look to go to bed by 9:30pm - kick any lingering students out! In bed at the latest by 10:30pm and go right off to sleep! I am waking up naturally around 5 am with solid sleep in between. Usually don't get out of bed immediately - I still like to think out my day and plan a bit while still luxuriating in bed.

Also the slight change in my diet (since starting this journey) has given a massive improvement in feeling and energy (also assisted by getting good quality sleep) - Before coming here I never ate breakfast but ate lunch and dinner of a small amount of chicken or lean beef with a good serve of broccoli, brussel sprouts, rocket, avacado and olive oil with pink salt. Now I am eating breakfast and have naturally gone to eating fish almost every meal, with a bit of bone broth sometimes and the offcuts from lamb and beef once or twice a week (I like the offcuts because they are fattier, tastier, cheaper, and have a bit of gristle and sinew in them - which I chew a bit then just swallow!!). Vege wise I am almost solely on seaweed and rocket, with avocado and maybe a few brussel sprouts and sauerkraut. And not a lot of them (used to eat a lot of green veges). Mostly seaweed - can't get enough of it - when I haven't eaten enough volume in my meal I will quickly fry a few sheets of nori in butter - so good! My meals always fit onto a side dish. My gut has been VERY happy since changing diet! Also, for a while I was having a sweet treat of some organic cacao with coconut oil and organic coconut cream with a little stevia - not looking for any sweet replacement anymore (the chocolate in the supermarket makes my tummy turn to think of eating it, but I still covet a packet of chippies.... = more salt and fat required!). I was already fairly low carb (most of the time), but also fairly low fat - now definitely high fat and no carb and lowish protein. I have no clue if I am in ketosis, but really I don't care - I feel good and have bucket loads of energy.....

This past two weeks I have taken up exercise again and have stopped eating three meals a day. Just eating when hungry - and since the fatty meals and fishy meals are SO satisfying I 'm really not that hungry!

So here is my current routine:

Up when I get up - usually around 6am or 6:30 am
Quick heavy weights - just 6 exercises, 3 x 8-12 reps, nothing too strenuous - weights are heavy but I'm not pushing it - just letting the weights work my muscles and stretch through a good range of movement - three mornings a week - the other mornings I have a private student to teach in martial arts.
If I have breakfast - fish, butter, an egg and seaweed!
Work....
Three evenings a week - walk/jog on the beach - barefoot - about 30 minutes
Two evening and one morning (Saturday and after breakfast) - kayak - maybe up to 40 minutes of nice interval training.

It feels SO good to have the energy to do these light workouts - and I am beginning to feel physically competent again - certainly not fit, but I will get really fit doing this in just a few weeks.

Food wise, I am eating only when hungry - thinking to do an IF routine of 24/12/12/24/12/12/24/12/12/with three meals on the 7th day. If I feel hungry (with a check to ensure its not just thirsty, and not just a habit or indulgence - drink some water, have a cup of licorice tea, wait - is it hunger?) anywhere outside this routine, I will just have a nice meal and get on with it!

CT - nearly every day - 15 - 25 minutes around 10C - a few times I shiver for a while after but mostly just warm up. I often feel very hot a few hours after CT!!

Since I last complained about putting on 5kg, I have lost 4kg! In a week and a half of starting my new battle plan!

Quality sleep tied to sunrise and sunset, minimize blue light and EMF, maximize sunlight, high fat, CT, don't eat unless hungry, plenty of water, and moderate exercise, especially outdoors on beach and water.

I FEEL GOOD.......

### rgp888 — 2015-03-11T06:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-158176>

Haven't posted in a while!

All trundling along well.

One meal a day - fish, seaweed, butter, avocado, rocket, oysters, an egg. Maybe a snack in the afternoon - coconut oil and cacao or some salad greens.

CT three or so times a week. Blue blockers, getting to bed on time, no computers after 7pm, candles when I can.

Currently running barefoot and doing some kettle bell at dawn on the beach, and paddling kayak every night. Beginning to feel fit and whole again.

Weight is barely inching down, but at least not going up. Seems I can't eat nuts at all, and I do much better if I don't eat meat, just seafood. Also if I have too much of the coconut oil treat - weight goes up.

I have a lot of loose skin (always had it - always said it was ready for me to get fat like my mother) and the arms are the worst - however, CT seems to be toning those up! I have two frozen bottles in with me and tuck them under my arms.....

Getting cramping in my feet at night :-( Never had that before. Worse after CT.

If I slip up with the blue blockers I don't sleep so well and don't feel as good the next day - pretty obvious, huh! The nights I don't use artificial light at all sleep like a baby and feel unstoppable the next day! For feeling good this is one of my main triggers I think.

Losing weight - well, I simply can't eat very much - definitely eat like a shark, eh! They are thought to eat every 12 days only if you base it on how far they swim and how much they get from eating a seal - but they are also maybe only eating once every 30 days, depends on the research and if they assume calories or simply observe!

My workouts are not very intense (can't run very fast barefoot yet and paddling just warming the base fitness up before going intense), but I manage them very easily on the food I am currently eating. I got asked to do the Molokai Na Wahine O Ke Kai again this year - so time to get fit!!!

And the cramping I'm experiencing is getting really annoying - feet, hands and abs! Nails are weaker than ever before as well! I read somewhere else on this forum where people were having problems with mineral deficiencies........sounds like me....

My biggest gains have come from better sleep. Everything else?????? Jury is out!

### rgp888 — 2015-06-18T10:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-ms-optimal.12443/post-168719>

OK - for past six months pretty much been eating salmon and oysters with an egg, avocado, seaweed (nori) (all cooked on low heat in NZ butter) and fresh baby spinach and rocket for breakfast. No lunch or just a snack (butter fried seaweed or coconut oil and a teaspoon of greens and cacao, or a handful of nuts, or some left overs, or salad, or bone broth...) with dinner usually bone broth with some green veges and a bit of the slow cooked meat from the bone broth. Walking on the beach at sunrise, sometimes jog, might do a bit of weights at night - not much exercise and not intense. 3L of water a day with at least 1.5L of it Italian water (rest filtered). Lights out and candle light after dark, blue blockers on if I have to be in light. Internet off at night, and computer hard wired during day - work online. No stress and handle stress well emotionally, but physical stress can get me run down.

My own history is checkered - in oxygen tent first 3 months of life not able to breath and not able to digest. No real clear diagnosis but not expected to live. Pancreatic stimulants, maybe diagnosed with cystic fibrosis. At age 4 my parents took me away from medical doctors to a naturopath and by 8 I was pretty much well. I began swimming at 4 and became an elite athlete - nationally ranked in swimming for over 10 years and then swapped to sprint kayak at 31 yo. Became a dual Olympian in that sport - retired at 42. From 8 - 48 no health issues what so ever. Very fit, very strong, very well. But I always ate mostly fruit, veges and meat, not much processed food.

From 42 - 48 put on about 5kg. At 48 I had an ovarian cyst that wouldn't go away so had my right ovary removed by keyhole surgery - then my uterus started playing up very badly and had that removed a year later - leaving just the left ovary. Took quite a while to recover from that surgery - about 3 years until I felt really well. Went through to 55 without any issues. From 48 - 55 put on about 5kg (now 10kg overweight).

Last year I got shingles after doing three months of a very stressful job to pull off a big event that didn't seem possible but I did it! 5 weeks after shingles I did a 42 km team race in very hot conditions and wound up in hospital with severe potassium deficiency. Felt like I had chronic fatigue after that - 6 months of it - lead me here!

Changes to sleep patterns, light exposure, sun time, beach time, and reduction in exercise recovered the chronic fatigue feeling within a week! Dietary changes are smaller as I ate pretty well all my life. I can go to under 20g of carbs very easily - don't have energy issues no matter how little I eat or how low carbs go - I question if I go into ketosis because I don't feel any different. Before here I wasn't eating breakfast and would have two meals of chicken or beef with green veges and olive oil, two pieces of fruit a day, a little yoghurt or cheese - no milk or bread or processed food.

Since I started here I was feeling really good - but no weight loss! Experimenting around how little to eat, how much intense exercise to do, when to do it, when to do CT (actually stopped CT when it was really hot - seemed counter intuitive to try to cold adapt in the hottest part of summer....) - nothing I changed moved any weight - if I eat too much I put on weight, but no matter how little I eat or how hard I work out I don't lose any! Regardless - I was feeling great!

Mother - just passed away at 90 years of age - morbidly obese all her life but no real health issues - emotional problems but physically strong - she died from congestive cardiac failure (old age). Her mother was ridiculously obese - she died at 52 but I don't know what from - obesity related - they had to take a wall of the house out to remove her body. My mother's father was a chronic asthmatic and died at 60 something. My mother and I did not communicate as she blamed me for her problems - being so ill I impacted on her freedom I think - so I didn't ever find out much about my health issues, her health issues, or her mother's issues but I do believe they both had ovarian cysts and uterine problems.

Father - still going strong at 95 years old. He was a two plus pack a day smoker all his life til he had an aortic aneurism at 63 - gave up smoking and never looked back! My father's father died of a heart attack young. My father's mother was half aboriginal (it was never spoken of) and she lived to 97. She had a gangrene related amputation of part of her foot (all of her siblings to the aboriginal mother had the same thing). She died from a stroke.

One of my brothers died of a heart attack at 46 - again a heavy smoker, drinker, poor diet, obese and a race car driver to boot! Two other brothers are alive and in their late 60's but I don't have enough to do with them to know how they are doing.

Ancestry is German on my mother's side and English and native Australian on my father's side.

So now I have another ovarian cyst at nearly 57 years old - pain is not fading and I'm worried that it is bleeding and forming tumors like the right one did. I didn't think my poor little remaining ovary was still working! When I read about PCOS I can say yes to most of the symptoms and apparently it is not unusual for female athletes to be silent sufferers of PCOS. As an athlete I rarely had a period, I have very oily skin with mild acne even now, very bad acne as a teenager, and too much hair in the wrong places!

What to do?

Thanks Caroline!

Live in Burleigh Heads QLD - actually in an industrial estate - no smart meters and everything shuts down at night. No close neighbors. I also live in Japan - Noda is considered the countryside but it looks like a large small town to me! Spent more time in QLD the past two years than in Japa, but plan on spending more time in Japan in the next year.

Try to read the blogs but they don't always make a lot of sense to me, except for the fact they "seem to make sense" - I have trouble pulling out of the deep ideas things that relate to me - it takes a lot of reading! I have the epi paleo rx and that is what i have put into practice as much as I understand. No webinars or podcasts - out of reach of my budget!

Jack thank you for posting this. The video is a real thought-provoker. I am at a cross-roads in deciding to live completely off-grid, subsistance hunting and fishing, etc. This may require a change in my relationships and some hard choices which I am really struggling with. Your book and this site go so far beyond the Paleo concept and adds a much needed balance to all of that. Thank you and Happy Holidays to you and your family.

### Josh — 2014-12-22T21:10:21+0200

We are always under the tree, found it in a dumpster in Boulder 18 years ago.....the cactus blooms every year too at this time, 17 years and counting.....

12 years ago I sat on the roof of a house in a small town in North India in view of the Himalayas and watched all 3 Matrix movies with my teacher....The next morning a friend asked if we were in the Matrix and he responded "you find out..." More than one tradition has characterized this planet as a "prison planet" akin to Australia for this part of the universe...so just what are we all doing here????

Gurdjieff said that the next planet we go to when we graduate is so blissful and calm that it is almost impossible to get anyone to do or learn anything.....

# Thread: Electromagnetic Sensitivity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-12-23T02:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150825>

I celebrated the Winter Solstice yesterday by experiencing a new form of electrosensitivity, with a little brain damage possibly thrown in. I got a flush on the skin of my upper arms and face, with a feeling of heat, and slight prickling. Later I got songs stuck in my head, and today as well.

The older half of our country house has just one electrical outlet per room, at chest height. They were all ungrounded until earlier this year when DH upgraded the wiring to most of the house. The outlet on the enclosed back porch was not upgraded, though. We recently installed a washer & dryer, and I have used the washer a few times with no apparent issues.But yesterday I was just starting a load, and the washer was filling with water, when I felt a fairly intense heat, like strong sunlight, on my arms. I looked around, then realized it must be the electrical outlet! In the mirror I saw my face and arms looked a bit sunburned. About thirty minutes outside relieved the sensation. I didn't get any ringing in the ears or throat issues. But later that day I started getting songs stuck in my head (and today also) and sleep was interrupted for two bathroom breaks, which is unusual.

I could Ask Jack, but I know what he'd say. Fix the outlet! And ground more.

I'm a little surprised that the problem occurred when the solenoid was activated to let in the water, instead of the motor running. But that's got a strong magnetic field too.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-23T17:27:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150861>

probably affected the magnetic moment of the protons in you.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-23T18:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150862>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=150861)
>
> probably affected the magnetic moment of the protons in you.

Is that why I just said "red" when referring to a picture of a brown house? I've been briefly forgetting words too, but not as bad as I've experienced in the past.

I've slept twice now on a magnetico, but I think grounding is key. Unfortunately I'm back in the city for Christmas and to do a final cleaning of the house. Spectacular sunset last night.

Bet my body (& mitochondria) are happy that they have figured out another way to communicate with my hard head. I still feel a slight tingle in my arms occasionally. This reminds me of the time I connected with an electric fence barefoot on damp ground. Lol!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-23T18:49:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150863>

### Alex97232 — 2014-12-24T22:35:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150937>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=150825)
>
> I celebrated the Winter Solstice yesterday by experiencing a new form of electrosensitivity, with a little brain damage possibly thrown in. I got a flush on the skin of my upper arms and face, with a feeling of heat, and slight prickling. Later I got songs stuck in my head, and today as well.
>
> The older half of our country house has just one electrical outlet per room, at chest height. They were all ungrounded until earlier this year when DH upgraded the wiring to most of the house. The outlet on the enclosed back porch was not upgraded, though. We recently installed a washer & dryer, and I have used the washer a few times with no apparent issues.But yesterday I was just starting a load, and the washer was filling with water, when I felt a fairly intense heat, like strong sunlight, on my arms. I looked around, then realized it must be the electrical outlet! In the mirror I saw my face and arms looked a bit sunburned. About thirty minutes outside relieved the sensation. I didn't get any ringing in the ears or throat issues. But later that day I started getting songs stuck in my head (and today also) and sleep was interrupted for two bathroom breaks, which is unusual.
>
> I could Ask Jack, but I know what he'd say. Fix the outlet! And ground more.
>
> I'm a little surprised that the problem occurred when the solenoid was activated to let in the water, instead of the motor running. But that's got a strong magnetic field too.

Hi non--sorry to hear of your not so fun adventure with the washer-dryer emf's. I am kind of stuck living in my old house in Portland for the past year. While I am here, this is what I have found that seems to work for me to avoid having the reactions you described: I leave the circuits for washer and dryer off most of the time. When I am going to do a wash, I set the washer to the setting I want (circuits still off); then, at the other end of the basement where the electric panel is, I flip the washer circuit to on (I use a long wooden stick to do this); when I hear that the water has finished filling up, I flip the breaker to off, then, with power off around the machines again, I load the clothes and close the top; then, go back to the panel, and flip the circuit to on for the washer.
Dryer is the same drill.
Hope the above is helpful to you--I have more tricks to surviving the emf nightmare.-- I am happy to share--just ask.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-25T03:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electromagnetic-sensitivity.12449/post-150953>

> [Alex97232 said:](/goto/post?id=150937)
>
> Hi non--sorry to hear of your not so fun adventure with the washer-dryer emf's. I am kind of stuck living in my old house in Portland for the past year. While I am here, this is what I have found that seems to work for me to avoid having the reactions you described: I leave the circuits for washer and dryer off most of the time. When I am going to do a wash, I set the washer to the setting I want (circuits still off); then, at the other end of the basement where the electric panel is, I flip the washer circuit to on (I use a long wooden stick to do this); when I hear that the water has finished filling up, I flip the breaker to off, then, with power off around the machines again, I load the clothes and close the top; then, go back to the panel, and flip the circuit to on for the washer.
> Dryer is the same drill.
> Hope the above is helpful to you--I have more tricks to surviving the emf nightmare.-- I am happy to share--just ask.

Thanks Alex; good idea. When we go back from the city, we are buying a new grounding rod for the house. I had been avoiding the washer, turning it on and quickly walking away, but I didn't realize the motor wasn't the only threat.

I also like the idea of using a long wooden stick on breakers.
My dryer is usually powered by solar and wind energy, heh, but I use the dryer if it is raining.

# Thread: Megnetico Mattress Pad User

### Crissie — 2014-12-24T20:10:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megnetico-mattress-pad-user.12459/post-150933>

Hi all,

I have been using this mattress pad since 2011. Honestly, I cannot say that I have experienced any positive results. What I have noticed is if I go out of town (vacation or business) I do not sleep well. So I feel like I may be addicted to this pad, which is really not ideal. I am thinking of purchasing a larger bed, do not want to buy a large pad, and feel like I will have difficulty sleeping.

Also, the only reviews I can find on this are from distributors, aside from one on Curezone who had the same concerns I do.

Is there anyone with specific experience who is not a distributor or somehow related to the Magnetico company?

Thanks!

### shah78 — 2014-12-24T22:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megnetico-mattress-pad-user.12459/post-150935>

I intend to die on my Magnetico. Is that a strong enough endorsement......It has turned 30 years of constant distracting pain into an occasional annoyance and an infrequent need for some top flight body work..... I've only had it for nine weeks and it keeps getting better each week.

Are you following the leptin reset or minding your circadian cycle??!?

I don't dry much water (3-4 cups / day), and am not sure what the cireadian cycles are.

BTW, it sound like you have been on a transformation - I'm impressed. Are you using the Magnetico sleep pads?

I travel for business several times a year, and don't sleep well when I am out of town. I am thinking it is the pads.

### Crissie — 2014-12-26T04:07:30+0200

### Crissie — 2014-12-26T04:14:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megnetico-mattress-pad-user.12459/post-151006>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=150999)
>
> I will put it another way for you. Your focus sleep,magnetico is too narrow. Did you sleep well before the magnetico? were conditions the same,were you ill stressed,are you now? When you travel how do you travel? Are your bed times the same? Little things and context matter.

I did sleep well before the pad. Occasionally, I would and do still have difficulty sleeping at home. If I am stressed, or sometimes if I forget and leave my cell phone on, I might not sleep as well.
Putting these sorts of ebbs and flows aside, I reliably do not sleep well when I am out of town. Even on the most relaxing vacations. I travel for business and am out perhaps one night or at the most two nights a few times a year (5-7 trips). I vacation and typically state in the US and take relaxing vacations, such as Sedona.

So, I am thinking that if the pad has created sort of an addiction for me, it is not good. I don't want that type of affliction. If numerous independent folks on this tell me they use the pad and do not have difficulty sleeping without it, I might think it is something else for me. But, I have not gotten that feedback.

Thank you!!

I can recall being on vacation and sleeping like a baby, so am thinking something else is impacting it. Thanks,

### nonchalant — 2014-12-30T16:08:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/megnetico-mattress-pad-user.12459/post-151181>

Crissie, there are three things that all life everywhere needs. Sunlight, water chemistry, and magnetism. If you had previously been strictly avoiding sunlight, like they tell you to, but decided to throw caution to the wind and started getting sunlight regularly, you would find that without good sun exposure, you don't sleep as well. Same with water.

The earth's magnetism we experience has been steadily declining recently, so the Magnetico is good for everyone. But if you had other bad environmental factors that interfered with the body's ability to sense the earth's hourly changing magnetic field, you would need the Magnetico much more than someone else in a different location. Like missing sunlight or water, you would find that you do not sleep well without it.

Scientists look for planets that have 3 factors - water, magnetism, and starlight - when they are looking for any sort of life. Those 3 factors are essential for humans, and all life on Earth.

For your situation, I would guess that you are in a location that really interferes with your ability to benefit from Earth's magnetism. The magnetico will help you quite a bit, but it would be even more important for you to move to a better location.

### Crissie — 2014-12-30T16:46:50+0200

Hi and Thanks. I live about 30 minutes outside of Chicago.

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-12-25T03:37:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-150952>

What are some possible reasons why my stomach may feel sore after eating raw oysters. I have been eating 2 per day. My stomach doesn't get sore straight away but normally by the second or third day of eating eating them. The pain is not strong.... just a fairly mild discomfort. Would it help if I cooked them....at least until my gut is able to handle them?

### Lahelada — 2014-12-25T11:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-150970>

Could it be that your discomfort comes from the shifting population of your gut microbiome? I wonder about this but the pain makes me hesitant to recommend sticking with it. hmm...

### nicld — 2014-12-25T22:11:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-150997>

Interesting. I had a dozen raw yesterday and another dozen raw today and no real discomfort but I also had some salmon with it.

### Ben1983 — 2014-12-26T08:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-151012>

A whole dozen wow....I've been having 2/day....but have temporarily stopped.

### nicld — 2014-12-26T17:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-151016>

Right now, I can get shucked raw oysters for about $10.99 a pound which is about 2 dozen.

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-07-21T18:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-246354>

I LOVE raw oysres and clams - when I was 6-years-old I ate them by the gallon happily but now in adulthood I have hemochromatosis (iron overload) so can't eat ANY raw shellfish because the bacterium called Vibrio Vulnificus thrives on iron and could kill me. You have no idea how envious I am reading your wonderful comments about guzzling down a couple of dozen but fortunately I like them cooked also (just not as much).

### JanSz — 2018-07-23T17:04:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-246518>

> [Christina Gagnon said:](/goto/post?id=246354)
>
> I LOVE raw oysres and clams - when I was 6-years-old I ate them by the gallon happily but now in adulthood I have hemochromatosis (iron overload) so can't eat ANY raw shellfish because the bacterium called Vibrio Vulnificus thrives on iron and could kill me. You have no idea how envious I am reading your wonderful comments about guzzling down a couple of dozen but fortunately I like them cooked also (just not as much).

Vibrio Vulnificus thrives on iron and could kill me.
/////////////////////////////////////////////////
Wonder what Jack would have to say
if you asked him at Ask Jack.

//////////
[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

/

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-07-24T16:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-246598>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=246518)

> if you asked him at Ask Jack.

[@JA](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/347/)

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=246518)

> /////////////////////////////////////////////////

> if you asked him at Ask Jack.

>

I would like to know what Jack thinks too but I'm guessing there is really nothing I can do about it. I have phlebotomies still but not as many as I had when I was first diagnosed. Believe or not I was giving up three pints of blood a week with no problem because I had so much iron in my blood.

### Jan Christer — 2018-09-19T21:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-251359>

Just watch out for potential zinc overdosing when eating oysters. Since oysters are very high in zinc, the daily upper tolerance of 40 mg per day can easily be exceeded unless one monitors the intake.
Intake also depends on variety of oysters, e.g. eastern wild oysters contain 74-182 mg zinc per 100 g serving, while Pacific oysters only contain 17-33 mg zinc per 100 g serving.

More info:
[https://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-015124000000000000000-w.html](https://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-015124000000000000000-w.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-19T21:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-stomach-from-raw-oysters.12461/post-251360>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=150952)
>
> What are some possible reasons why my stomach may feel sore after eating raw oysters. I have been eating 2 per day. My stomach doesn't get sore straight away but normally by the second or third day of eating them. The pain is not strong.... just a fairly mild discomfort. Would it help if I cooked them....at least until my gut is able to handle them?

I never argue with the ignorant, because people watching on social media may not be able to tell the difference due to their own dopamine state.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-150962>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This is the picture of what is left of a friends house after we had tornado's in the Gulf South. If you can help out please do so. We are taking donations at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) via paypal. If you want to send the family a gift card at amazon they are coming to our house at ten AM to shop on my laptop. Clothing and shoes would also be helpful. Aaliyah Howard, Alexis Howard, Cornelius Howard. youngest has size 8 pants 13 shoes 13 yr old petite teen size pants size 8 shoe, 10.5 female shirt oldest XL shirt 9.5-10 shoes. They need clothing and shoes most now. Cash is also helpful. For those who have already reached out to them thank you from the bottom of my heart. They also could use male 2XL shirts 40/32 pants; size 18 ladies pants.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-25T15:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-150975>

Thanks for the second choice. Amazon is easier for me than dealing with Paypal.

### fitness@home — 2014-12-25T15:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-150976>

Their home is destroyed. Truly hope that they are all okay (physically) and that any pets made it through too.

Grew up in midwest and witnessed firsthand the power of tornadoes. In 1973 a tornado picked up my aunt and uncles home and dumped it in the middle of the road. They were asleep and never knew it was coming. Aunt Alma spent the rest of her life in a wheelchair.

Glad the Howard's have friends like your family to help, especially today on Christmas.

### caroline — 2014-12-27T03:03:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-151030>

How much destruction did this tornado cause? Haven't been watching the news.

### nicld — 2014-12-27T21:17:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-can-help-please-consider-it.12462/post-151066>

I think 4 people lost their lives  Not massive destruction but enough that peeple lost homes. Just so sad.

# Thread: new beginnings, everyday blessings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 84
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rlee314 — 2014-12-27T21:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-151069>

aloha all,
time to start a journal.
i've been reading dr kruse's blogs for a few years, checking in on the forum from time to time too. it wasn't until the optimal reset at the beginning of 2014 that i really got into and inspired to commit to epi-paleo and all the rest of it.
bit of intro--i'm 35, mother to a jovial 4 year old boy. we live on maui and enjoy 'island life'.
as a child i was sick often. vaginal birth, 2nd born, breastfed for 1 year. tonsils out at age 5, chronic pneumonia from years 6-8. (started having symptoms of gluten intolerance at age 11 though didn't make the connection of why i hated pizza til 16 years later). born and raised in utah, moved to idaho at 15-18, moved to oregon at 18-31, moved to maui 5 months pregnant at 31. wisdom teeth out at 29, kidney stones a year later. on BCP for 10 years. after going off BCP hormones fluctuated a bit, eczema appeared on face--that's when i decided to go 'low gluten', skin improved, pregnant a few months later. excellent pregnancy and birth-home birth, breastfed baby for 3.5 years--around the time my boy weaned is when i started having some health issues...noticed pain/tenderness in right breast and lump, determined it was plugged milk ducts from weaning...around the same time i decreased dosage of lugols iodine--i had previously been supplementing 50mg for 6 months. breast pain, tenderness and lump appeared to be cyclical--assumed was related to decreased iodine and possibly hormones. traveled to utah to visit family--was careless w/my diet, returned home and became ill w/a stomach virus---recovered from stomach virus but gut felt 'off'. post stomach virus symptoms of sinus infection started. dealt w/breast pain/tenderness, lump/cyst and sinus infection symptoms from july-october. beginning of october i felt getting some labs would be a good idea--i posted the results in the 'optimal labs forum'. not great results. TPO ab, not great thyroid, elevated TSH, clear indication of chronic bacterial infection etc. at this point i became VERY serious about epi-paleo and avoiding nnEMF, CT and grounding every day. I ate seafoods 2-3x/day for several weeks--feeling awesome. labs improved-though TSH increased, i did increase lugols dose in addition to eating seafood every day, also increased selenium dose, infection cleared-mostly....
so...after getting my labs back and realizing i was dealing w/a chronic infection-i really should have done something about it before i had the labs done-and realizing that i may be developing an AI-dis-ease/hashis i was pretty depressed and discouraged. i also assumed w/my labs and elevated TSH that i was infertile--i had been thinking of considering another child for several months. well...much to my surprise-shock really-i got a + pregnancy test on november 9th, just one month after getting the horrible lab results! obviously not the 'optimal' time to get pregnant. (this is certainly not the optimal pregnancy i was hoping for/planning for) up until the week of thanksgiving i was continuing w/my seafood diet 2-3x/day, avocado, raw onion and raw garlic + salad greens...then the feelings of nausea and not much appetite started to set in and the thought and smell of seafood was repulsive. Other than not being able to continue w/the daily seafoods intake i've felt pretty good. this past week i've been able to start eating some seafoods again-sardines wrapped in seaweed, some salmon and shrimp too.
i had some labs done again about a month ago--vast improvement in most areas from the previous labs done in october. vitamin d level is 70, metabolic panel looked good except for alkaline phosphatase is low, mcv is still a bit elevated, hemoglobin and hemotocrit are low/normal at 12.5 and 37.3. LDH is also low at 121 and TSH is elevated at 4.7
reading Kovita's optimal journal and the lovely words expressed to her when she announced her pregnancy have been very inspiring for me. my ND said it's quite a miracle i conceived w/my previous labs, infection and TSH and likely thyroid issues-though i am inclined to think the thyroid may be linked to the infection-as i don't have symptoms of hypothyroid-and reading about iodine, perhaps the increased TSH is due to supplementing iodine?
anyhow...hoping from this point forward i will be able to have a healthy and enjoyable pregnancy. i contine to CT-in the ocean several times/week. ground daily. do daily epsom salts baths. trying to focus on the blessings that a baby brings and not the unfortunate state of my health...though, i'm not going to be ignorant about that either.

thanks for reading....

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T00:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154298>

thanks all!!

so, i have a positive update-i think-on some of my labs.
got TSH retested a week ago and it went from 4.7 in november to 2.5 i'm not exactly sure what pregnancy does to the thyroid or TSH? but, i'm interpreting this as a good thing?

also, my irons increased from november as well. had labs done on 1/7
hemoglobin 11/25 was 12.5 now, 1/7, 13.4
hemotocrit 11/25 was 37.3 now, 1/7, 39.5
i'm wondering if epsom salts baths and lots of sun could have improved irons? i thought i read something from dr seneff -and dr kruse- that iron is connected to sulfate?

anyhow. feeling pretty great overall. i'm able to eat seafood daily-ideally multiple times/day-again now. i wasn't able to stomach my beloved salmon and shrimp for a few weeks. and i was told at a restaurant they would NOT serve me oysters since i was pregnant--my fault for opening my mouth bc at the time i didn't look pregnant!

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T01:08:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154301>

what's not been so great is sleep. sigh. wondering about adenosine. safe for pregnancy? also curious about DHEA. safe to supplement? how can i increase? it was low on labs from october-before conception.
part of the issue may be that i still co-sleep w/my 4 year old. i'm trying to transition him to his own bed--slowly.
i don't eat carbs past 6. i wear blue blockers at night. and candle light after sunset. i was doing good w/not having to get up in the night to urinate--i normally never do, but, now being 14 weeks pregnant i am waking about 3am to urinate. should a pregnant woman be able to go all night w/out needing to urinate? so, sleep from 3-6am is not great.

thoughts or suggestions on how to improve sleep?

also, i feel like my glutathione levels have improved...but, recently reading how important glutathione/GSH is during pregnancy. would a transdermal or liposomal form be ok to do? i know sulfur is also connected to glutathione--maybe the sulfur-epsom salts baths and garlic and onions-really is doing me good? looking up old threads for the sulfur/iron connection.

### rlee314 — 2015-01-31T07:11:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154334>

just reread the hasi/melasma blog. holy fucking hell!! this is/was me. i'm going to go w/was b/c i feel soooo much better. and my labs seem to have improved--thanks to tons of grounding. epsom salts, iodine, selenium, dha, epi-paleo, positive thoughts, blue blockers, electrons from the sun and ocean.

quote from the blog. ""The amino acid tyrosine is a precursor for thyroid hormones, DHEA, and dopamine synthesis. An enzyme activated during chronic bacterial infection (think altered quorum sensing in the gut due to synthetic estrogens) can deplete tyrosine levels, which is why there is an association between low thyroid function, DHEA, and chronic sinus infections. It also lowers iodine and lithium levels. This is why Hashimoto’s can be a gateway disease to many other diseases like PCOS, Multiple Sclerosis, Devic’s syndrome, or anxiety/depression too." question for dr jack or anyone else who knows. what is the enzymre that is activated during chronic bacterial infection?

### kovita — 2015-01-31T09:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-154345>

good to have another pregnant woman here! I have chronic infection (lyme), I was very afraid when I found I wa pregnant, considering a lot... So far so good (26 w now) , actually much better than anyone could expected. I feel great! Raw fish, seafood and oysters are my stable, I eat sea vegetables almost with every meal as well, iodine recomendations for pregnancy are extra underestimated. My hormone levels were excellent during the first and second trimester, i am about to see what the entrance e to the third trimester will fo to the hormone demand. I actually feel healthy after years of being sick. I do not meditate much about why is it so, I am emjoying it and nurturing it and I have no intentions to come back where I was before. I have already plans for BHRT after the delivery to avoid the hormonal swing, which could hurt me big time.

### rlee314 — 2015-05-26T21:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-165459>

so...reading UBI 10 again...and UBI 12...bc i am thinking/feeling that my baby-almost 33 weeks-is getting hiccups sometimes. at night mostly it seems. and, i've also been experiencing what i assume is heartburn? reflux maybe? i honestly don't know bc i have only ever experienced heartburn/reflux during the last few weeks of my previous pregnancy-and that seemed to be triggered by foods-specifically tomatoes...so, i'm wondering if both of these symptoms are 'red flags' that something is not quite right w/me and baby...or, if both are perhaps a 'normal' part of pregnancy caused by organs being shifted-reflux-and baby learning to breathe-hiccups.

i myself do not hiccup-though the reflux has caused some. and like i mentioned i don't experience heartburn or reflux.
i've also been yawning some--but, i experience interupted sleep due to waking to urinate 1-2x during the night. other than that i feel like i have good energy levels and aside from the slight reflux and possible hiccups from baby i feel digestion is good.

### rlee314 — 2015-05-29T01:42:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-165726>

thanks hope.

the reflux has subsided thankfully. i spent a few days at the beach, did some epsom salts baths and have been drinking more water.
i was about ready to biohack my diet--which i feel like is pretty good. though, idk. i eat fish 2-3x/day and have pretty much the entire pregnancy. i live in hawai'i and while i \*could\* be eating a variety of fruits i don't much. i don't really crave fruits. i don't really have any pregnancy cravings--which friends feel is odd. but, i didn't have pregnancy cravings my last pregnancy either. so..idk? i've been doing a mostly leptin type method of eating most of the pregnancy. i just wake up hungry-bc i don't eat past 6pm-and have just always felt better in general if i eat a BAB. though the past few weeks it takes me a while-like an hour or more-to eat the BAB. i usually eat it in 2 installments. since the reflux-which was only awful and noticable for a day or 2-i've not been eating so much at breakie...just breaking it up a bit. so, i feel like i'm eating regularly from about wake-up, 30 min of waking til about 9 or 10 am, so that's like 3-4 hours of eating kindof.

### caroline — 2015-05-29T02:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-165731>

How is your son?

### rlee314 — 2015-06-10T05:59:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-167331>

thanks caroline. i'm hoping so!!

my boy is doing great!! his issues-fat absorption-and some tooth decay have improved!! stools are perfect bristol 4s at least 2x/day. so, i'm super stoked about that!
tooth decay is improving. i actually need to take photos of where they're at now-should have taken them a few months ago to compare-so i can compare in a few weeks/months. epi-paleo for the win!!! seriously so amazing!!
another amazing thing--i don't want to jinx myself here--i should add this to the optimal kids forum--but, the past month or so ALL of his friends have had this bug or that bug, fevers, ear infections, coughs etc--he has NOT!! what are the differences with these kids and him? what i know is he rarely watches ANY TV/screens. IF he does it's during the day-never at night. he doesn't have a kindle, ipad etc--he's freaking 4 after all--why the hell does he need one? (he really wants one bc ALL of his friends have one or more) and--he is an epi-paleo kid every day. we eat fish at least 1x if not 2x/day, every. single. day. his friends--ALL have some sort of screen device. and, their diet is different, completely.

will be interesting to see what this baby is like.
i started hard core epi paleo several weeks prior to conception and have continued throughout the pregnancy...so, loads of DHA-maybe not the most optimal forms per se--lots of sardines, smoked salmon, salmon, raw salmon, and shrimp have been my main sources for fish/dha. anyhow..i've also had exposure to natural blue light-i live in hawai'i, so, lots of sun=blue light. and of course being online-blue light exposure, though i have filtered that w/screen filters and blueblockers for the past several months also. i'm just curious how much DHA was wasted due to my exposure to bluelight?
and...since my sleep has not been super awesome the past few weeks--due to discomfort and some reflux and waking to urinate 1-3x during the night-i'm curious about the melatonin exchange between me and baby. also curious if i've been using/stealing baby's adrenals--i know this was discussed some in the optimal reset Q&A.

anyhow...almost 36 weeks...looking forward to meeting baby soon. i had my son at 38 weeks...so, curious to see when this one arrives.

### rlee314 — 2015-07-05T22:49:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-170959>

sigh.
38 weeks and feeling a bit discouraged.
at last weeks apt my hemoglobin was tested via finger prick-came back under 9. i know that iron lowers due to blood volume expansion during pregnancy. but, unless the device used to measure this was way off-which is possible i guess-then my hg has gone down significantly in the last month--it was a little over 10 when checked about a month ago.

rereading the redox rx i came across this.
"12. Your **hemoglobin and hematocrit, called an H/H level,** can tell you if you have a lowered redox potential (you do if you are anemic)."

i'm curious how this applies to pregnancy? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) my midwives didn't seem terribly alarmed but noted they wanted to see the level come up and would be cautiously watching post delivery for signs of too much bleeding and consider a possible pitocin shot. i'd really like to avoid this. but, of course also desire a safe birth.

really hoping my redox is not low.

### rlee314 — 2015-08-07T01:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-174145>

happy to introduce my aloha baby girl!!!!! (i was a bit surprised she's a girl...the entire pregnancy and before i actually thought she'd be a girl but most everyone else would look at me and say boy. my little boy was also pretty set on having a brother...but he's pretty pleased w/his little sister.

she arrived on 7/15 at dawn, 6:02am. 7'7 18.5 inches
i called the midwives at 3:30. they arrived at 4am. she was born 2 hours later.
she came very aware. she cried for about 1 min and then stopped and just looked around.

she is very strong. holds her head up well wants to stand. can roll from side ti side well.
nursing is going great on the left breast, however the right breast has been a struggle w/mastitis and plugged milk ducts.

she is very content. her circadian rhythm at night iss almost too good. for the first several days she would sleep the entire night w/out waking. not so great for breastfeeding, mastitis, engorgement etc.

she is my LIGHT baby.

born at dawn on a beautiful sunny day.
her name is Ilee Solana (eye-lee)
Ailey is irish meaning light. i'm spelling it differently--my middle name is lee. i also think the irish pronunciation might be different.
Solana is spanish meaning sunshine.
Sol=sun
Lana is hawai'ian meaning of calm waters.

i spent a lot of time in the water during my pregnancy with her. i was in the ocean the day before giving birth. i had a water labor but stood up to birth her so she was born above the water. she's very calm and alert.
she naturally wakes at her time of birth every day and stays awake and alert for a few hours. the morning hours are her most awake, alert parts of the day so far-3 weeks in no. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### kovita — 2015-08-07T14:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-174177>

The hemoglobin levels, I had some decline during the winter too, but it went to 11,5 at the delivery. I did some plant based isron supps, very mild to see such a good levels again, but it was probably necessary since I ate liver but it did not go up and the iron metabolism showed iron reserve depletion. I continue with this herbal approach now and my baby does not look anemic, so I assume it works. We spend a lot of time naked in the morning and late afternoon sun. The summer is very hot here, unusually hot. My baby started to reach for the things before age of two months and is now able to catch them. He continues sleeping so well in the night (1 max 2 feedings) that it is a real pleasure to share a bed with him. I feel blessed while listening to a lot of mothers who cannot breastfeed and have babies who wake up every hour and are much older than Loren. Of course they ask what I do and they think. i am crazy, when I say what I do and in what. i believe ;-) Light is too ordinary to be considered the best medicine.

### rlee314 — 2015-08-30T08:30:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-176054>

thanks all!!

so...my breastfeeding struggles turned into abscesses that needed surgery!! ugh! sigh. so....it's been many years since i've taken antibiotics...i was on IV abx for 5 days and then oral abx for 10 days. how do i repair the damage to my gut?
i'm currently breastfeeding my sweet girl on one breast and having to give her donor milk---so grateful for the mommas who have shared their milk so that i can keep my girl off formula! i do hope and plan to nurse on the other side once completely healed.
i'm loading up-even more so-on dha, sunlight and drinking tons of turmeric tea and water.

anyhow, aside from that....my little girl is amazing! very alert and SO CALM and CONTENT. <---this makes her very predictable. her fussing has a reason. no guessing. and fussing is rare and short. fussing is...i am hungry, i need to burp, i am tired. she sleeps a lot. i forgot how much newborns sleep. her circadian rhythm continues to be pretty great--and she is night nursing more which i am pretty happy about actually. co-sleeping makes 'breast sleeping/night nursing' easy. and my circadian rhythm doesn't get messed up. she wakes with the roosters-just like my little boy.

so, again friends, id love advice/suggestions on how to repair my gut and body after the onslaught of antibiotics--of which i am grateful for--the abscesses in my breast were cultured as strep.

### rlee314 — 2017-07-18T03:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-beginnings-everyday-blessings.12477/post-219225>

spent the weekend celebrating my 2nd baby's 2nd birthday. my epi paleo baby. these 2 LOVE the water/ocean. it's our happy place. i'm forevre grateful that i listened to my intuition during pregnancy w/my son and moved from portland oregon-very little LIGHT-to SUNNY hawai'i.

feeling blessed and grateful to be part of dr. kruse's starfish community.
