### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T19:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-151842>

I posted this this morning in my [only thread here](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/) that isn't my journal -

"
ASI is adrenal stress index? They don't call it that there but pretty sure thats' what it is.

I'm having real trouble figuring this out because I am going to see my GP on Thursday (day after tomorrow) to see if he can order any tests for me and get my insurance to cover them. I don't know which ones he will order and which ones he won't. So I don't know what to order myself!

I find it likely that he can and will order all the thyroid stuff for me because he has before. I can't imagine him saying no to vitamin d testing either. But I don't know what adrenal testing consists of. Can he just send me for fasting bloodwork the next morning and have them do that or is it different? All my doctors tests will be bloodwork.

I don't have any cash on hand so anything I order will have to go on credit and I really hate to do that. I feel like I Should forgo testing and just trust the pharmacist to throw stuff at me based on a telephone interview of my symptoms, because I can maybe afford that. But maybe not.. I guess whatever they prescribe I will have to pay cash for...? What does BHRT cost anyway?

so many questions and nobody to ask... no sisters, mom passed away ten years ago, no friends to speak of... \*sigh\*

I know I want my hormones tested and DHEA and DHA. And cortisol maybe? But I don't understand cortisol... everyone says it's important to have enough in the morning but then say it's bad because it's related to stress. So am I not supposed to stress in the morning? Because i thought you WANTED cortisol in the morning so I do my bills and stuff at that point and spend all morning in a crappy stressed out mood.. but see, you all say you want cortisol in the morning right?

Likewise I read articles about people who are like me, over 40 and with glaucoma, and it says they have BAD eyes because they DON"T let in blue light. So.. I thought I was supposed to never have blue light, ever. No lightbulbs, use blueblockers, etc. But now I read that my eyes a re bad because they DON"T LET blue light in!

I am so confused. I only ever found this website because I googled BHRT and landed in a monster thread all about it, so I signed up. Now I find that Jack has a whole thing about eating special, special EMF limits, all kinds of things I never head of before and never had a concern about.

Whic his GREAT and I'm incorporating much of it into my own life because it reads sensibly and seems legit... but it has led to overwhelming information overload and has made it so that now I don't know where to start. It had seemed so simple at first.. I'm constantly a bitch, so I need more hormones. Well okay that's counter intuitive, I've always lamented that I need LESS hormones because I'm so impossible. But now I think I need more because... no sex drive.

Oh I am just so confused and sad this morning at the fact that when the pharmacist calls all I can really tell him is please don't order any test for me because I don't know what I need yet. Some days I feel like I'm making progress and other days it hits me that I have changed nothing ,except I now spend MORE time on the couch on the computer, because I'm reading here and trying to learn. But I haven't. I still don't know what to do about BHRT or testing. On one hand my days have been less boring and more fulfilling because I found some things that sound interesting to try, which should keep me busy buut on the other hand they're more depressing and frustrating because I'm not getting anywhere, and now I'm more confused than ever. I thought I'd just ask to be put on the pill, because that should be completely balanced hormones right?

But now I don't know anymore after reading here.

Most people sign up here, I bet, because they wanted to lose weight and had tried everything and then decided to try Jack's book/diet/Rxes. So I can understand why everyone is so quick to advise me to do all the stuff they do. But my goals aren't the same.... so I feel like I don't know how to pick and choose what to do now. My n=1 is different, which I initially thought would be a great fit here but now I just.. well.. I'm used to being the smart one. Not so much here. I don't like that feeling.

hopefully the day will improve from here. This isn't like me. bleh."

....

"
Oh boy. I need to apologize... I'm so sorry for being in such a mood this morning. I surely shouldn't have had such an outburst. I'm sorry.

I have since turned off the laptop (wifi connection + blue light from wake up to bed time has to be the worst thing for me yet I just seem to have completely not even thought about that until now.. I have many ethernet cables and will dig one out once I'm done posting this).. Yesterday morning I had steak for breakfast with raw greens and cucumber.

This morning I threw two slices of cinnamon raisin bread from the (discount) grocery store into the toaster and then used "light margarine spread" on them, then added on a banana.

...

Ya think this is it?

(could also be that my almost-blind, diabetic, 13 year old rat terrier, Daisy, had a warm and fragrant "accident" on the floor right in front of me this morning quite deliberately because I told her "not right now" when she wanted to go out. Touche!)"

Thoughts? Am I on the right track?

### Penny — 2015-01-06T20:04:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-151857>

So, about the blue light thing - just go outside in the morning for like a minute and let the blue light from the sun hit your eyes - this will kill melatonin and raise cortisol and wake you up - basically - but if you don't let in blue light - like a lot of older people with cataracts, they have found it affects their moods - in fact, once they get their cataract surgery, they are happier and more motivated...

Delete all wheat. Period. No more toast. Wheat has glutamates - glutamates - glutamates are bad for the eyes - not to mention how the gliadin in wheat and casein in milk are indigestible by a human...

I would just assume you are inflamed and have a screwed up 03/06 ratio and start a healthier diet with a lot of zinc, iodine and DHA - canned sardines/herring are cheap - look up the Leaky gut RX - I would be doing the leptin reset, going out into nature and getting cold -
I would be doing a lot of fermented food - according to Tennant's theory, glaucoma is a blocked lymphatic system which is there because there is so much cellular debris coming from all the toxicity in our bodies... zinc, DHA, blood pressure and the lymphatic system all affect the eyes...

Another thing I would try - and you really should check with an eye doctor to make sure you don't dislodge something - is jumping on a trampoline - it helps move the lymph through gravity - you can also look up dry skin brushing which also moves the lymph -

As far as blood testing goes, the first thing \*you\* should do IMHO is get a food allergy test to see if there is any seafood you can eat - in the meantime, take krill oil pills to try to raise your DHA - and take some zinc pills/lozengers and kelp -
I would also ask for a basic CBC - you can check your BUN/creatinine ratio to see how dehydrated you are - which also affects the eyes BTW...

For hormones, I would just get a vitamin D and DHEA a
For hormones, just check DHEA and vitamin D - this will give you an idea of what hormones you are making -the last thing to get is an HSCRP - this will tell you how inflamed you are...
[http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003356.htm](http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003356.htm)

As far as cortisol goes, if you are stressed, you make cortisol instead of all the other hormones you should be making - too much blue light from a screen can do that alone - not eating enough cholesterol will also do it -
[http://metabolichealing.com/the-endocrine-journey-steroidal-hormones-their-pathways/](http://metabolichealing.com/the-endocrine-journey-steroidal-hormones-their-pathways/)

So, food allergy test, CBC, vit D, DHEA, HSCRP -

DHA in a pill and zinc - oysters have lots of zinc, but that's a fail for you -

Here is a nice summary of Dr. Tennant's ideas - what I'm trying to say here is to go after the low hanging fruit first...
[http://www.senergy.us/Assets/PDFs/Dr. Tennant Articles/Voltage, The Key to Rebuilding You Life.pdf](http://www.senergy.us/Assets/PDFs/Dr.%20Tennant%20Articles/Voltage,%20The%20Key%20to%20Rebuilding%20You%20Life.pdf)

What is your blood pressure BTW? Not enough is bad for the eyes and so is too much - plus, he talks about whether you have glaucoma with low pressure - if you have that, he calls it "optic nutritional deficiency syndrome"...

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-07T14:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-151952>

well... as I read, I begin to realize things.... like the fact that I am stuck in my paleocortex.

Sad all the way around. Not surprising though - I've always said I'm a "Deep Feeler". But come on.. knowing this should've put me on the path to bypassing it but instead I just clung to it as an excuse for my paleocortex always being in control and my actions/ RE-actions always being emotionally based.

No more.

I need to keep in mind that I am a spirit given a vehicle for my time on earth (my physical body) but I am not that body, and further I must not RESENT that body! I must treat it properly or else my spirit will have to utilize some other vehicle which necessarily will result in my physical body's death. I fail to see how I could be so negligent up to this point. If I didn't put oil in my car, didn't replace the brake pads, didn't keep water and radiator fluid in it, it would ran crappier and ccrappier until it became useless. No question about it. Why would my body be any different? Easy to say "well no kidding, obviously" but then one must ask oneself "if it is so obvious, what keeps us from behaving differently?".... Convenience (laziness) mostly.

Well.. that doesn't sit well with me. The problem with shining a light into a corner is that you suddenly see all the filth that's been hiding there. And then you have no choice but to clean it up or realize your responsibility in the matter. And if you are a parent, particularly a mother, actions are teachings. Thankfully one of the best lessons is that when we know better, we must do better. My daughter wanted to try out my blue blockers today. She wanted to know what to do about EMF, how her Kindle is affecting her, why the USA allows this to happen... I did my best to answer her questions but in the end sometimes all you can say is "I don't know why it is how it is, I only know that it IS how it is... so we have to do for ourselves."

\*sigh\*... rambleramble back to reading.

ok Hope - here is my best guess for what is going on with you... your teeth are infecting you, cruising into your eyes via an acupuncture medidian, causing your lymphocytes to attack, creating cellular debris in your lymph nodes and you're either not clearing the lymph because your cranial sacrum pump isn't working well so it's not pumping the lymph out and/or you have so many toxins coming from your mouth that it is overwhelming the lymph's ability to clear itself out...

How to solve this? Clean your teeth - a lot - I would recommend drowning them in hydrogen peroxide and baking soda - frequently - in addition to that, you most likely are inflammed in general due to your sugar and SAD diet in general - which creates more crap in your lymph nodes - the dehydration from the EMF probably doesn't help the lymph flow either - you might be harboring some microbes under your fillings... they live under biofilms...

Using this chart, here are the tooth numbers with merideans leading to the eyes:

[https://www.google.com/search?q=numbered dental chart teeth&rlz=1C1MSIM\_enUS620US620&espv=2&biw=1920&bih=955&tbm=isch&imgil=BKgZ\_Qum1q3BXM%3A%3BDy7kMHnTy8pYBM%3Bhttp%253A%252F%252Fwww.mexicandentalclinic.com%252FTijuana-Dental-Chart.html&source=iu&pf=m&fir=BKgZ\_Qum1q3BXM%3A%2CDy7kMHnTy8pYBM%2C\_&usg=\_\_nas5fW4\_wzczJydA8cgwwV0tNdw=&ved=0CCkQyjc&ei=R16tVLaDI5TkoASb-oL4AQ#imgdii=\_&imgrc=BKgZ\_Qum1q3BXM%3A;l44eqn3Qpe1QGM;http%3A%2F%2Fwww.mexicandentalclinic.com%2FTooth\_chart\_2.jpg;http%3A%2F%2Fbesttoddlertoys.eu%2Ftag%2Fdental-chart;1242;749](https://www.google.com/search?q=numbered+dental+chart+teeth&rlz=1C1MSIM_enUS620US620&espv=2&biw=1920&bih=955&tbm=isch&imgil=BKgZ_Qum1q3BXM%253A%253BDy7kMHnTy8pYBM%253Bhttp%25253A%25252F%25252Fwww.mexicandentalclinic.com%25252FTijuana-Dental-Chart.html&source=iu&pf=m&fir=BKgZ_Qum1q3BXM%253A%252CDy7kMHnTy8pYBM%252C_&usg=__nas5fW4_wzczJydA8cgwwV0tNdw%3D&ved=0CCkQyjc&ei=R16tVLaDI5TkoASb-oL4AQ#imgdii=_&imgrc=BKgZ_Qum1q3BXM%253A%3Bl44eqn3Qpe1QGM%3Bhttp%253A%252F%252Fwww.mexicandentalclinic.com%252FTooth_chart_2.jpg%3Bhttp%253A%252F%252Fbesttoddlertoys.eu%252Ftag%252Fdental-chart%3B1242%3B749)

#1,2,3,6,7,8,9,10,11,14,15,16,17,20,21,28,29,32

So, as I was learning about the lymphatic system this morning - gee that was eye opening - I read that someone with lymphoma would have lymph that was cancerous - wondering if there was a relationship between glaucoma and lymphoma, I found this:
[http://journals.lww.com/glaucomajou...ion\_of\_Lymphoma\_associated\_Open\_angle.13.aspx](http://journals.lww.com/glaucomajournal/Abstract/2011/08000/Resolution_of_Lymphoma_associated_Open_angle.13.aspx)

Apparently, this person had lesions in her lymph, and this caused glaucoma - which resolved after removal of the tumors in the lymph... I don't know what other correlation one would need at this point...

So, here is a video on how to massage your lymph glands to clear them - be maximally hydrated of course for this and don't press too hard or they can rupture - this was also mentioned in the Tennant book BTW... Tennant uses electricity to do the massage he says: "By gently following the normal flow of lymphatic sewage with the electrode, the tubes begin to move the sewage along. It is important to be sure the patient is hydrated. One must start where the system flows into the heart just behind the clavicles. Next open the neck channels. Now start down the body. As you are opening the channels, it is important to be sure the lymph nodes are able to pass the fluid through them. This can be accomplished by pressing on them like you are pressing the fluid out of a sponge. The hardest one is the thoracic duct that takes the sewage from the inguinal area to the heart. It runs deep in the body next to the aorta. It requires some training to do this successfully."

"Remember, the channels above the naval run to the nodes in the axilla (armpits) whereas the channels below the naval run to the inguinal region and then deep into the thoracic duct. When the thoracic duct is obstructed, people get fluid retention below the belly button. They think it is strange that they are getting fat just below the belly button and not above it. It's not fat; it's sewage."

"The essential oil Bay Laurel is also helpful in getting the lymphatics to drain. So is a mini-trampoline."

"Remember the reason we have a lymphatic system is to get rid of waste proteins because they are too large to fit into the various capillaries. The waste proteins are from worn out proteins in the cells."

"We replace the rods and cones in our retina every 48 hours, so we create a lot of "sewage" in our eyes. Therefore we need a special system capable of handling all this sewage. The inside of the eye is hollow so it has the room for all this sewage. To keep it flowing, some fluid is added by the lining of the focusing muscle (ciliary body) secreting what is known as "aqueous humor". Thus aqueous humor is like extracellular fluid in the rest of the body."

He also says that massage therapists can do it too hard and cause a rupture...
[http://massage-tutorials.wonderhowto.com/how-to/massage-clear-your-lymph-nodes-143153/](http://massage-tutorials.wonderhowto.com/how-to/massage-clear-your-lymph-nodes-143153/)

So there you go - if I think of something else, I will certainly let you know

Or just do a lot of jumping...

Hope... dear... if it makes you feel any better... i am in the same boat as you..... it hurts to see the filth in the corners  in my corners are a lot, unfortunately. I think I lost my boyfriend.... in the beginning he really wanted me to come live by his place... but lately he thinks i need to stay here, find a real work and get my head together.... which he sure is right about. I understand he does not like too much the ex husband helps me with money so i just have to work a little bit.... and i have lots of things that are very weird to him that i have not hidden either... blueblockers.... early bed times... MHS... well i think those are good things but I sure understand he is shocked... then there are bad things too... like how hard time i have to trust. Really broken in that way after my marriage. This i guess is what have done the damage the most. I really love him... i do... and it breaks my heart it went like this but i just could no better at the time. I was at his place Christmas and i did no good. I just was so exhausted from too little sleep and stuff i just did not behave appropriately i guess. I am so ashamed but i can not go back. I can apologize but it may be too late. So i just try to think, there is lots of stuff for me to learn from this... so i go along and execute the stuff i learned from the boyfriend, i am searching for a real job now to not be dependent on my ex husbands money (but he loves to help, that is...) - and it actually feels very good, i am so excited.    Had my first interview today... went great! I am so waiting to stand all on my own feet money wise... it is going to be great! The boyfriend is very wealthy... i guess those guys easily think women are only after their money too? So if i have none and he has a lot it is kind of a mismatch? And i will have to heal and learn to trust... the worst thing is i am so open.. and tell about my weird thoughts and fears and what i have experienced.. and for another person it can be very upsetting i guess... i need to learn to keep stuff for myself too. So yeah... i might have lost it... but i refuse to let it drag me down... yeah, i can cry, cause it hurts too, but then i shake off the tears and i just stand up and go on..... change things that i did wrong in the past. Every experience is good for something, even if it was hurtful. And the boyfriend have given me so many beautiful moment for which i am so thankful... who knows.. maybe it is not too late but it seems like it is. Anyhow i am going to go forward and take good care of me and implement all i have learned and learn from where i failed. I am feeling so positive about life and i, strangely, feel so happy even if it is not easy! That is always a blessing when we can learn something new, and change for the better................

i think we are all here on earth to learn our lessons... the sooner the better, so come on challenges! Uh!
(the most painful thing i guess is to see ones owns failures..... But the pain can be changed into joy .. when we just do the changes!)

sorry for me rambling in your journal now....

Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels **I ASSUME I MUST PAY FOR THESE OUTRIGHT..NOT SURE A GP CAN EVER GET INSURANCE TO COVER THIS STUFF..?**

12.  Complete thyroid panel with antibody screening (TPO etc) **WILL REQUEST - HISTORY OF FATIGUE..?**

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T13:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152073>

Good morning! 6:09am here and my daughter's school has a two hour delay due to windchill temperatures. This has only happened here once before that I recall in the past 30 years or so. Right now it is -15F / -26C but the amazing thing is that up in the Adirondacks where we go for vacation each summer it is currently SO cold that F and C are the same .... -38. They say exposed skin for five minutes gets frostbite in this weather. Crossingguards are wearing facemasks. Eyelashes are frozen like Inuits in Alaska. Crazy cold day. Of course it's still pitch black out but where I live it's dark until 730 and dark again at 430... so much of my waking hours are in darkness with artificial light.

Which brings me to the question, what exactly is the deal with blue blockers - do we put them on when it gets dark out so that our eyes don't think it is still daylight and so our melatonin production ramps up? If so, I should not put them on in the morning because despite the sun still being down, I must wake up. Right?

Or option two would be, put them on at 430pm when the sun goes down and put them back on upon waking and leave them there until 730am when the sun comes up.

Or the third option would be, if artificial light is damaging to your eyes, that ANY time I am exposed to ANY blue light/artificial light I put them on to protect my eyes due to my glaucoma.

I'm not really sure which it is, because I don't have the education about blue light yet. So I can't actually figure out what to do based on people saying "buy these blue blockers for blue light and especially wear them in the evening" without context.. because that could mean it's only important when you're trying to ramp up melatonin for bedtime, or it could mean that some people are lucky enough to live where they only put on artificial lighting in the evening, so they only think to advise others to wear the glasses in the evening because they presume there will not BE any artificial light the rest of the time.

In which case, that advice doesn't work for me because I am in artificial light 24/7 except for sleeping hours, because it's dark here year round due to clouds.

But if I wear them ALL THE TIME because of artificial lighting ALL THE TIME, then I am pretty sure that means my body will always only make melatonin and never wake up via cortisol spiking... so in my case I should NOT wear blue blockers EVER because to block out more sun would be even more confusing to my eyes, right? Maybe that is why I sleep way worse the past two nights after wearing them much of the day and night.

ACtually if it has to do with cortisol/melatonin AT ALL, then I would think putting on blue blockers in times of stress would be a good plan to limit cortisol dumping.

But that doesn't seem like sound science so I really need to know why I was told to wear these things so that I know WHEN to use them. Someone told me how to fish but didn't teach me and now I'm hungry all over again!

[http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-11-is-technology-your-achilles-heel/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-11-is-technology-your-achilles-heel/) - yes I'm reading this. I haven't found anything in the book yet about it so I'll see if this explains what I need to know. I'm a "but WHY??" kinda girl.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T13:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152075>

"**KEY POINT:** This is also why peri and menopausal women’s FBG go up once their ovaries have failed. Ladies pay attention to it. Very few clinicians seem to know this, so they do not pay attention to it. Look at your own glucose levels on your labs. **If they are over 88** you likely have this issue as well. I pay attention to it. When we have have high normal blood glucose it is a sign of brain damage. [High normal blood glucose causes the brain to shrink](http://health.usnews.com/health-news/news/articles/2012/09/04/even-high-normal-blood-sugar-tied-to-brain-shrinkage)! I have been on the artificial light rampage for 10 years now because I found I was very sensitive to it myself before i fixed the problem."

**Going backwards from most recent:**

2014 labs: On November 26 my fasting blood glucose was **76**. On October 9 it was **86.** On August 15 it was **103**.

previous labs: In October of 2007 it was **92**, and in August of 2007 it was **86**.

My mother and oldest brother were/are type 2 diabetics. My favorite food group is carbohydrates. I will have to keep a strict eye on this.

balance but yes they are key players.

n diet is way out of whack.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T14:06:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152077>

"**Blue light after sunset** reduces the prolactin surge we normally see in humans. When we see chronic lowered prolactin surges we also see lower growth hormone secretion during the anabolic phases of sleep. Lowered chronic GH secretion directly affects cardiac and skeletal muscle function because the process of autophagy is made less efficient as our life continues. Lowered GH and the sex steroid hormones at sleep lead to loss of cardiac function. This is why heart failure is strongly associated with low IGF-1 and low sex steroid hormone levels. When growth hormone is not released in normal amounts, it also decreases our lean muscle mass, and increases our body fat percentage in all our organs and in our body. This leads to slowly declining organ dysfunction and poor body composition."

Okay. Put 'em on when it gets dark out, then. Gotcha. Will still read the rest of this though of course.

Oh I am so NOT a bandwagon girl. I'm a hardcore negative skeptic who tends to pessimistically ignore every bandwagon that goes by, often laughing and pointing at the poor saps who are so naively on it.

In this case, however, we're talking about educating ourselves about scientific facts, which is entirely different. I consider this a free university level course on science/biology/nutrition/chemistry. Cults are bandwagons. Until Jack starts selling tiny homes in a city he owns and builds himself as an EMF-Free zone complete with neighborhood / community CT stations / rainwater catchment bins, community free organic gardens, and a commune-style free-range organic farm setup, this is no cult.

So I'm still confused. First you mention putting them on at evening but hen you said anytime you have artificial light. I wasn't joking when I said that we can't live here during the day without artificial light. It is on literally any time i am awake or else it's too dark to even read most of the time.

So which is it? Put them on from wake to bed because that's when I'm exposed to blue/artificial light? Or only put them on once the sun goes down?

Still reading to try to figure this out.

PS - I need that warm hug! Right back at you! \*hugs\*

### Lahelada — 2015-01-08T14:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152082>

Hope you are defining day by function. You use blue light to replace the sun in your winter months. The blu blockers are there to give a signal to yourb body that it is now night deal with it,body. So if you have the ight onat 1 pm because a snow storm is passing is immaterial. It is daytime for your body, the blue blockers say ok you had your fun in the sun/under the kitchen lamp now do all the night stuff.

Re hard science questions/matters : yes but the basics are light,water and magnetism . The lowest hanging fruit (easiest)in the season you are in is water as all it takes is a trip to the supermarket to buy RO water,next is magnetism. This is the season when you are meant to be cold...Now of course that does not mean go out in a bikini but how high is your thermostat set? Is it summer inside and winter outside? (also a circadian mismatch)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T14:39:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152086>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=152084)
>
> Replace your lighting with amber bulbs?

Sounds possible for a split second but realistically then I would walk around nauseous all the time. Honestly, I fail to see how that is better than what I started with.

I came here able to sleep at night. Since adopting the blue blockers that is no longer the case, AND I have issues with nauseousness from wearing them.

I woudl say that's zero steps forward and two steps back.

This is where N=1 comes in. My issues and solutions will not be the same as everyone else's. I need to remember this and NOT jump on the blue-blocker bandwagon, or any other, just because enthusiastic and charismatic people say to.

Thanks for reminding me of that.

Now that I own them, I can experiment with them. But from now on they will be an add-on tweak, not a routine. First I will get baseline tests done, then begin with supplements for nutrition as well as hormones, then will re-test and tweak with some additional things like blue-blockers and a grounding mat and whatnot if/when it becomes necessary.

You have to let your kid fall and hurt himself sometimes before he understands why you're warning him not to engage in a certain risky act.

That's me. I'm that kid. I trust you'll all be here when I fall, and that's a great thing.

### Lahelada — 2015-01-08T14:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152088>

Blue light is good,blue light is bad,both statements apply. The problem js with the amount in our modern lives. Wear the blue blockers not for the purpose of eliminating blue light but excess blue light. Lets give you some hard examples.
I am sitting here typing on a smartphone at 9.40 in the morning getting more light in my eyes than I would get naturally from sunlight. I should wear blue blockers. bad,bad,bad
If I was sitting here under 700 watts of fluorescent light and it is under my control not to do so then no blue blockers. Switch it off or change to a source of amber light.
Put the blue blockers on at night to rduce blue light level to prepare you for sleep.

Wearing your blue blockers all day is probably affecting your sleep as it disrupts your circadian rythm .you are meant to see some blue light to mark the passing / winding down of the day.

Water: RO water is best. Next is bottled spring water. Don't lose sight of the ball. You are trying to get better,the university degree you. Earn with theory and practicals as you go along.
66f? Kudos ! I thought you were a 72 f woman

Gotta go . Blu light overdose.

### Penny — 2015-01-08T17:50:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152119>

You know Hope it is interesting that the blue blockers made you more nauseous - there are some kids who regularly come to my house who are so blue light toxic it is ridiculous - always on a cell phone or playing video games - sleep is irregular or nonexistent - one gets migraines - the other had to be home schooled because of frequent headaches - \*both\* of them reacted to the blue blockers with headaches as soon as they tried them...

I would love to know why that happened?

I thought a low body temp means your thyroid is screwed up per Broda Barnes?

### shah78 — 2015-01-08T18:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152122>

Maybe (probably) the blue light is like methamphetamine, cocaine, et al. They're so addictied to it, that any "relief/down time away from it" is treated like withdrawal. Nausea= withdraw.

### Penny — 2015-01-08T19:20:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152131>

A decent site on amalgam fillings and EBV - it sounds paranoid in the beginning - but they do have sources that they cite...
[http://www.bettyelders.com/EBV.htm](http://www.bettyelders.com/EBV.htm)

BTW, I think my friend with the 3 autoimmune diseases is going to be pulling the rest of her teeth... so many of them are already gone - she had previously discussed it with her dentist but it occurred to her that all these autoimmune diseases might disappear if the source of the toxins was eliminated... will let you know how it works out for her... in the interview above, Westin Price took an infected tooth out of someone with rheumatoid arthritis and she was cured... he then stuck the tooth into a rabbit and the rabbit developed RA and died 10 days later -

They talk about how just removing the tooth isn't enough when removal proves necessary. He found bacteria in the tissues and bone just adjacent to the tooth's root - so now they recommend slow-speed drilling with a burr, to remove one millimeter of the entire bony socket. The purpose is to remove the periodontal ligament (which is always infected with toxins produced by streptococcus bacteria living in the dentin tubules) and the first millimeter of bone that lines the socket (which is usually infected.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T21:59:31+0200

>
> I would love to know why that happened?
>
> I thought a low body temp means your thyroid is screwed up per Broda Barnes?

It's probably not a good thing medically, I was just playing around because of what Dr. K. says about CT and each degree that your core body temperature lowers.

For me the nausea only sets in when walking with them on. I'm fine wearing them sitting down. The same thing happens if I try to walk with my reading glasses on.. because my depth perception gets screwed up, at a minimum (with my reading glasses at least). Not sure exactly what makes my brain react as though it's not quite on level ground when I'm wearing them but it sort of feels like the dizziness after getting off an amusement park right when you're not quite steady on your feet. And even that wasn't right away, only after wearing them for hours. So I don't know.. interesting about the kids with the headaches though! I will have to let my daughter try these for longer than just a few minutes.. she's a regular "tech kid" (almost 12 years old now) but when she wore them for about 3-4 minutes the other day sitting still in the car she didn't complain. I'll have her try them walking, too. Should be interesting!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T22:30:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152149>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=152123)
>
> DHEA raises more testosterone than estrogen so taking too much can make one psycho... speaking from experience - sort of like when I eat too many gluatamates... switch to 7 Keto DHA - I think that's better for women or use pregnenalone -
> [http://www.raysahelian.com/dhea.html](http://www.raysahelian.com/dhea.html)
> [http://www.life-enhancement.com/mag...h-ray-sahelian-md-the-promise-of-pregnenolone](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/95-exclusive-interview-with-ray-sahelian-md-the-promise-of-pregnenolone)

Yeah I took like 25 mg one day instead of 12.5 like I had been for a few days prior and I was just way more impossible than normal.

Right now I'm only taking a woman's daily multivitamin and two of those fish oil pills.

Also, I went to my GP today with a list of tests I'd ideally want to have done if possible (so it was pretty long) and explained I'm turning 42 in a couple of weeks, I have an appt with KJ on the 16th already and one with the pharmacy on the 21st, but I wanted to just run some stuff by him that I was hoping to do. I told him that I wasn't there with a list to say, like, "I want these done! hurr!!" or anything, I just had discussed some thoughts with KJ, the pharmacy lady, and some other people who I know (that would be you all. ) and that with all the input I had plus some reading and research over the past several weeks, I thought these might be good. But I said that I am not a doctor and do not know and was hoping he could help me decide if they were reasonable choices. I hurried to say that I realized I might have to order some myself online, but...

at that point he interrupted me to say "but then you would have to pay for them yourself! let me look at them" and he asked for my written list (this pleased me quite a bit) and then he asked me first if I needed a DEXA scan, to which I replied "yes please!" (I love this guy - we've been together for almost 20 years and it's so nice that he knows my history/family that well)

Then we discussed some of the tests.. he got caught up in telling me I just had a lot of bloodwork done and I reassured him that I hadn't written down any repeats. I explained that I get no real DHA due to not knowing which seafoods I'm allergic to and asked for a blood test for that. He wanted to refer me to an allergist and I said whatever he thinks is best, but I had seen some results online thata looked like they were from a blood test, and it listed a lot of foods with low, medium, and high sensitivity markers. He fiddled on his computer for a few minutes, maybe looking that test up, I'm not sure. Then he wrote a script for an allergy test and handed it to me (yay!).

Next he pulled up my extended/comprehensive/whatever (outside just his office) health record on his computer and went over all of my most recent bloodwork from the oncologist (lymph node issue) and then finally got out his Rx pad and copied what I'm pretty sure is my whole request list.

He wrote..
allergy RAST panel
lipid panel
vit d3 level
hs-crp
thyroid antibodies, t3, ft4, tsh
urinalysis (he didn't write "with beta hcg". uh oh..?)
serum cortisol
salivary cortisol
fsh, lh, estradiol, dhea, 170 H progesterone (I think that's what that says.. ??)

Actually that's all he wrote. (Aside from the dx codes that I'm curious enough to look up in a little while here) So, whatever else I asked for that he didn't put in, or didn't put in exactly, will have to wait and/or be redone at my own expense or as a recheck later, if he agrees.

Overall it was a good appointment!

17-OH Progesterone - [http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/6-17hydroxy/tab/test/](http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/6-17hydroxy/tab/test/)

I think he read my "adrenal stress index' and wrote this in response.. ? Then again I did write "progesterone" on my list... I have no idea.

edit: nevermind, yes I was right.

[http://www.diagnostechs.com/Pages/AdrenalStressIndex.aspx](http://www.diagnostechs.com/Pages/AdrenalStressIndex.aspx)

"The Adrenal Stress Index (ASI) panel was introduced in 1989 to evaluate stress, a leading cause of morbidity and mortality. Additional tests have been added to evaluate glycemic control using multiple salivary insulin measurements, and to evaluate adrenal capacity to produce cortisol using **17-Hydroxyprogesterone**."

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T18:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152227>

Just got back from getting my labs done. I had to do a 12-hour-fasting lipid panel of course.. my doc didn't write for VAP or NMR though.

Similarly they took a urine sample but my doc only wrote "urinalysis'.. I told the lady who drew my blood that it should be for the purpose of testing my beta hcg and she said well that isn't on here but if my doc calls the lab and tells them to add it on they can still do it with this sample. I will try to see if he will do that for me.

My DEXA scan will be later this month.

They took two vials for my allergy RAST test, just in case one wasn't enough.

Then seven more vials for the hormone and thyroid tests and whatever else I listed above in the other post about my labs.

Then she gave me a single swab in a tube and said it's for the salivary cortisol test. She said take it home and her advice is wait until tomorrow and do it in the morning, even though no actual instructions to that end were given by my doctor or on the labratory instruction sheet. I thought that was sort of unfortunate that it's not documented how to do the test other than to rinse your mouth with water, spit it out (do not swallow!!) and then put the swab under your tongue for at least 60 seconds.

I told her that I had expected f our swabs and she double checked and said nope he didn't order that one. NO big deal, I said, I'll just have it redone in a couple of months along with all the re0check testing I plan to have done. Hopefully that's fine, otherwise I"ll order it online from ZRT and do it myself. It's just a HUGE hassle being in NY is all. I don't have a PA post office box to have them mail ot to and to send it back from, so I'll have to find out exactly how strict they want to be.

But I love this lab... I drove out of state to go to this particular hospital this morning even knowinig it would be a 2 hour round trip by the time all was said and done... know why?

Because they put my results online by automated process the second their lab guy finishes going over them. I get my results before my doctor does by nearly a week because he gets them snail-mailed to him.

I freakin love that place.

So then after all that fasting and whatnot I finally was able to take my vitamins, and added vitamin d3 5000 IU daily to my regimen. I asked the lady if they had vitamin K2 and she said "K \*TWO\*? not TWO, no" so I didn't buy any. I was proud of myself for that. baby steps!

What about grassfed beef bones, can you get them?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T19:06:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152230>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=152228)

> What about grassfed beef bones, can you get them?

They sell grassfed beef at a local grocery store (sort of local.. I have to drive about 30 minutes to get there on the highway, but I live so far out in the middle of trees and nature that I have to do that to get anywhere anyway)... but it looks pre-packaged. I will ask them if their butcher can either save me, or get me, joint-cut grass-fed beef bones though, with some meat still on them if possible.

I am going to make broth today. I think that compared to my regular diet it can't possibly be WORSE. Normally I'd stop at a fast food place and get a greasy hamburger and fries sometime this afternoon, my fast food habit has been once daily for a very long time due to convenience. Almost anything I can do differently in that regard has to be an improvement I think.

I had a big allergy test done today. I will find out exactly what seafood I can eat, and then I will really get started on all this.

I realize now that it's a blessing to live where I do. I often complain we don't get cell service, there's nothing to do, I'm surrounded by nothing but pine trees and cornfields and cows. I complain about the winter cold.... well.... bottom line is that I think I have it all wrong. These are all GOOD things. We also live on a little semi-private pond/lake and we have a peddle-boat that I never use... I think I could magnify how much sun I get despite our terribly short days if I start going out on the boat and sunning on the water once spring comes... thoughts on that, anyone?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T19:18:49+0200

> Replace your lighting with amber bulbs?

How did I miss this post before? Maybe I didn't miss it but I meant to reply to it..

We actually have an amber bulb outside the front door, they sell them here as "bug lights" to help keep the bugs from swarming around the lights in the summertime. Not sure how well it works but this time of year, in the winnter here, I bet I could find some on clearance somewhere. I might have to do that! Thank you for the suggestion!

(Problem is, they're "energy saver" bulbs usually... those always give off high EMF as it turns out. The one lamp we have a regular old fashioned bulb in doesn't even make the meter register anything... the other 35 lightbulbs in the house approximately are all energy savers and give off EMF. It's supposed to save on your power bill but I haven't seen any change whatsoever since putting them in.. except now we have EMF coming at us from places we didn't use to. So I will keep an eye out for regular old fashioned amber bulbs.  )

If only I had known then.... but the good news is that my daughter will always take this knowledge with her from here on out.. this is all interesting her to no end.. I even foudn a piace of paper that she wrote a list of diet and exercise information onto.. I had told her that I was changing how i eat and cook for thehm all but didn't explain in what way, so her list contains traditional "diet planning" stuff on it.

I look forward to blowing her mind when she finds out that we're not going to just eat nothing but salads and fresh fruit with lean chicken and egg whites. heh.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T19:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152235>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=152229)
>
> So cool with your labs  Your doctor/his place rock

Yeah.  I finally got lucky. So pleased about that. I've been with this doctor since my boys were 3 and 1, and they're 21 and 19 now (my daughter is 11, almost 12).

The background story of this lymph node that is swollen still, which I had to see the cancer doctor about, illustrates how lucky I am righ tnow. The first people that I got referred to were an absolute nightmare. I'm lucky my neck didn't get slit from my ear to my throat on the right side and stitched back up again back in October. I had already done my pre-surgery intake and testing and everything when I got my last minute reprieve. My gut said it was all wrong so I stopped the whole process in it's tracks and said nevermind, I'm startinig from scratch. Got a new onoclogist and now am very happy. But the details of how bad the first people were would make you squirm.

Two days ago two more lymph nodes popped up on the other side. My GP told me yesterday he wants me to go back to oncology but I'm not going to right now. I will wait at least a week to see if they go back down first because there's a little acne sore nearby.. hopefully they're just doing their job. Otherwise this blog is about to take an entirely different tone, and I already have a glaucoma-lymphoma blog elsewhere, that I had hoped not to need...

\*fingers crossed\* and time to make the broth. Didn't get it started yesterday because I was going to be out and couldn't watch it properly. I prefer to do stock/broth on the stove as compared to a slow cooker... far more vortexing takes place that way.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T19:42:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152237>

Oh wow, just called the doc and asked if they can call the lab and add on the beta-HCG thing to the urinalysis. The nurse answered (we're about the same age, have had similar issues with our kids who are similar ages, etc - tiny clinic with only one doctor in a tiny town means we get friendly with the staff which is nice)... I told her that the urinalysis didn't have "beta hcg" on it and asked if she thought they could maybe call the lab and add it since it's one of the hormone-related things I wanted to have a look at, and she said sure, no problem, I'll give them a call for you.

Love this place. Seriously.

I will never say a bad thing about Robert Packer Hospital / the Guthrie clinic in Sayre, Pennsylvania, nor about my (non-affiliated-but-uses-them-anyway-for-me-cuz-I-love-them) GP.

If you're down that way and need a compounding pharmacy (who will do your BHRT without any doctor involvement whatsoever until prescription stage, which means if your'e gonna do over the counter and out of pocket anyway, they can be your "everything") then talk to the people at Bert's Pharmacy in Sayre, PA. They can order your labs from ZRT for you, do your intake, give you your recommendations, and fax them to your doctor if you have one.

(I'm not affiliated with them and haven't even ever been there yet but so far I'm so pleased that might as well put in a good word. Because I'll sure as heck come back and put in a bad one if I don't like them later.  )

### Inger — 2015-01-09T20:50:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152248>

You are a tough woman for sure.......
nodes popping up and you seem pretty relaxed...lol I am a bit the opposite... i would get nervous with stuff like that... i once all of a sudden had a bump on my neck... where the lymph nodes are... big as a hazelnut (in shell!) and it was hard and did not hurt by touching.. i was so freaked out I immediately stopped every bad habit i had (smoked a little... had wine every day... etc) and did what good for health i knew of back then... it is several years ago... the bump stayed with me for a month or so... and then all of a sudden got smaller and then disappeared totally.. never came back. I had hubby examine the bump every day if it got bigger or smaller... i was too scared to do it myself
I did not went to the doctor at all i was sure it was cancer and i will die if they start cutting me up and feed me chemo...lol Now i never know what it was but so glad it went away  Today i am not scared to go to the doctor at all.... after meeting Jack  if i could and had the money i would go all the time...lol

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-09T21:59:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152256>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=152248)
>
> You are a tough woman for sure.......
> nodes popping up and you seem pretty relaxed...lol I am a bit the opposite... i would get nervous with stuff like that... i once all of a sudden had a bump on my neck... where the lymph nodes are... big as a hazelnut (in shell!) and it was hard and did not hurt by touching.. i was so freaked out I immediately stopped every bad habit i had (smoked a little... had wine every day... etc) and did what good for health i knew of back then... it is several years ago... the bump stayed with me for a month or so... and then all of a sudden got smaller and then disappeared totally.. never came back. I had hubby examine the bump every day if it got bigger or smaller... i was too scared to do it myself
> I did not went to the doctor at all i was sure it was cancer and i will die if they start cutting me up and feed me chemo...lol Now i never know what it was but so glad it went away  Today i am not scared to go to the doctor at all.... after meeting Jack  if i could and had the money i would go all the time...lol

Oh wow, I'm so glad it went away and you haven't had trouble since! You knew what to look for obviously.. hard, only on one side, doesn't hurt.. that's how mine is (EDIT: I mean, how mine \*are\*.. yikes). They ought to swell on both sides when you have an infection but that hasn't been the case for me with these particular nodes, which is part of the problem.

You're so kind and sweet to call me a tough woman, and maybe I am (as are you!) but in this case I think it is just the opposite... because I just got done going through this process with the lymph node on the right side that's still there.. from the time I found it until the time I got the word that they thought it was okay, it took over three months. I know that this one will probably go away within a week if it's an infection issue.. and if it gets bigger FAST then I'll go in soon. But if it stays the same and doesn't go away after a week, then I will go see my oncology guy and have him take a look. But no point in rushing to start the process right now, because once I start, I worry. And it will be months... so might as well try to push back the months of worry until after my birthday.

So, not so much "tough" as "realistic and a little bit bratty"... LOL.. because my regular doctor looked at it yesterday and said to call the oncologist. I said I disagreed for now because I thought it was the little scab nearby and he said "maybe" and left it at that.

I think that he and Dr. L, the oncologist, probably know something I don't but that's okay. I can ask for more info and choose not to, and I'm absolutely sure they realize that and are acting accordingly. Dr. L. had said it's not lymphoma "or not one that you will probably have to treat any time soon at least, and maybe never" which I realize has very real possible implications attached. But I also know my bloodwork and all my CT scans showed that there's nothing else going on for now, or wasn't when I got them done between August and December.

Anyway.. all this being the case I know that if it grows fast I have to act fast but if it stays here like it is and stops growing I'll wait and see him next week. And it'll probably go away by then anyway, statistically speaking. He gave me a good worry free Christmas and New Year, and i want to steal my birthday too before going back for more testing, if it comes to that. So... selfish, not "tough".

### Penny — 2015-01-10T01:39:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152274>

great news about the labs - let's hope there's some kind of seafood you're not allergic to... I wonder if massaging the lymph would help? CT on the tramp? K2 would be great to take with vitamin d - mk7 - I think - because it stays longer -

Colored bulbs are usually at the store for 3.00 and are incandescent - they sell the mercury ones, but ignore them...

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-10T10:59:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152302>

Hi

Just passing on something I found useful when I started (when the cold mains water wasn't cold enough and I had nowhere to store lots of ice). I used to make bars of soap sized ice. I could use them in a cold shower like soap. I would just keep the ice soap moving over the skin, its easy to control skimming some areas, (just working on the temperature receptors) or pressing down/massaging others.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-14T13:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152726>

I just woke to find 13 new test results in my health account online.

I will make a new thread in the labs section at some point today or tomorrow detailing it but right now the thing that sticks out the most to me is the trace amounts of blood and ketones in the urine. Will be interesting to find out why that is. Also my lipid panel has gone quite a bit worse since August, which was already worse than the time or two previous. Now I'm actually out of range for LDL. Also of note - I was tested 2 days after stating vitamin d3 supplementation of 5000 units (i forget the unit of measure) per day and my result was still only 38.9 (standard range is 32-100). Pretty cruddy.

OK well I'll come back in a bit to post specifics, but thought I'd put those down quick. And noipe, I do not have a UTI because it doesn't feel like the end of hte world when I pee, so not sure why the blood in the urine... :S

I will have to wait for my next period and count backwards. Thankfully (or not!) that should be any day now.

And my doc didn't do any testosterone testing which stinks because I really think DHEA turns into testosterone when I take it. But now we'll never know. :S

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-14T13:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152728>

By way of an actual update: had a bigtime scare yesterday.

I bought a seaweed salad from Wegman's along with some veggie sushi like I've been doing for several days now (cooking steak for breakfast, veggie sushi for lunch, and dinner is generally unscripted as of yet while i ease the whole family into a new way of eating - so far they all are really into it which is awesome!).

But yesterday I opened the salad and took a bite and while swallowing it I thought "this tastes fishy" and then saw on the ingredients the caution that it may contain crustaceans and shellfish. I ALWAYS check ingredients very carefully, and have for at least 20 years (since I found out I was allergic to clams and probably scallops, at least).

Well I rinsed my mouth out with water and went and woke up my 19 year old son and told him get his shoes on and grab his keys, we live 30 minutes fom the hospital and my doctor is on vacation and I my throat was already touching itself from the swelling.

I do not have an epi pen... and I am allergic to benadryl. My doctor's nurse said to call 911. I didn't want to. She wanted me to promise. I wouldn't. I was thinking to myself, last reaction was to a whole dozen clams. This was ONE BITE of non-shellfish that just may contain traces... how bad could it really get? Dumb but still what I decided to do. But I did go into town and spend the day within 5 minutes of hte hospital. I got my hair colored. She did a great job.

After a couple of hours I returned to normal... but I will not be taking any chances any more. I need to get my allergy test results back before I add in ANYTHING else. I should've known better. I had two kids with me when I bought that salad and I thought I checked the ingredients but I guess I got distracted with them... and I can't let that happen ever again.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-14T14:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152733>

ok so, my test results.... (BTW they use Quest Diagnostics, if it matters - these were all done on January 9, 2015)...

1\. my urinalysis is posted above of course.

2\. Urine - Beta-HCG: **negative**

3\. Vitamin D25 Hydroxy: (STANDARD RANGE: 32.0 - 100.00 ng/mL) - MINE: **38.9**
INTERPRETATION: "Sufficiency" (under 32 is "Insufficiency" and over 100 is "Potential toxicity")

4\. Cardio CRP: (STANDARD RANGE: 0.00 - 1.00 mg/L) - MINE: **0.85**

5\. TSH: (STANDARD RANGE: 0.47 - 4.68 uIU/mL) - MINE: **2.0**

6\. T3, Total: (STANDARD RANGE: 80 - 200 ng/dL) - MINE: **124**

7\. Free T4: (STANDARD RANGE: 0.8 - 2.2 ng/dL) - MINE: **1.0**

8\. Lipid Profile - fasting (note that it's gotten worse and worse lately.. ugh):

9\. Cortisol \[ blood was taken after fasting for 12 hours, about 5 hours after waking. I get up at 5:30am, they took this blood at 10:25am.\] : (STANDARD RANGE: 4.5 - 22.7 ug/dL) - MINE: **8.1
**
_**NOTE -** FOR THE NEXT FEW TESTS YOU PROBABLY NEED TO KNOW MY CYCLE DAY ON TESTING ... THAT"S TRICKY. I have about a 28 day cycle pretty darn regularly, so if I'm due about the 18th (give or take a couple of days) then t**he 9th was probably around day 19** or something, which I \*think\* is the "Luteal phase" but I don't know much about this._

10\. FSH: (STANDARD RANGE: 1.4 - 18.1 mIU/mL) - MINE: **6.7**

11\. LH: (STANDARD RANGE: 1.30 - 8.00 mIU/mL) - MINE: **10.00

LH INTERPRETATION -
PHASE RANGE (mIU/mL):
**
LH FOLLICULAR PHASE: 0.6 - 12.1 (Begins on cycle day 1 with first full day of menstruation)
LH MID-CYCLE PHASE: 27.3 - 96.1
LH LUTEAL PHASE: 0.8 - 15.5
LH POST-MENOPAUSAL: 13.1 - 86.5

12\. Estradiol: (see interpretation below) - MINE: **140**

ESTRADIOL - PHASE RANGE (pg/mL):
FOLLICULAR: 19 - 83
MID-CYCLE: 150 - 528
LUTEAL: 58 - 157
POST-MENOPAUSAL: 0 - 31

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-15T12:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152840>

**A HA!!!!!**

Is THIS The autoimmune disease we've been waiting to see manifest since 2006?? And I had to order it myself? Really?

Anyone recognize this? Is this Hashimoto's, or Graves?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-15T14:41:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-152843>

"The majority of anti-TPO antibodies are produced by thyroid infiltrating lymphocytes, with minor contributions from lymph nodes and the bone marrow.\[8\] They cause thyroid cell damage by complement activation and antibody dependent cell cytotoxicity.\[6\] However, anti-TPO antibodies are not believed to contribute significantly to the destruction of the thyroid"

So THAT is what the lymph nodes on either side of my neck are swollen from... they're probably busy filtering out the debris that is bits of my thyroid that these autoantibodies are mutilating as we speak. How much you wanna bet.

Amazing.

I had to order MY OWN TESTS to find this after being medicated since Feb of 2009 for debilitating fatigue, memory loss, weight gain, some constipation issues, cold intolerance, peripheral neuropathy (I had a stinkin EMG done because of this, no joke!), joint pains diagnosed as early osteoarthritis, etc etc etc.

IT's been my freakin thyroid. This is the hidden autoimmune disorder all my docs have said they were waiting to rear it's head. My T3, T4, and TSH are always in normal ranges and they never tested anything else.. shoot it was hard enough to get them to test those all ot once, they tend to pick and choose ONE to test when I say I have fatigue, then ask if I want a psychiatrist!!

It's my thyroid. Today is my birthday and I got the best gift ever... an ANSWER. A direction to move in. Amazing. I am 42 now and will spend this year getting my shit together. And cannot wait!

(EDITED TO ADD: I wonder if this is why I sometimes get a sensation on the left side of my throat/neck that feels like my windpipe cracks/rubs against itself/something else? I was scared that I had cancer growing into my windpipe for awhile because it felt so creepy... holy cow. I hope this answers everything. If these 15 pounds come off as easily and quickly and inexplicably as they came on I would be SO HAPPY. Does thyroid meds help lose the weight that thyroid problems help you put on? Oh I hope so!!)

I'm glad to have the chance to do better now that I know better.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-16T21:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153042>

Today I saw JK, my ladyparts doc. Had my annual, talked to her about BHRT soon here, and told her about recent test results, which she pulled up on her computer. I could see over her shoulder thaat my allergy test results were in so I asked her about them (I expect to find them on my online health portal tomorrow morning, but the doctors get access a day sooner). She printed me off my own copy.

Bottom line: my doctor ordered a basic allergy test, NOT the food allergy test, so now all I know is that I am NOT allergic to any kind of pollen or dander or dust mite.

However, my IgE was flagged as high at 143 (reference range: < 115 KU/L). She said "I don't understand this, your IgE is high but you're not allergic to any of these" and I replied, "well yeah, I asked to be tested for food allergies, specifically all shellfish and seafood, but that's all environmental stuff that I already know I have no problems with".

\*sigh\*

So I called my GP and left a message asking if they can have the lab add on the food stuff at this point (blood was taken on 1/9, today is 1/16) or if I have to go give more or what. They said they'd call back.. we'll see I guess. So super frustrating though!!

do you have any previous Ig e value to compare to? I can tell you one thing...some of us appear to be born with tendency for higher IgE, no need to show allergy really. It is running int he families epigentically, usuallyt he first child being the most affected. it is common to see during the childhood issues such as adenoids, kissing tonsild, eczema, asthma... when you observe this youc an usually see elevated IgE. Boswelia seems to have some positive effect. I am this childa nd my kids are these children and my mother was this child....I made a difference with epipaleo lifestyle and intranasal red light for my kids. It is just an idea thrown onto you...

You may check Josh's journal, he did very good suppressing his allergy issues by infected himself with worms. Sorry for not posting a direct link...I am sure you find it easily...

> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/allergic-to-seafood-or-iodine-dont-believe-it.11831/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/allergic-to-seafood-or-iodine-dont-believe-it.11831/)
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-eat-epi-paleo-if-allergic-to-seafood.7588/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-eat-epi-paleo-if-allergic-to-seafood.7588/)

Those threads above are for people who are sensitive to seafood and call it being allergic.

Jack says in the second thread listed above, "very few people are truly allergic.......most have a hypersensitivity due to a weakened immune system from Calcium efflux. It can be helped."

However, what I have \*is\* a true food allergy, so I am unlike most people, and unlike the people in the threads above.

"**IgE-mediated food allergies are true food allergies involving an abnormal response of the immune system to one or more specific foods.** These reactions are associated with the rapid onset of symptoms – usually within a few minutes to a few hours after the ingestion of the offending food.

Immediate hypersensitivity reactions are mediated by an allergen-specific immunoglobulin E (IgE) antibody. The food allergens involved in IgE-mediate reactions are typically naturally-occurring proteins in foods.

In IgE-mediated food allergies, exposure to the allergen stimulates the production of allergen-specific IgE antibodies by plasma cells in susceptible individuals. The allergen-specific IgE attaches itself to the surface of mast cells in various tissues and basophils in the blood in a process known as sensitization. No symptoms occur during the sensitization phase but, once that phase is completed, the individual is primed to react to the offending allergen.

Upon subsequent exposure to the allergenic food, the allergen cross-links IgE molecules on the surface of the mast cell or basophil membrane causing these cells to release various mediators of the allergic reaction into the bloodstream and tissues."

[http://food.unl.edu/allergy/ige-mediated](http://food.unl.edu/allergy/ige-mediated)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-16T23:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153067>

Also from the link above...

"Histamine is one of the most important mediators of the immediate hypersensitivity reaction. Histamine alone can elicit inflammation, pruritis (itching), and contraction of the smooth muscles in the blood vessels, gastrointestinal tract, and respiratory tract. Several dozen physiologically active mediators of the allergic reaction have been identified."

QUESTION FOR ANYONE READING: I am allergic to Benadryl, which is the only antihistamine that I know of that is advisable for people with food allergies. Do you know of any other? My doctor's nurse didn't when I asked her.

It's funny, it's like my body is one step stronger than most, because it attacks the percieved antigen (real or autoimmune-style) AND simultaneously stands ready to block the most likely thing to be thrown at it - an antihistamine! My body is holding up a shield that deflects benadryl and says "KEEP OUT OR HIVES YE SHALL HAVE!" while using the sword in it's other hand to simultaneously beat off all shellfish AND some of my thyroid, apparently. geez. LMAO

"Other important mediators include various leukotrienes and **prostaglandins.** The released mediators interact with receptors in various tissues eliciting a wide range of physiologic responses. Because the mediators are released into the bloodstream, systemic reactions involving multiple tissues and organs can ensue."

THOUGHT: For my glaucoma I take a PROSTAGLANDIN ANALOGUE. Prostaglandins are something your body makes and uses to fight off antigens, whether it is a food-based antigen, or an auto-immune reaction to a perceived antigen due to mimicry. Why is it helpful to my optic nerve for me to put it into my eye every night?

hmmm. So you ingest an antigen that you have a true food, IgE-Mediated food allergy to, and histamine goes batty inside your body causing much of the allergic reaction. You can't take an anti-histamine to combat that effect because you get big monster hives from benadryl. Now what? Throat closes within minutes of exposure to even trace amounts of shellfish, so time is of the essence.

What is there to do?

That terrifies me.

What does an epi pen really do? How much time does it buy?

I need to educate myself.

Today I finally figured out that "BG" doesn't mean "Background" or "Blog" it seems to mean "Brain - Gut". So I'm gonna guess that "FBG" means "F?? ### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-17T15:06:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153129>

One question though ... some things are written by someone who clearly isn't Jack. They refer to "Dr. Kruse" but also say things like "me" and "I"... but never say who the heck they are! Like here - [http://jackkruse.com/setting-up-your-water-system/](http://jackkruse.com/setting-up-your-water-system/) - "As you all know, I’m pretty deep in my journey to optimal health. Last year when Dr. Kruse first taught us about the importance of reverse osmosis water, I set up two water stations in my house and have had reverse osmosis water delivered so I’ve got good quality drinking water at my fingertips."

Who even wrote that? There is no author on this article. I have a difficult time reading information and taking it seriously when I don't know the qualifications of the individual writing it, so it would be helpful t have the author's name listed, IMO. I can tell by the vernacular used in the article that it is somebody from the USA, southern area. But that's the best guess I have at this point.

My husband wasnt going to get an R/O system installed when the water company advised it so he definitely won't do so when a random person advises to.. I need source info! LOL.. not that we're ever going to get R/O. We have to build onto the house to do that, the system is immense. But you get my point. Who is the mystery writer?

(NOTE: I'm not talking about reverse osmosis systems that are under the sink, or that are for novelty/health purposes only. I'm talking about whole-home R/O systems.

We have bad well water that has been tested as contaminated with e-coli and 14 forms of total coliform bacteria, after getting my husband so sick he had to take cipro and flagyl for months and then start his whole diet from scratch reintroducing one food at a time, etc.

We had to either:
1\. sell the house,
2\. dig a new well and re-install the entire water system in our home,
or
3\. filter the bacteria out.

The water company said the best way to get rid of as much bacteria as possible will be R/O installed between the well and the house, but our laundry room is where the water supply enters the home and they said the system includes two HUGE tanks which cannot sit outdoors in upstate NY because of the freezing pipe potential, so we would've had to build ointo our home just to house the massive tanks/system. This was all YEARS before I ever found this site or heard of Jack, so my information comes not from healthful stuff but from actual life or death health based information and needs. Anyway, we had to opt for an in-line UV system instead to kill hte bacteria.

The page listed above says something about "don't install whole home RO because it will ruin your pipes". Really? Says who? Not Auchinachie. Sure, when one googles Reverse osmosis ruins your pipes, you find a page with all those keywords but if you actually click the link and READ the page, it says THIS:

"Let’s review **water softening systems**. They don’t filter anything out of the water unless they are accompanied by an RO system. Residents still are bathing in contaminated water. They **can cause damage to pipes i**n the home and the municipal water treatment plants."

This is why I require primary sources, or at LEAST author credentials. RO doesn't ruin your pipes. Water softeners CAN hurt your pipes.. depending on a number of factors. Of course the most important is whether you have old copper piping or new PVC. But I guess I better stop here, I don't want my intense love - and NEED - of primary sources to be mistaken for snarkiness.)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-17T15:31:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153133>

"The best kind of swimming pool is an ozonated pool. Salt water is the second best, though halogens are used to cleanse it. For in-ground pools, the best materials are rebar, ceramic, rock or concrete instead of a liner (these items all conduct the earth’s magnetic field, so you’re grounded while in the pool)."

Interesting to note that when we had our pool installed the code enforcement officer made us ground it. That's because it has an electric pump and filtration system, like all pools (except kiddie pools). And electrical stuff always has to be grounded.

All pools are grounded.

I don't get the difference. Unless the point is "the POOL is grounded but you are not because when you're inside it you are standing on a plastic liner". In which case I would reply "I will keep one hand on the metal edge". BAM. problem solved.

(PS you cannot make a pool liner out of rebar, :lol... )

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-17T16:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153136>

Also... Creatine is not the same thing as Creatinine.

Usually you see BUN: Creatinine ratios talked about but once in awhile I see someone talking about how BUN relates to Creatine instead.

Is this a mistake, like when people think that "Dominate" is the right word when they really want "Dominant"? (OMG that drives me insane. You can not BE dominate. Dominate is what you DO, Dominant is what you ARE. Example: Our team will dominate this tournament because they are the dominant team.

People would probably not screw that up so badly and so often if they'd mentally substitute a different word they understood better.. like masturbate instead of dominate for example. Would you ever say I AM MASTURBATE! No, you might say you are a masturbant if you're hot, but you wouldn't say you're a masturbate, because you know that's what you DO, not what you are. Same thing with dominate/dominant. One is what you do, the other is what you are. NOT THE SAME.)

OR are there times when you actually want to look at your creatine instead of your creatinine?

( [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creatinine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creatinine) - **Creatinine** ([/](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA_for_English)[krɪˈætɪniːn](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA_for_English#Key)[/](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA_for_English); [Greek](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greek_language): κρέας, "flesh") **is a breakdown product of [creatine phosphate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creatine_phosphate) in [muscle](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muscle)**, and is usually produced at a fairly constant rate by the body (depending on muscle mass). "

Totally different.

**Words matter just as much as context matters.**

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-21T00:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-153470>

Terrible day. I wasn't supposed to see the glaucoma specialist until May, but I asked to be seen early because the Travatan Z gives me dark circles under my eyes and on my eyelids. Good thing I went in... my pressures are back up to where they were before I started the eyedrops. These are no longer effective. This is rotten news since it's only been 10 weeks. If I blow through every available drop in that time I will be out of non-surgical options before a year has passed. I'm only 42. At this rate I am seriously looking at the very real possibility of going blind in my lifetime, and probably in under 20 years. I'm almost HOPING for 20 years the way things are accelerating.

The medicine he is having me try switching to is now twice daily instead of once, and I didn't notice until I got home that the warnings include "Patients taking beta-blockers with a history of atopy or severe anaphylactic reactions to a variety of allergens may be more reactive to repeated accidental, diagnostic, or therapeutic challenge with such allergens. **Such patients may be unresponsive to the usual doses of epinephrine used to treat anaphylactic reactions**."

No. Way.

Just, no.

I can't do this.

Diagnosed with glaucoma, referred to an oncologist for suspicion of cancer, find out I have Hashi's, narrowly avoid a trip to the ER due to exposure to some seafood that I \*STILL\* don't have allergy test results in regards to, then find out my eyes are continuing to worsen to an extent that actually has my glaucoma specialist concerned. Which I didn't think this guy could do, he's so laid back.

W.
T.
F.

I know that I'm new here. I know that I"m not wll known, and lately all that I've shown is my moody PMS side, and now this. But SERIOUSLY. Seriously. OMG.

The last thing I expected was to find out that this wasn't under control. F\*&&!!!!!!!!

WHY. I mean, I know I should be happy, right? It's not like i'm going deaf, this way I can still hear the music. And I always said if I had to lose one sense just don't take my hearing, because I just need to hear the music and it'll be okay.

But nobody is home and I just got crappy news and my eyes are STILL NOT cooperating, seriously? AND I CAN"T TAKE THESE NEW DROPS NOW?

I'm just ... out of words. Not even Jack can save my eye sight, I don't think. Not Jack, not me, not my eye doctor.

This sucks. There is SO MUCH I haven't seen yet... d@m&^!!!
