> that is a great news! I always welcome good news and make them even bigger in their greatness, because there is nothing as valuable for my health such as feeling great inside and have a smile on my face!

I really struggle with this. I have had issues with depression/anxiety for many years (since 1993 when I was first medicated for them) and it is a constant struggle to try to be positive. It is so hard with all parents and grandparents and a sibling all passed away.. and no friends that aren't just online friends. In the few weeks since we had to put our dog to sleep we adopted a new kitten and she was only home a day and a half before she had to go to the vet for emergency surgery.. she spent over a week there and we've been nursing her back to health at home for several days now since then.. being surrounded by so much illness and suffering is so hard on me. I think my thyroid also contributes to the depression.

I wish I was a happy person, really I do. I don't know how to be happy.

### Lahelada — 2015-03-12T14:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158350>

Good morning Hope! Can you play?
I mean really silly play like kids do. I start you off. You have snow still. Made a snowman/wpman/person? If it is too cold build it in stages. I made a bow and arrow from a conveniently dropped branch,really not one of my immediate needs....but it was fun...Have some innocent fun.
PS: fun dpes not have to make sense not even to you.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-12T14:51:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158351>

I had a lot of fun sledding for a little while a couple of times last month but for some odd reason we've suddenly gone from having temperatures so cold that it is dangerous, to having temperatures just warm enough to melt the snow. It isn't all gone yet though. You know what I love to do for fun? Rollerskate. I don't know how to rollerblade but I like the old fashioned rollerskates a lot. But nobody ever will go with me and I don't really want to go alone. But it would be so much fun, I would get to do something that makes me smile, gets me exercise, and I would get to listen to music the whole time which is my favorite thing in the world (aside from getting sunlight).

I wish somebody from the forum lived in my area. I would love to have somebody to do things with.

### nicld — 2015-03-12T14:58:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158352>

What's stopping you from learning how to roller blade (besides maybe not having some). It is great for the mind and body to learn something new.

I used to roller skate every weekend as a kid and then when I got older and started working in a city with great trails, I got roller blades and started doing that. When I was stupid and ignored circadian rhythms, I would go for about a 8-10 miles in the morning before work alone. It was so peaceful. Find a safe place and go for it. I also used to bike almost 20 miles in the morning before work too. I LOVE biking.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-12T15:05:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158357>

I did get a bike two summers ago and rode it twice and it's been in the garage ever since. We live where I can't ride safely because of the roads/cars/visibility issues. I like the idea of rollerskating because I can go to the indoor skating rink and it's very young-people oriented so it's very safe. I don't care if I rollerblade versus rollerskate because the skates are still available to rent there anyway.

I would love to ride my bike but I hate to do things alone and have nobody to ever do anything with. I'd be afraid to put it on the back of the jeep and drive to the local rail trail to ride it because I've never been there before and always see people walking and riding in pairs and just would be sad/lonely/paranoid the whole time about creepy people. And it's half an hour drive each way to do either of those things because we live in the middle of nowhere, so factoring in an hour driving time round trip makes it tricky sometimes because of having to be here to get my daughter off the bus each day at 3:10.. frustrating not being able to find a solution.

But really what it comes down to in the end is probably just the fact that I don't want to go do things alone. That isn't fun to me. It's just a depressing illustration of my failures and lack of friends. And my husband would absolutely freak out and probably wouldn't let me anyway. I told him I wanted a gym membership for Christmas and he bought me a yoga DVD set instead because I could do it at home. I returned it and got a digital camera. I hate being alone doing nothing all the time.

### nicld — 2015-03-12T15:12:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158360>

I LOVED riding by myself on trails and they are 30 minutes from my house too. It was so peaceful and I could go at whatever pace I wanted. I did it all the time and never felt unsafe, except for maybe hitting a deer on the bike. If there are always people around you should be safe. Challenge yourself to know what you are capable of

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-12T15:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158361>

BUT want some good news (I know I do)....

Today is Cycle Day 21 and I \*think\* my period/bleeding is finally stopped. That was only a three week long period, hehe. Fun. Can't believe I had a regular period from Feb 5 to 10 and it came back on Feb 20 and stayed thru March 12 but that's what happened. If I hadn't started progesterone when I did I don't know if it would've ever stopped. Of course now I'm due for another period in a few days but whatever. I'm thinking it will probably come late and start a whole new schedule. We'll see, but this is at least hopefully going to be a few days' break which will be a nice change!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-16T12:19:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158656>

what is the endometrial stripe? I assume it is the thickness of the endometrium... which should be thinnest at the end of your period because it's just shed.... this is weird... backk in 2009 my ultrasound showd my endometrial stripe was 2 - 3 mm with both walls measured together. In 2010 it was 4mm with both walla measured together. In my new ultrasound from lats week to try to understand why such prolonged bleeding with clots, it measured 7.5mm.

Why would it be 3x thicker than the first time and nearly 2x thicker than last time, when I was already bleeding for 20 days when they measured it? Fibroids don't evenly line the walls of the uterus... (not that I have fibroids).... what the heck? Any ideas? Not sure I'd get any information posting in any other part of the forums any better than here, but I guess I need to go lurking at some fertility boards to find out the answers to this one. Thanks to anyone who can help me troubleshoot my uterus! LOL

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-16T12:31:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-158657>

source - [http://radiopaedia.org/articles/endometrial-thickness](http://radiopaedia.org/articles/endometrial-thickness) -
Info:

"**Premenopausal**

In premenopausal patients, there is significant variation at different stages of the menstrual cycle.

-   during menstruation 1,4: 2-4 mm
-   early proliferative phase (day 6-14): 5-7 mm
-   late proliferative-pre ovulatory phase: up to 11 mm
-   secretory phase: 7-16 mm
-   following dilatation and curettage or spontaneous abortion: <5 mm, if it is thicker consider [retained products of conception](http://radiopaedia.org/articles/retained-products-of-conception)."

Hm. Well, mine was done during menstruation, on day 20. Which seems contradictory but that was the whole problem. No clue what to make of it, so I guess I'll let KJ look at the results and get back to me (which will probably take quite awhile and annoy her once she gets them, because she seems to always indicate that there's a "go-between" type of person that is awfully slow to get stuff to her. We shall see.)

Not that I expect anyone to be able to weigh in on this really (haven't seen indication of any ultrasound techs or radiologists here), but my uterus is "antroverted and bulbous" in appearance as well as having this somewhat thickened endometrium (IMO), which is "fairly homogenous" in appearance.

I did stop bleeding finally around day 21, but tomorrow is day 28 so I expect a period again any time here if my body is decided to go back on schedule.

Today being day 27, I should not be taking progesterone anymore for two weeks. But I am taking it anyway because the instructions say to take it day 12-26, but I didn't pick it up until day 13 so might as well take it day 13-27 this month so as to not waste any lozenges. Once you get back to a regular schedule keep track of it and I would say adjust if needed.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-22T16:20:32+0200

maybe 50mg/day isn't enough.. maybe just taking it half a cycle isn't enough... wonder how long till i know... meanwhile spring break is less htan a week away and i sure haven't lost any weight... gained any sex drive... nothin. this year so far has been a bust.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-22T16:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159184>

[https://www.womentowomen.com/menstruation/the-menstrual-cycle/](https://www.womentowomen.com/menstruation/the-menstrual-cycle/)

that site says progesterone levels are highest at ovulation and then fall from that point forward which signals your body to menstruate (unless you get pregnant in which case they remain high), reaching it's lowest point on the last day of your period.

so... why do I start taking it on day 12 and take the exact same amount until day 26? that just makes my body think it's pregnant and having a sudden miscarriage. how the hell is this helpful??

### nicld — 2015-03-22T17:57:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159196>

Everyone is very different but mine usually shows up while I am on the cream. There are solar flares going on that seem to be effecting people (ME, ME, ME) so it may not be just the Pg. As long as you are still cycling, it is not really suggested to take it all the time. Check out Gretchen's (Shinjin), she has has a bunch of experience using Pg.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-25T13:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159406>

My oldest son has addiction issues that has him currently located in an environment against his control. He's trying to get better. I ordered him Dr. Kruse's book back when I first joined here and he's been wanting another one but there isn't another one to send to him (I can't just send him whatever I want, it has to be from amazon or another official website). Last night I spoke to him on the phone briefly and asked if there's any rule that says I can't print blogs off the internet and send those to him in an envelope along with letters from home, and he said he thinks that should be fine. He was super excited then. He was into hormones and body building and nutrition in high school, before all this terrible substance stuff took control of his life. So I just got done printing up several blogs for him and putting them in envelopes labeled "read first", "read second", and "read third"... I can't make them too thick and don't even know if they'll get to him or not but I'm gonna try.

So far he's got Dr. K's book, and I'm sending him:

Envelope number one / Read first: Introduction by Dr. Kruse plus Leptin Rx FAQs (because this envelope was light but the next one is too heavy to add this to)
Envelope number two / Read second: The Leptin Rx blog one, two (Liver), and three (why is Oprah still fat)
Envelope number three / Read third: Hormones 101 and Hormones 102

He specifically asked for the Quilt but I can't send it right now because it's so long, it takes up too many pages to send in a regular envelope, so that will have to wait.

edited to add: on a whim I just went to amazon.com and searched for the monk who sold his ferrari and found a paperback version used for a penny plus 3.99 shipping so I just had that sent to him as well. Now oI just need my own version, LOL. But I think he will like it. (edited again to say that I ordered myself one for the same price, so we'll still be on the same page.)

He told me last night on the phone that he only works out between 1-4pm now because Dr. K. said that will utilize protein for muscle building 400% better or something like that. Everyone in his 'block' has been really looking up to him for exercise and nutrition/wellness information for months now since I sent him Dr. K's book - a large group of men with nothing to lose are embracing his teachings... When you're put away like my boy is right now, they only feed you soy for protein, there is no meat whatsoever in there, which I didn't know until a couple of months ago. I told him that I understand his dilemma but soy will just give him estrogen issues and he said he sure knows it. I didn't know that was how they treat people in his situation. I guess I never cared before....)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-27T13:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159524>

\*sigh\* ah the joys of parenthood. Yesterday my daughter came home from school (sixth grade, 12 years old) and said they're doing Jumprope for Heart again this year (where they raise donations for the American Heart Association).

Me: "cool, we'll make a donation. Do you have your paperwork?"
Her: "yeah... also can we go to the craft store because I said I would do the team's t-shirts this year because we all have team colors and stuff"
Me: "ohhhhhhkayyyyy, how many people are on your team?"
Her: "Ten"
Me: " ....... um... okay well we'll look online for a coupon, I'm sure it'll be fine. What's your team's color?"
Her: "Rainbow"
Me: " ................ seriously? You couldn't just pick red or blue or something so I could just buy 10 shirts the same color? How are we going to do rainbow? We're NOT tie-dying ten shirts."
Her: "I thought I could get fabric markers or puffy paint or something and decorate them and put the team name on them."
Me: "well that's going to take some time to do ten custom t-shirts.. what's your team name, anyway?"
Her: "The Undead Fluffycorns!"
Me: "....."
Her: "oh and it's tomorrow."

....

....

So last night we went out to the craft store and bought 10 white tshirts for $25 and a 10-pack of iron-on transfer paper and I came home and designed a team logo featuring a unicorn with red eyes in front of a rainbow with the words The Undead Fluffycorns underneath in a blood-drip-type font. Each iron-on has to be ironed with both hands and full body weight for 2.5 minutes.... I did five last night and five this morning around 5:30am.... no time for fabric paint or markers to dry overnight so this was the only choice.

The things you never think you'll have to do! LOL!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-27T13:25:57+0200

PS I got A big thing of coconut oil yesterday and a tub of shucked oysters. Last time I had a reaction to seafood I had time to get to a hospital so I decided I should just take a deep breath and give it a try again. The problem is how the heck do I deal with these things? And what shall I do with the coconut oil? I'm finally trying the food component of this whole thing again after screwing up bigtime before and gaining back whatever I had lost and probably more but I do most things on autopilot so I need to make new habits in order to have the best shot at this.. and the fastest way to make new habits is to not fool around with all different ideas, but to just pick one and stick with it.. so I figure I better ask here what to do with this stuff rather than experiment too much, because that will prolly just throw me back off the wagon.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-27T15:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159552>

Yesterday I was driving home and thinking about how miserable I've been lately, emotionally, mentally, and physically. I was thinking "and if I change how i eat then that one small thing that gives me some brief pleasure will be gone and then life will REALLY suck, so I can't give up fast food and sweets and carbs"....

then I realize I had it COMPLETELY freaking backassed...

and I'm having a hard time putting the explanation into words...

basically I have been feeling all along that I'm sick, sad, frustrated, angry, bitter 90% of my current life, and have been hanging onto the 10% of the time that I'm sitting in fake light watching tv or eating total garbage food for dear life, because it has been the only thing in my life that has been giving me a little bit of happiness...

but realized this is backassed because I'm already spending 90% of my time feeling bad and not getting what I want, and the only "pay" I get for it is 10% of my time feeling brief surface pleasure from eating crap food while sitting in blue light. My net benefit from living like this is nonexistant, and in fact it's even got me in the red - I'm HURTING myself, not maintaining myself, by doing this.

The backassed part is the fact that I'm alotting 90% of my time to feeling like shit (doing nothing differently) and 10% of my time to feeling good (doing nothing differently), with a net benefit of 0 or even negatives,

when instead I could alot 90% of my time to feeling like shit (doing EVERYTHING differently) and 10% of my time feeling good (rewarding myself with something I still like to eat or watch on tv) with a net benefit of ACTUALLY GETTING HEALTHIER.

I mean I'm gonna feel like shit for 90% of my time no matter what if I do nothing different and I'm going to keep going downhill. If I'm going to feel like crap for 90% of my time anyhow should it not be because I'm sitting in a cold tub of water instead of being because I just got back some medical results that show something I don't want? Or because I'm eating unpleasant foods that I don't enjoy rather than because my husband resents my lack of sex drive? Or because my grocery bill was terribly expensive due to grass-fed meat and oysters rather than because of paying out of pocket for antibiotics and maalox?

I have been so backassed for so long that I can't even believe I ever figured this out.

Feeling bad is feeling bad... why not feel bad while working towards a solution rather than while wallowing in all the WRONG?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-27T22:49:17+0200

Thanks for the encouragement.
This is Nutiva that i bought, so I'm glad to know it's good stuff. It sure isn't cheap (this was the big jar on the shelf, it is 23 ounces and was $12.99 I think - don't know if that's average or what.. anyone ever find coupons for Nutiva?).

But I used it one time and already I'm hooked. Then hours later, when moving on auto-pilot executing a familiar routine of making my daughter an egg and cheese sandwich, I unthinkingly opened the cabinet next to the stove and took out the cooking spray stuff I've used for years and sprayed the pan down with that and poured in the eggs. I meant to use the coconut oil but I will re-organize the cabinet and put the oil where the spray usually is - or maybe just a second jar of it - so that I can make a new habit that much more easily. But this jar I keep by the coffee/near the stove because I thought I'd remember it use it there, but instead it just made me want to put it in coffee. I thought it sounded crazy until I went to the Nutiva site just now and saw that it's one of the ingredients in Bulletproof Coffee, then came here and saw you mention it. It's meant to be I guess! I think I would love it.. except right now I drink LOTS of coffee and I dont' think I'd want to drink nearly as much of this stuff... which would probably be better for me anyway.

### nicld — 2015-03-27T23:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159578>

Yes, straight from the jar. When I was working on fixing my gut, Dr. Tim had me get Lauricidin which is derived from coconut oil. I just listened to a podcast from Ben Greenfield that said the same thing about the solid coconut oil versus the liquid at room temperature (sometimes called MCT oil). What brand to you have?

I use a dark cocoa powder that I bought in bulk so I have LOTS left. It also tastes good blended with an avocado.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-28T15:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159618>

Today is CD37. Never had one of these before. Maybe my last period WAS my last period...?

\*fingers crossed\*

I have no use for them anyway. I'd rather be menoapausal on BHRT than perimenopausal on BHRT I think - seems like it'd be easier to find that hormonal sweet spot if it wasn't fluctuating anymore. But who knows.

edited to add: I am supposed to start the progesterone lozenges on the 12th cycle day each time and continue on them until day 26. So I took them from day 13 to day 27 this first cycle since I filled the Rx on day 13. Of course, I was still bleeding on day 13 when I started them, and kept on bleeding through day 21 (three week period FTL). And here we are on day 37, 16 days after stopping bleeding and no period in sight. I feel like crap without progesterone and my mood is as foul as any I've ever had, except it's lasting more than usual because the period that usually bleeds out the evil mood isn't starting.

I mean, mathematically speaking, Today should be CD9 and I should start progesterone again in just a couple of days, but instead I'm just waiting and waiting for a period to show so that I can start counting over again at CD1, so that I can eventually GET to CD 12. So until I bleed I know I'm at least 2 weeks from pg, and that's really terrible news. Having to wait for a period sucks and having to deal with a pharmacist doing the recommendations to my gyn, who then writes the rx, sends it back to the pharmacist, who waits for insurance to rule on it, etc means that I dont' think I will ever be able to adjust my dose without a massive hassle. If there is ANY cream or ANYTHING I can get over the counter I would SO MUCH appreciate a private message to et me know because this is absolutely ridiculous.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-31T15:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159927>

You're all such kind people. I'm sorry I'm such a whiny little brat. I could list reasons for my mood but that doesn't help anything. (But for the record, March 27 was my mom's birthday (she's been gone for a decade now but still feels like yesterday) and March 28 was my brother's, and he's been dead less than a year and it's just all been a little overwhelming in combination with my stupid eyes (glaucoma can be scary when you're barely in your 40s and don't trust people around you to take care of you instead of taking advantage of you) etc.

But none of that would bug me as much if I just could make my brain do what it always has done so well for me. Except with math or science. I expect too much of it or take it for granted or something. I collect two- year degrees like some children collect scraped knees, but for some reason THIS is too hard for me? I just find it really hard to accept. My brain is the one thing I have always been able to count on. I can usually shut off emotion if I have to, and logic will carry me through. With this, the most important thing I will probably ever have to learn, if I shut off emotion then I have nothing left, because my brain doesn't have anything else to go on.

But yeah. like I said, reasons/excuses/whatever means nothing in the end. No point in listing my failures in life as a mother, wife, or daughter to illustrate why I feel terrible all the time. It is what it is.

Sorry again.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-31T15:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159928>

Guys... how can I not use the computer anymore when it's the only thing that makes me not be isolated and alone?

Everyone always says my marriage is an illustration of stockholm syndrome.. I literally have zero friends, my parents and grandparents and a sibling are dead, I have not spoken to anyone on the phone in a friendly manner in 20 years because I REALLY TRULY do not have any humans in my life who ae not my husband and my children. I live out in the middle of nowhere, am forbidden to work outside the home, and live about an hour roudtrip drive from the nearest real town.

I.

Am.

Alone.

You don't understand.

Without internet I have nothing and nobody. No escape at all. Why do you think I join forums? Because it's the closest thing to human interaction I can get.

I don't know what to do.

but yeah my sleep is actually one of the only good things I have going on. That and my hair grows so damn fast.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-31T16:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-159945>

Hey ya wanna hear me say something positive for a change?

When I have my steak with coconut oil and pink himalayan salt for breakfast, I am not hungry until dinner. And then dinner is like 4 vegetable sushi pieces and then I feel like I don't want anything else.

And pretzels are starting to just annoy me, rathe than feeling like my best, most relaxing friend.

And yesterday for the first time in my life I had a few pieces of easter candy and just felt slightly nauseated and disgusted (my husband thinks that bringing bags of candy home every time he upsets me is the way to fix it no matter how much I beg him to PLEASE stop sabotaging me).

But then I went back to Wegmans to buy more steaks and they didn't have anymore of the kind I can afford, so this morning I have no plans as of yet. oh well.

Can't afford grass fed organic meat at $36 per pound or whatever it was though. Just a complete impossibility, so no point in feeling bad about that. I can only feel bad about things I could do better but don't, not the things I have no control over.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-01T01:41:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160000>

Other people have always drained my energy,requiring me to get alone-time to regroup/recharge/reboot. Interesingly, I never knew my extended family on my father's side until the advent of facebook, due to not keeping in touch after my father died when I was barely four years old.. but when I went to the reunion a few years ago for the first time I was struck by how they all laughed when I told them something I'd been agonizing about.. seeming so weird or antisocial.. asked if there might be a quiet room where I could escape to recharge a bit if needed, and they explained that most of the family spends most of their time in the designated "quiet room" and I fit right in.

anyway... I'm sorry again.. and thank you lovely people for being here for me... I don't mean to make it sound like I'm throwing my hands up and doing nothing.. I can afford the grass fed organic GROUND beef, just not steaks, so I can/do get that... but like steak for breakfast at least for now as I transition my diet, even though it's not organic or anything... better than yogurt and toast I've been having for years... and I'm using my Nutiva coconut oil (my middle son loves to cook and is wonderful at it and he's loving this stuff too. I told him today to use a little bit on his finger and rub it onto his chapped lips and he thought that was excellent as well)... and tonight I made myself about a third of a pound of ground beef with my pink himalayan salt and coconut oil and had it with onion and garlic powders and a side of kimchi (which reminds me of a cross between the ginger salad dressing at the local hibachi place and dill pickle brine, with a kick)... I'm tryin... but we have girl scout cookies in the house. those things are evil, man...

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-01T14:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160045>

And now just a note in general to my blog:

I wish I could get blood tests run this morning. I feel much less terrible this morning than each morning for the past week. Usually that means I shuold get my period today, and I'd love to know what it is that happens to me that is so predictable like this. I get one calm day (period day one) and then my anxiety and frustration ramps up from there all month long, getting as bad as it can possibly be for the one week prior to my period starting (which I call PMS week).

Problem is, I can't count on it starting today THIS time.

Got my tubes tied at 30, had regular 28 day cycles with periods lasting 4.5 days or so the whole time until I was 42 this year, and then suddenly my period came about a week early on Feb 5, lasted til Feb 10, went away... came back Feb 20 and lasted for 21 days, through March 12. My gyn ordered an ultrasound which was done on day 20 of bleeding and it showed my uterine lining was still 7.5mm thick. Stopped bleeding on cycle day 21.... and today is cycle day 41. Still no period.

Does it ever just stop forever THAT fast and suddenly?

I'm sitting her with a progesterone subscription all set to go but can't take it till cycle day 12... it's like my body is just dragging it's feet here... yeesh.

I actually filled the subscription last month on cycle day 13 - which happened to be day 13 of "still bleeding". The pharmacist and my gyn said to go ahead and start it, take it through cycle day 26, andd said maybe it'd help to stop that period and get me on track again. We were thinking what a great coincidence it was that the same time my period went crazy, I had progesterone lozengest to start taking. But now it just is adding to my confusion ofo what the heck could be going on.. ?? And I rerally want to go back on them ASAP. I felt half human on them after a couple of weeks, and just as I was satarting to feel pretty good it was time to stop for the month and I've been a royal bitch ever since.

I need more pg!!

And I really want to know what's the most likely reason for the bleeding and then NOT. crazy.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-01T14:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160046>

Today is april fool's day, which I have not participated in a single time in the 21 years I've been married.

I should go tell my husband I'm pregnant, and that's why I'm not bleeding yet. "I just got a follow up letter from my gyn regarding my ultrasound... THERE"S A BABY IN THERE OMG THE TECH COULDN"T TELL ME BECAUSE OF HOSPITAL RULES SO BY NOW I"M LIKE TWO MONTHS ALONG"

then I'd have to call an ambulance because chances are he'd knock himself out cold when he faints.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-01T14:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160050>

Just got this in an email titled "Women make better Asassins".

Too funny not to share:

**The CIA had an opening for an assassin…**

\> After all the background checks, interviews and

\> testing were done, there were three finalists: two men

\> and a woman. For the final test, the CIA agents took one of

\> the men to a large metal door and handed him a gun. "We

\> must know that you will follow your instructions no matter

\> what the circumstances. Inside the room you will find

\> your wife sitting in a chair. Kill her.”

\> The man said “You can't be serious. I could never shoot my wife". The agent said,

“Then you are not the right man for this job. Take your wife and go home".

\> The second man was given the same instructions.

\> He took the gun and went into the room. All was quiet

\> for about five minutes. The man came out with tears in his

\> eyes, "I tried, but I can't kill my wife." The agent said, "You don't have

\> what it takes, so take your wife and go home."

Finally, it was the woman's turn. She was

\> given the same instructions: to kill her

\> husband. She took the gun and went into the room. Shots

\> were heard one after another.

\> They heard screaming, crashing, and banging on

\> the walls. After a few minutes, all was quiet. The door

\> opened slowly and there stood the woman,

\> wiping sweat from her brow. "The gun was loaded with blanks." she

\> said. "I had to kill him with the chair."

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-02T14:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160121>

What do you all do around 4pm when you get hungry again after your BAB? That's when my day falls apart.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-02T15:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160125>

4pm is close to dinner time . You. Should be hungry by then. Between 4 and 6 is your ideal window to eat protein,so if your dinner is not yet then a protein snack would be gut. If you can find good salami or raw ham that would be excellent,paired with olives and or sugar free mixed pickles.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-02T15:16:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160127>

If I need something at 4 pm I have some beef jerky, almonds, or buttered pecans. I found a good nitrate free, sugar free jerky at a local store.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-02T15:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160129>

Those are great suggestions, thanks! 4pm is a time that I am perfectly happy having dinner.. I tend towards two meals a day anyway BUT then I snack all night, so I need that second meal to be as filling as the first one (BAB with steak).

I do like black olives, but not the little green ones with pimentos in the holes. I've never tried any others but Wegman's has a large "Mediterranean bar" that has all kinds of olives and other things that you can take home, like a salad bar. I could check that out.

I've never heard of sugar-free pickles but then again I never looked to see what the sugar content IS in pickles before! So that's something else for me to consider, I'm glad it was mentioned. Also never heard of raw ham.. but that reminds me that we do have two hams in the fridge that need to be cooked because my mother in law got one on sale she didn't actually need and I'd already bought one for easter dinner. I could cook that for tonight maybe.

I love the idea of buttered pecans, love almonds, and would also love to find GOOD beef jerky that isn't too terribly expensive (because the packaged stuff is already very expensive and I'm sure something fresher would be even more so).

Hm. Not sure what to do here. Would rather not buy a bunch of snacky stuff because I'll just eat that and not do dinner at all. That's 'how I usually do things... cook my BAB, feel fine until my daughter gets home from school, she wants to eat when she gets her at 315 so I make her an egg and cheese on raisin bagel with chocolate milk (she's a creature of habit like me so this is a daily routine) and then by 4pm I'm also hungry so I usually ask her if she wants to go down to Dunkin Donuts with me for a latte, which I have them throw whipped cream onto every time (habit). Then once we get back home y latte is mostly gone and it's time to sit on the couch and watch several hours of Seinfeld and The Big Bang Theory while browsing the internet and eating pretzels until bedtime.

So yeah as you can see, from 315 onward my day is a mess but I can't function without routine. So I have to know what is breakfas,t what is lunch, what is snack/dinner, or else I can't do it. I just default to auto-pilot. I do try, I just can't help it.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-02T15:31:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160130>

I should also mention that I get up at 530 and drink coffee until about 10am when I finally make breakfast. Can't eat when I first wake up. Besides then I would have to figure out how to deal with even more hours without food. This way I'm only going from 10-3 or 4 or whatever. If I eat at 6am I won't get her to the bus on time, so I have to wait until at least 645-7 at the very earliest.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-02T18:24:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160142>

When I was doing the Leptin reset and BAB, I had to make food preps ahead of time. I am up and out of the house by no later than 5:45 am. I mixed up my own version of breakfast sausage with ground pork and seasoning. it helped to cook up servings in advance of meats and then I could quickly scramble some eggs with it. BPC was a staple too.

I even ate shrimp for breakfast because it was easy. Boil water, throw in shrimp...

Leftovers were a lifesaver too.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-02T20:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160153>

Hi Hope,it is vital that you eat within 30 mins of waking , preference does not come into it. You do not have to subsist on two meals a day though. You can and should have lunch at "lunchtime" . As you regain leptin sensitivity your hunger will fall off and you can do 2 meals quite comfortably. Don't skip lunch trying to "diet" at the same time. Maybe you could wean your DD off the nighttime bagel and pretzels and you could make her dinner your breakfast. Maybe make different dinners together. Gerts you away from "the blue light source" for a bit. Meatballs for her dinner , cold meatballs for your breakfast ,things like that. I suggest meatballs as they are protein dense so if you do not have an appetite it is easier to force down 8 0zs of meatballs than have 6 eggs and ham .Then again you might prefer the second.
You wondered about my expression "raw ham" earlier .What I mean is things like Black forest ham or Parma ham ,stuff like that. Bresaola is good too. Don\`t worry it is not vital that you get these. Do get some olives though. Being products of lactic fermentation they will do you good. Sauerkraut as well .I suggest it as it helps with restructuring your gut flora . ( tensegrity blog ,one of them ; 2 or 3 I believe)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-02T20:48:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160155>

Thanks to you both.  I have a difficult time with meatballs/ground beef in the morning, theyy seem too dry to me so far the times I've tried to make that work. When I don't have steak I prefer to substitute eggs, but I don't like yolks very much. I've been trying to add in at least one or two yolks though to break my eggwhites-only habit that I had made years ago when I thought ALL cholesterol was bad ALL the time. I will have to come up with a small menu of things I can always have in the house to make evening/nighttime easier.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-02T21:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160156>

As a refresher, before my update post -

the way I'm getting my BHRT may or may not be different than most people. Not really sure, but I live in NY state which makes self-testing tricky, BUT my gynecologist is in Pennsylvania, so sometimes we have to jump through hoops to make things work. For exammple, many years ago when I was on depo-provera I had to go to the pharmacy to fill the Rx to pick up the shot in NY then hand-carry it to my doc in PA for them to be able to give it to me and have insurance cover each part of it. So I don't know if my BHRT situation is also convoluted, but here's how it works:

My and my GP are in NY. My gyn (Karen) and my compounding pharmacy are in PA. My GP doesn't "do" BHRT, so he told me to go through my gyn down in PA.

My gyn down in PA, Karen, told me that she will prescribe it BUT she has her patients go for a free consult first with the local compounding pharmacy down there, whose pharmacists specialize in BHRT and wellness. So I had to go for my consult with Lee, the compounding guy, who then faxed his recommendations back to Karen, who then discussed them with me and wrote the prescriptions once we were all on board.

It's about a 45-50 minute drive down there each way for me, so to avoid having to go back for tons of consults or appointments, I try to utilize phone/email.

So today I called the pharmacy and asked to talk to someone about my progesterone dosage, and they put Paula on the phone who is another one of the great pharmacists there who has helped me before. I explained that I still don't have a period and she remembered who I was and everything about this messed up cycle and apologized and said that they try to be sure to let ladies know that if they don't get a period when it's expected, do this: On day 29 of your cycle, call it day one whether your period comes or not, and when day 12 comes aroudn take your progesterone as scheduled for days 12-26. So just always count by 28's and always behave accordingly when day 12 comes around and just take it no matter what.

BUT she said because my cycle is all messed up AND I didn't realize that so haven't taken it, she said she just refilled it for me right now while we were talking and I could come down and pick it up and start it right now today and call today day 12 and go from here taking it thru day 26, then stop, as scheduled.

Then I said that's awesome, but you know what? This dosage barely takes the edge off and I'm a freaking raving lunatic every day I'm not on PG until it gets so bad that I'm like.. THIS. LOL. So she said ok, let's do this, let me call Karen with a new recommendation... I want you on it every day but we'll do a lower dose from days 1-11 and a higher dose from days 12-26. I said YES PLEASE AND THANK YOU YOU"RE AWESOME ... and btw... I changed my mind about iodine, I'm not waiting for the endo, I want it now. She said okeydoke, we'll fill that too, then mentioned it'll be topically applied but said she'll give me full instructions in a few days when the new Rx's come in from Karen and get filled and I come get them.

So I hun up the phone and realized I had pg in my purse, two lozenges left ove fom last month becuase I didn't pick them up till day 13. So I took one and I swear by the time it was done dissolving I was having a hot flash that lasted an hour. Then when that subsided I started feeling a little bit relaxed and by the time a few hours had gone by I was feeling almost tolerable.

What an amazing difference!

I hope that Karen gets the message fast and gets back to the pharmacist before easter sunday but if not that's okay, at least I'm doing SOMETHING and have some very helpful ladies helping me out along the way! And my endo is a female too even though I haven't met her yet so that really gives me a lot of hope \*fingers crossed\*. She's at the same place as Karen and they're a fantastic medical place so I think that's hopefully going to be very helpful as well, since they share records very easily on their computer system there and can both instantly access my info any time they need to get on the same page.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-03T02:18:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160171>

From this other side of the perspective-fence, my progesterone lozenges are delicious. LOL. Because it means I HAVE SOME PROGESTERONE IN ME. I don't even care that I hated the taste before - now it doesn't bug me a bit. Change isn't that hard, just gotta stick with it long enough to realize that fact each time. Funny how that works.

Seriously though, I love this stuff. I hope I get a lot more hot flashes when I get on it daily, because that makes me WANT to do CT. That's the next step. Funny.. I used to tell people that one of my tricks for looking and feeling so good (back when I \*did\* look and feel so good) was that I ended every shower with cold water. Haven't done that in a few years now... should be easier to start again but it hasn't been. Time to just make that a routine no matter what as well. It's FREE. Why not?

And today it was like 62 outside randomly amidst the low-40-degee days we've still got up here in upstate NY. I was outside in just a short sleeve shirt and jeans taking all the lights off the trees outside (don't worry I don't turn them on after new year's but it's been such a thick snow layer that I haven't been able to get the lights down since Christmas!). Beautiful day! I could be outside in shorts in that weather without any trouble at all as long as I kept moving. But it's going to get back in the lower 40's almost instantly for awhile so I won't get the chance for a bit yet.

But yeah, had my other progesterone lozenge already this evening and I feel so much better. This is amazing. I'm tolerable!!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-03T02:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160172>

Earlier when I was hungry around 4pm I found some salami I had in the fridge (prolly not the right kind but I'm doin my best right now) and got a couple more spoonfuls of kim chi. resisted the yogurt urge since I'm pretty sure dairy is inflammatory. When I went out a little bit ago I grabbed some antipasta since it's olives and salami and onions and whatnot. It has some cheese but I cut out milk and cereal and also yogurt, so I'll allow some cheese in the antipasta for now. Hopefully these are acceptable compromises for now. We'll see how I feel tomorrow. Also got anothehr Wildbrine product (I got their miso horseradish kim chi a few days ago) - this one is a curried cauliflower slaw. Hoping that's also good for me.

Great stuff Hope  So happy for you
I would stay off dairy...... or if... just have some great quality raw cheeses.....
I do not eat pasteurized dairy. Does me no good....... (except grassfed butter) If I cheat with cream I get pimples, yeah... not so fun

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-03T14:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160218>

Thanks! I wonder if dairy is the reason why I do get pimples in batches every so often? Then they take forever to go away.
(Or if I take DHEA in pill form it turns straight to \[painful, scarring\] cystic acne, which Karen said happens to her too, so that made me feel great that she GETS it.)

edited to add - I slept so much better last night with pg in my system, it's not even funny. I really don't want to ever NOT have this in me. It's so nice not to be cold all the time.. the hot flashes feel like little hints of normalcy to come. \*fingers crossed\*

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-03T14:37:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160219>

A page or two back, Josh had suggested some spiritual-type links/activities/readings to me, and at the time I was reminded of the fact that there is a "Holistic Resource" guide that is printed up every month and there's usually a stack of them on the windowsill in the lobby of my GPs office, so I had been thinking about going to get one and look into local yoga teachers or to see if maybe there's a naturopathic doctor advertised in there or something, since my little area of upstate NY is largely cornfields and cows and people in cowboy hats who don't understand that the North East is as opposite from the South West as it can be.. and pretty sure we all know cowboy hats are for people in the south or people out west, but who ever saw someone in NYC or Boston wearing a cowboy hat anyway??

LOL

BUT point being it's not easy to find resources for unconventional stuff around here. I still don't know where to buy white sage for example. But I digress.

So I happened across a Holistic Resource guide for my area last night at Wegmans and have it here and it's FULL of stuff.

The only thing that for some reason jumped at me was one tiny classified ad in there that offers private lessons in "Pranayama Yoga" and says it's by one of "the very few" Pranayama Yoga teachers in the USA.

I wonder if there's any exaggeration going on there? And I wonder why my spirit was attracted to THAT ad instead of any other?

edit - oh now i know why it sounds familiar, it's mentioned in the Bhagavad Gita. It's been a long time but my second degree was in liberal arts - philosophy of religion, but it was just a 2 year degree that I didn't get to transition into a 4-year one at the university because my back went out in Sept 2006 and everything between then and now is listed in my opening post... it's been health that held me back ever since. That, and no longer having the same goals. But I'm still pleased that I chose such vastly different degree programs for each one. Computers/Bus Info Mgmt/ Web Design was fun for a long time but I already knew that wasn't the best option for me, I guess, becasue SOMETHING pulled me from computers towards spirituality between 1998 and 2005 (my computer degree program took me from 98-00, the spiritual one took me from 05-06).

Anyway. Rambleramble. It's the pg. I'm so much more pleasant this morning than every other morning for the past few weeks eh?

oh yeah, and it's like 66 inside and not even that warm outside and i'm running around in shorts and a tank top today. and sweating after awhile. Which means wantingn more water, so I've been chugging it. I feel so much better already.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-03T21:25:31+0300

Then printed him up his own copy of Dopamine =- good choices or bad? all three sections. Which I also already sent my oldest boy. I need to print him up some more material. I don't know if he wants to be an endocrinologist or a wellness doc or what but no matter what, I can spoonfeed him his sources for now so I'm printing him up all of Dr K's blogs and mailing him several envelopes a day now that I know he's receiving them just fine!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-06T16:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160419>

Hm. yesterday I definitely took the day off from eating right, cooked my normal breakfast steak but indulged in plenty of chocolate. Also cooked ham, mashed potatoes, and green beans. Then had some cake with icing and one bite of key lime pie. At that point my stomach started gurgling and sounding like it used to, before I changed how I eat. Not sure if it was the dairy in the chocolate or key lime pie, or the carbs from the cake and potatoes or what...

this morning I woke up starving and made coffee like normal but I'm definitely going to have to cook breakfast earlier than normal. Funny how eating way MORE than usual yesterday (all of it crap) left me hungrier than usual today. I can eat less, but better, and it leads to me having a smaller appetite which leads to me continuing to eat less.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T14:22:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160513>

That was definitely a smudge on you screen. I would never admit to anything so deliciously wrong.... \*whistles innocently while looking the other direction\*

So, this progesterone/cycle thing is really a handful to get down to a predictable schedule!

Had the 21-day long period from Feb 20 through March 12.

Then nothing from March 13 - April 2, so I called the pharmacy, who told me that I was supposed to start taking the pg again twelve days after cycle day 29 whether my period ever came or not (oops!)

So started taking the pg again on April 2, and re-named it "cycle day 12" from what I had been calling it ("cycle day 42"). The pharmacist said that I shoudl call her if I get a period, because normally htey have you take 3 days off if you get a period and are on an every-day pg regimen (which she's trying to switch me to with the help of my gyn who still has to sign the new prescription).

So of course today my period finally starts on cycle day 17... (or 47 depending on how you want to look at it).... (except now I guess it's cycle day 1??) so now I will have to call her and see what she wants me to do.

So confusing!

edited to add: the hot flashes at night are becoming much more annoying, soaking through my shirt and under my hair in the back - never had issues til the pg. I can tolerate it of course, but I'm wondering whether progesterone is really what I need. There are so many hormones in the female body.. why do they only prescribe progesterone? Isn't it best to rx BHRT equally to avoid problems? Shouldn't I be taking estrogen too, or something? I've had no tests to suggest I have issues with estrogen dominance, and everything suggests I need testosterone but I don't know how to get it. DHEA surely isn't the answer because it gives cystic acne that scars and hurts, and makes me a total witch.... \*sigh\*.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T14:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160516>

Hm saw a post in Ask Jack where Jack commented that an MRI does not cost $8K. This leads me to ask, does it cost $5K? Because I've been told that's ballpark and without medical need I have to pay out of pocket.

The reason?

I need a mammogram and can't get one with silicone implants. Saline, sure, if htey burst it just absorbs and I get my plastic surgeon to replace it. Silicone, not so much. According to my plastic surgeon back in 2010 when I got these put in, conventional allopathic rules for plastic surgery of this kind states that MRIs every five years instead of mammogram is standard protocol in my situation BUT it's not covered by insurance because it's only preventative AND only needed due to an elective surgery situation, the way it's been explained to me.

So of course I just don't do anything. But that little comment piqued my interest. If i could get it done for under a grand I would do it every five years happily. I wonder what it really costs?

### fitness@home — 2015-04-07T15:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160522>

What about thermography? Is that an option? Not sure with the silicone.

I do thermography now to monitor breast health, instead of mammograms. Much less expensive than $5k! Did a full body scan last year and it was maybe $400 (and FSA covered it). The breast thermogram is only $200-250.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T15:10:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160523>

Hmm I never heard of that - I bet I could ask my compounding pharmacist about it, and if she has no clue then I'll ask my gyn. Thanks for the suggestion! My gyn says that women relied on manual self-exams for a lot longer than we've had mammograms and be sure to at least be doing THAT, because my implants are under the muscle so what little breast tissue I have of my own should be very easy to evaluate, since it's pushed out by the silicone and muscle. I dont' do it though, I let her do it every couple of years instead. Not sure why. I can't be the only woman who in all honesty doesn't feel herself up every month, lol.

edited to add: My entire body (legs and arms aside) was CT scanned around November, 2014 and reviewed by my oncologist, so for now I feel certain that I'm okay without the mammo, but am definitely interested in finding an alternative so this is great to know about!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T16:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160532>

Did you ever feel like not only does nobody understand you, but everyone always takes you wrong?

The only logical conclusion is that the fault lies in the delivery.

But delivery is often a big part of what makes a person who they are.

Is it more important to have people like you, or have people hear you? Will they hear you if they don't like you? Should you care? DO you care?

When I was in college for my first degree in webmastering, it was commonplace for people to brag about their cool websites and to do everything possible to hide the source code so that they could feel superior and guard their hard work from theft by others. The guy sitting next to me said that he was a huge fan of Linux, the entire open-source movement really, and that he couldn't understand hoarding knowledge just to make one feel superior to others, when the whole point of education is the SHARING of knowledge.

Thoughts to ponder. (edit - but maybe not for reasons you think. I was a hoarder. I became ashamed of that fact. Then later regretted it when many things that I could've capitalized on financially were implemented by other people instead of me, like the guy who invented facebook for example. Or the invention of cross-platform messaging back when I first submitted the suggestion to AOL, Prodigy, and Compuserve all the same day that they come up with a messenger similar to AOL's instant messenger, but which would be able to connect to ANYONE on the internet, not just AOL customers....or thte suggestion to ancestry.com years ago that they start a show called "who do you think you are?" and feature famous people doing their genealogy... yes, my ideas... and perhaps other people had the same ones... I will never know because I put it all out there freely..... should I have? I don't know.... again, just thoughts flowing in the breeze.... Should I care if people like me? Probably not. Do I? very much so... not that it matters... because they never do.... hm.)

### NeilBB — 2015-04-07T16:57:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160539>

May not be your delivery. Most will respond badly to anything that they sense might threaten their ingrained paradigm. Should you care? You have to decide that for yourself.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T17:02:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160542>

I care because we are all connected. If I don't care about you, I don't care about me....that is my default, ingrained belief.... but it's difficult to change one's default beliefs as you said.... but SHOULD I change it and just think about me.... ? I don't think I should actually..... just thinking out loud...

### NeilBB — 2015-04-07T17:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160543>

We may all be connected. But what does a surgeon do when a body gets sick enough, Hope? He amputates the dead limb. He resects the cancerous tumor. He _disconnects_ the dead parts to save the living ones.

You have to start with yourself. You have to chose your battles. You cannot help others until you help yourself.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T17:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160544>

My compounding pharmacist, Paula, called. My progesterone and iodine prescriptions are ready to pick up. I will continue with the same dose (50mg daily split into two 25mg doses) on days 12-26 as I am already taking on those days, and in addition to that I will take 25 mg daily on days 4-11 (split into two 12.5mg doses). Days 1, 2, and 3 I am to take off completely. I guess they expect me to have 26-day cycles since she doesn't address days 27 or 28 at all. She said that if this spotting turns to a full bleed, then call it CD1, stop taking the pg for 3 days, and start taking them again on day 4. I think I've got it down.

She also said they go 3-4 full cycles on pg before upping the dose so it's way too early to consider changing my dose right now. Fair enough I guess.

I asked about the hot flashes on pg and she said that's not the progesterone, it's an adrenal issue. She said that I don't have to stop the pg and get my adrenals in order first, because pg isn't going to affect my adrenals or vice versa. She said that I need to be practicing mindfulness and de-stressing with meditation or otherwise. I acknowledged my high stress levels and she indicated her belief that most if not all of my problems come back to stress and not dealing with it healthily, saying eventually what will happen is I will not be able to produce cortisol at all and it will all cascade into a bunch of health issues. I said I presume that's why I now have hashi's and had the joy of getting an oncologist and she said that, and the bleeding for 3 weeks then NOT for a month, etc, yeah - it's stress.

Interesting take on it. Also mentioned their adrenal fatigue protocol and supplements again, but I don't understand how my whole problem is stress yet they can fix it with supplements. So I won't buy the supplements.

They also said my iodine is ready, I apply like 2 drops on my thyroid area of my neck once a day for a few days/weeks and then after that it's any part of my body, she thinks, but will check with the main pharmacist guy and find out before I get there to pick it up.

I asked about hormone testing and she said I definitely dont' need estrogen to be supplemented because I still bleed, regardless of how often that means I don't need any. But for testing purposes, I should test pg between CD19-21 if I decide to do it at some point. For testosterone, she said it doesn't matter when in my cycle I do it but she said testosterone and DHEA arena't as important as progesterone so that's why we focus on pg.

Just relaying it all for feedback purposes. I trust you each equally right now (the pharmacist and you fine people here).

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T17:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160545>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=160543)
>
> We may all be connected. But what does a surgeon do when a body gets sick enough, Hope? He amputates the dead limb. He resects the cancerous tumor. He _disconnects_ the dead parts to save the living ones.
>
> You have to start with yourself. You have to chose your battles. You cannot help others until you help yourself.

It's also a matter of how far the caring goes, I think. It is easy to try to address people with kindness and respect, so that level of caring doesn't deplete me. It is harder to care so much about someone, like your child with a drug problem who you can't get clean because it's his own life and choice but it freaking KILLS YOU, and not have it affect you...

### fitness@home — 2015-04-07T18:01:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160550>

> It is harder to care so much about someone, like your child with a drug problem who you can't get clean because it's his own life and choice but it freaking KILLS YOU, and not have it affect you...

Can relate.

One year ago exactly, my stepson was in rehab for a variety of drugs. Went into a halfway house after completing it and got booted from there within two weeks for his mouth.

We cannot take him back in...too stressful. Having him back in Houston with his "friends" is also a recipe for disaster. He is now living in NW Ohio and made it one full year being clean. Still has a long way to go to become an adult, even at age 26. Not on our medical insurance any longer.

It was very hard watching him self-destruct. He destroyed his teeth using meth - - they honestly need to be pulled and replaced with dentures (they are that bad). I am happy he went into rehab when he did and pray he never falls back into that quicksand.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T22:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160574>

(not let them back in to continue wreaking havoc)... he is our oldest and our youngest was in elementary school still... he couldn't be living here and things only got worse when he was elsewhere... but for now he's staying put and been clean for quite some months.. since about thanksgiving I guess.... he's supposed to be waiting to be transferred to rehab but the longer they take the more likely he'll just get time served and be put right back into the same place/people/situations that he was in before... I really keep praying he gets into that rehab and it does some good... and pray your stepson continues with his living clean and independently as well!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-07T22:39:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160576>

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-08T13:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160642>

OMG that iodine sucked. Wasted $25 on that little bit of dirtywater.

Last night I did not take progesterone since my period started yesterday, yet I still had hot flashes overnight, so I'm sure Paula was right and it's my adrenals not the pg. And not just hot flashes... I soaked through three shirts and had to get up and shower at 5:30am straight away because I was so wet under my hair and on the back of my neck.

That is no good for my adrenals, no good at all. The oncologist already explained to me the deal behind the sweating that happens at night with lymphoma patients and it all comes down to the liver not being able to clear toxins fast enough, basically... I have to wonder if that's what's happening to me now with this iodine.

I just don't get it. Topical iodine for hashi's is supposed to be one of those "JK says it but nobdy else does so it's radical and new" kinds of things, except even JK doesn't support it in reality as stated above... at least not for me, but I don't see why I'd be any different from any other woman with Hashi's since we're ALL unique and intriguing, right?

It's like the bread crumbs I'm picking up are all just carbs in the end and I'm supposed to avoid 'em like the plague accordingly, ha. .... ha.

This is where the circle of bewilderment, frustration, and giving up comes full circle yet AGAIN. Don't take supplements. Don't use the iodine for your thyroid. And don't use your computer to come here to read this advice cuz it has the weefee.. (want to crack up your kids, or make any computer store guy or cellphone store person try hard not to laugh at you? Call it that. "the weefee". DOES THIS PHONE HAVE THE WEE FEE? CAN"T STAND FER NO WEE FEE!)

### Lahelada — 2015-04-08T17:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160655>

what I mean is this : If you cannot leave then you need to separate your Self in situ with all the challenges around you. In other words you need to put your Self first. A simple thing to illustrate . You and your daughter watch TV at night. How about dimming the lights and having a seventies dance party for two. Allow yourself to be different from yesterday. The benefits. ? You get less blue light and your daughter a new memory of her childhood,an idea that life does not have to be routine. She does not wanna? That is where the tough comes in. The fun menu tpday is music. Only an example. Reinterpret your life within Your parameters and stretch them a little at a time.
Now I will go and do some of that on my own.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-09T14:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160732>

"For more than 20 years these Sierras have saved my life every day. \[snip\] I live in my tent for at least a quarter of every year and go stir crazy in a manmade environment very quickly.

Some say I am damaged, anti-social. I say they are wrong. I like a different society. Birds and deer and bears are well worth knowing, and trees and the smell of a meadow 10,000 feet above the sea after a rain. And the wildflowers in the spring and berries in the fall. These are my society. And they never litter, never double-park and always, always act as you expect them to act. If you give respect, you are welcome.

I have danced naked on top of peaks and swam naked in many rivers and lakes. I've taken 3 years and multiple attempts to climb a single peak. I've run for long distances over bare granite above 11,000 feet with rain drops the size of pennies falling to reach camp ahead of a storm. These are not your every day endeavors. But they're mine. And I am happy each and every day. How about you?"

\-a snippet from the book "Still Wandering Aimlessly" by one of my brothers, Richard "Yogi" Bair

[http://www.amazon.com/Still-Wandering-Aimlessly-Richard-Yogi/dp/160836996X](http://www.amazon.com/Still-Wandering-Aimlessly-Richard-Yogi/dp/160836996X)

(he left when I was about 5... another brother "found" him when I was about 30 via a reference to this book on facebook just after it was published... he's still living as a mountain man in the Sierra Nevada mountains.... You can google him and look for a couple of videos on youtube. I haven't seen or spoken to him personally in 25+ years, since he walked away and just... didn't come back.)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-09T15:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160736>

But yes he does have interesting tales to share... the book is worthwhile. It inspires me, despite the fact that I will never do as he did. And it definitely helps to illustrate that certain things run in the family. The whole reason he went there was to commit suicide. Which is why he said the mountains have saved his life every day for 20 years. I wrote to him once in 1993 (years after I last saw him, which was about 1979) to say I'd gotten married and had a baby and asked him if he'd ever had issues with depression (because I was suffering with post partum depression at the time). He replied and told me in a letter that he was going on a trip around the world with a girl he'd met. A year later a storage company called my mother to say he'd abandoned his unit, his truck, all of his belongings and they'd need to auction it off to recoup their losses. We never heard from him again. She died not knowing if he was alive or dead. Twenty years later I got the book and read that was when he took off intending to walk off a cliff and end his life - just after that letter. Anyway... moving on.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-10T14:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160806>

General update - still not using the iodine. Don't plan to. Didn't realize until the next day that after I spilled it on my hand I stopped speaking almost entirely for like 16 hours. I typed, nodded, maybe muttered a couple of words when I needed to. But just felt much more sedated and "inside of myself" than I have at all ever except for back when they were trying different antidepressants on me after my oldest was born in 93. Some of those made me the same way. Felt like I was living way deep down inside a big oversized mascot costume of myself, and didn't have the ability to speak without a ton of effort. So odd.

I got my progesterone refilled last Thursday, 8 days ago, and renamed it CD12 as instructed by Paula/pharmacist. Took it as directed thru Monday night then she called Tuesday morning and I mentioned my period was starting. She told me to stop the pg for 3 days, then restart on my new dose. I will be cycling it now, taking 12.5 mg in the morning plus 12.5 mg at night on days 4-11 (for a total of 25mg per day). Then on days 12-26 I take 25mg twice a day for a total of 50mg daily. Then stop and hopefully get a period, during which time I take 3 days off the pg on CD1,2,and 3. I guess I also don't take it on days 27 and 28.. or maybe they're expecting me to eventually just have 26 day cycles..? Not sure how that will work.

Anyway, today I took 12.5 this morning after not taking any on Tues, Wed, or Thurs.

Last night I had mor eof those nasty night sweats. Not sure what's causing it - could be the iodine still but now it's back to the level of sweating I was having while on the pg last time.. though they told me it's not the pg that's doing it, it's my adrenals. But the timing sure makes me question their assertion that pg doesn't affect adrenals. I have a SNP that says I don't process hormones as fast as I should in my liver b ut I think that would only mean synthetic ones. Not sure...

**CYP2C19 Intermediate Metabolizer** CYP2C19 Intermediate Metabolizer. Your body breaks down some medicines at a slightly slower than normal rate (which is represented by gs150). Individuals with gs152 genotypes have even slower metabolism.
\*anti-epileptics (such as diazepam, phenytoin, and phenobarbitone)
\*anti-depressants (such as amitriptyline and clomipramine)
\*anti-platelet drug clopidogrel (Plavix)
\*anti-ulcer proton pump inhibitors like omeprazole (trade names Losec and Prilosec), esomeprazole (trade name Nexium), and lansoprazole (Prevacid)
**\*hormones (estrogen, progesterone)**

**But then on top of that, I have a SNP compounding the issue,taking me from intermediate metabolizer to non-functioning completely as shown below:**

[rs4244285](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4244285)[(A;G)](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4244285%28A;G%29)
Magnitude: 3
Frequency: 19.0%
Repute:Bad
References:29

The risk allele is rs4244285(A).
**As a nonfunctioning CYP2C19,** this variant would be expected to be a poor metabolizer of several commonly prescribed drugs, including anti-ulcer drugs like omeprazole (trade names Losec and Prilosec), esomeprazole (trade name Nexium), and lansoprazole (trade name Prevacid). In Caucasians, SNPs in CYP2C19 are relatively rare (in contrast to SNPs in CYP2D6), but SNPs in this gene are common in Asians. Ulcer treatment with omeprazole to reduce Helicobacter pylori has been shown to vary depending on a patient's [CYP2C19](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/CYP2C19) genotype, varying from 28% in patients homozygous for [CYP2C19](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/CYP2C19) alleles encoding fully functional proteins to 100% in patients with variations leading to poor metabolism. The fact that poor metabolizers for many cytochrome p450s achieve higher therapeutic success for some drugs is speculated to be because for some of the drug being broken down (ie metabolized) slower, the effective concentrations are both higher and longer lasting. \[[PMID 9867757](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9867757?dopt=Abstract)\]

However, other drugs clearly work less well in carriers of reduced function CYP2C19 alleles. An example of such a drug is [clopidogrel](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Clopidogrel), sold under the brand name [Plavix](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Plavix). This has now (2010) been acknowledged by the FDA, who have added a boxed warning to Plavix, alerting patients and health care professionals that the drug can be less effective in people who have CYP2C19 variants and cannot convert the drug as effectively to its active form.[\[1\]](http://www.fda.gov/NewsEvents/Newsroom/PressAnnouncements/ucm204253.htma)
(more info here - [http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Rs4244285](http://www.snpedia.com/index.php/Rs4244285) )

>
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fluoride-quinolone-fluoroquinolone-such-as-cipro….9756/#post-110469](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fluoride-quinolone-fluoroquinolone-such-as-cipro%E2%80%A6.9756/#post-110469)

well but hte severe psychiatric effect would be disasterous. this child is teetering on the edge of schizoaffective disorder at best IMO as it is. He's very very sensitive and his gut has been absolutely destroyed as long as he's been alive. At 13 days old we found out his gut was bad and it's never come back. (He wasn't gaining weight on formula and even the lactofree wasn't helping so I switched to breastfeeding at that point and things picked up but were never the same as with the others who breastfed from day one.)

and yeah his dad has never been the same since. he's a completely different person and his gut has never come back either.

but what choice do i really have, you know?

alright. switched out my non-organic, non-grassfed breakfast steaks for organic GF groudn beef. ### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-12T16:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-160971>

Still having hot flashes in the night. Waking up to change my shirt at least once a night and sometimes have to shower the second I wake. Not sure if it's because of hte progesterone or if I would've coincidentally gone into perimenopause right now anyway, but either way I think that's where I'm at. Today is CD 6, so I'm still taking 12.5 mg of pg in the morning and another 12.5 mg of pg at night. It seems to be taking the edge off my bitchiness a bit. I'm also still eating this nasty grassfed organic groudn beef in the morning and I can only get down about a third as much of it as a steak. I don't like it so I won't be sticking with it, even though it's cheaper than the non-gf, non-organic steaks. The depression is still here but I've dealt with this since I was about 16 so I guess any day I'm not suicidal is an improvement over all the days I have been, so I don't factor it into things. I've been really cold in my hands and feet again the past few days, which I wasn't before. I think when I take the larger dose of pg I don't get the cold hands and feet, but on the lower dose, or when I'm not on any, it's right back again. I don't know why.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-15T14:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-161271>

Like, is endo just for thyroid or do I bring up all the hormone issues with her? I'm at a complete loss here. I just know that after I had my second or third child I was put on thyroid meds and told I'd be on it for the rest of my life but I was also put on antidepressants and I thought maybe I could just start to wean off th meds after awhile so I did, but then I went downhill and never added the thyroid stuff back in because I completely forgot about it until January 2015. So do I just be like, hey, I was on meds before and they worked so gimme, or do I prep for a fight about TSH? I'm out of the loop on this, been concentrating on a couple of completely different things lately, so now I really have no clue what this appointment is even about.

I guess for fun I could bring up iodine treatment though, just to see if I can make her head spin around like a posessed person.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-15T15:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/post-161275>

Nude tanning: I can do it but only at an exact time otherwise I use a bed sheet to screen off an area. As far as kids go,just tell them tbat unless they leave you alone for 45 mins they will have a direct visual of where they came from and that you will have the extended sex talk with them. Basically gross them out.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-15T20:32:13+0300
