### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-08T18:48:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152125>

Wow!
Redox rx and CT 6.

Read them both and had lightbulb's coming on all over the place!Now I see just HOW important CT is for people with AI diseases(certainly helps with the motivation)and water,(there is a spring in pretty much every village here so just find the one that you like the )been guzzling it all week now,freezing cold.

Started with stage 2 of CT today after 16 days of face dunking(I think I will keep up the face dunking too as it is still quite challenging and I have found the process very mindful and use it like a meditation session,10 to 15 minutes twice a day,great for waking up and winding down and as a preparation for my CT session's.
your quite right Caroline just get those ice bags on the chest and get on with it!!It wasn't as intense as the face dunking just a gradual cooling feeling,did about 20mins I think,more tonight.

Been eating a load of fish,the closest I can get to oysters right now is mussels but making up with plenty of tinned sardines.I have found a strong appetite all week,starting with protein and then bacon and egg for breakfast,lunch of seasonal fruit(apples and tangerines) and sardines and tuna or mussels(a weird combination but it is really fresh and tasty.Then a dinner of fish fried in butter and some steamed veg with butter and black pepper on top.

It is quite cold in my house 12 deg c and hovering around zero outside so perhaps that is fueling my appetite but that kind of goes against what Dr kruse say's,perhaps part of the adjustment process.

all lights are off and electrical screens an hour after sundown and just sat by firelight listening to audiobooks and really enjoying it!!

Sleep is still patchy(but its early days)get off well and then wake between 2 and 5 normally for a pee(all that water!!)then struggle to get off again,so on average getting 7 hours I reckon.

Just got to build up the routine now and be good,I'm really enjoying it,its all new and a different perspective on life.

Best wishes to you all and thank you for all the advise and pointers

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-11T19:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152418>

Perhaps I overdid it a bit with the cold this week,been out walking a lot with ambient temps around zero mostly without a tee shirt and really enjoyed it but I have ended up with painful chill blains on hands and feet!I wondered what it was at first,thought I had been bitten by something!So lesson learned,keep extremities warm and protected.
Ct is getting easier although still only at second stage of the protocol,I can quite happily do an hour with ice on the chest and no marks or problems with skin so will do it without comp shirt tomorrow,still face dunking,doing a regular 15 mins twice a day now(breathing in between!!) and really enjoy it,I used to suffer with a stuffed up nose but this is a great cure for it.
Bought an oil lamp yesterday and just investigating options for a CT tank.

Great to read other members stories and journals,its very motivating.

Looking forward to the new week,more study and a better understanding of all the concepts.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-12T19:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152565>

Another great day,15 min face dunk and an hour with ice on the torso after a huge breakfast(two pieces of steak,3 eggs and a tin of sardines).I did yesterday and today on just the two meals,but find I need a really big breakfast to survive but it does work well as this way I am ready for an early dinner and gives me a good four to five hours before bed.
Another face dunk tonight,great to see Kristi doing her stuff,helps me a lot to know we are all "embracing the cold" together,so to speak.

I am nodding off in the chair at night about 10pm and getting straight into bed and sleeping deeply till I awake to go to the loo,anytime between 3 and 5 am and then struggle to go back to sleep,but I have noticed remembering my dreams more in the last week.

My thirst has been improving and I can now drink a litre or more of ice cold spring water a day,this is a drastic change,before I found Jack's website I perhaps drank a couple of glasses along with plenty of coffee.

Just going dark here so time to turn all the tech off for the day!

Wish everyone well

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-13T19:46:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152664>

Yes,loving the fat!!!big slabs of butter in everything,its sooo nice.
Coconut fat is a bit tougher here as it is very expensive(need to see if I can find a source cheaper somewhere).Looking to find some good lard or suet too but its hard to find out what the animals have been eating,there is very little "food awareness"here,you see a few past their best bits of organic veg in the supermarkets,but no grass fed anything!!
Eating a fair bit of 90% cocoa chocolate as I can get that here reasonably and I do buy whole coconuts and eat them whenever I see them.
I was all olive oil before,but not touched it since finding you guys!

Thank you kristi!Keep motivating me and I'l do the same for you.

Found a blow up paddling pool,a biggish one that I can fit in the bathroom and just about lie down in it for CT so another week or so and I should be in there.

Slept poorly last night,woke at 2am and struggled to sleep much after that and it really affects me when I don't get good sleep.Its been a tough day today,exhausted all day,Been good though,face dunk this morning and another one tonight and some ice packing.I've realised its better for me at night as I lose an hour in the day otherwise when its light,night is ideal as I am in pretty much blackout conditions so I can do it and listen to a podcast or audiobook.

Just making some beef broth,well had the bones in the slow cooker all day,don't know what else to put in it?Looked on the site for other people's recipe's but didn't come up with anything really,any of you got a good recipe up your sleeves?

Scrambled eggs with prawns tomorrow and loads of butter for a change so looking forward to that.

Best wishes to you all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-15T15:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152853>

Kristi thanks for the recipe,looks great!won't throw the veg away though,seems a waste,sure they'l be lovely drained and lightly fried in some butter!!

Thank you Caroline,looked into iherb,its not really cheap the delivery but its an option if there is nothing else.I need to look a bit deeper here to see if anyone is supplying at a good price.

Had a busy day yesterday,didn't get back in time for a journal entry.Feel much better today,slept well till 5am and then dozed till sun up.

I was thinking the other day about what Dr kruse had said about what he thinks causes IBM and other AI associated diseases and look at how my own situation fits in with it.
I was for 7 years in the UK a firefighter and for the last 3 years I worked for the military on a busy military base covering helicopter hot refuels and base Emergency medical technician cover.This was when I first became ill(I think)I caught a cold which turned to bronchitis or so it seemed but my chest was always clear,just to go and answer the front door left me breathless and exhausted and I was like that for six months to a year,the doctors were testing for a lung problem as it looked like that was the cause but everything came back clear and I eventually recovered and returned to work.

The fire station was a mass of EMF and of course we slept there two nights in every eight and the base was home to the signallers regiment!!!Imagine all the EMF they created on top of the usual comms traffic of a military base.

For the last 4 years I have lived in a 3rd floor apartment directly above a supermarket and the freezers(you could here them at night,they sounded like a bus ticking over!)apart from the wifi signals and something else which to this day the residents of the building have never discovered.Something was affecting our car key fobs and the motorised gate in the parking lot,many times it would not open and to open your car doors you had to be right at the door,when normally they would open a good 3 metres away.My ex parked her car next to the supermarket one day and when she went back to it it refused to start,we called the mechanic and he reset the immobiliser and it started,he said it happened a lot and was caused by strong electrical fields!!

I am very motivated by all I have and am learning here and realise that if I can cure myself it may well help many sufferers of myositis if put on the regimen early on before to much muscle wastage takes place.So this really helps on those days you are tired or just don't feel like it!

Forgot to buy the ice yesterday(shopping can wear me out a lot),didn't realise till I was on the way home but then I remembered the frozen brussel sprouts and veg I had bought so that was what I packed on my chest!Adapt and overcome!!I can do an hour with the ice directly on my skin no problems.I will probably do this for another week along with the face dunking(twice daily)and always drink ice water prior.Slowly i am noticing that I feel the cold less and today it is quite warm outside(7 deg c or 44f)and I am shedding clothes in the house which is at about 15 c.

Blue blockers are on the way and will be used for emergencies only!as I really enjoy the dark nights,oh almost forgot,got the oil lamp working last night,it was lovely,only cost me six euros too!

Best wishes to all

### Graeme — 2015-01-15T16:34:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-152866>

Hello Jeff,

I've been in Madrid for 15+ years. So I have some experience of sourcing decent foods here in Spain. Here's my input:

Coconut Oil has recently become available in Herbolarios and even in El Corte Ingles. It is a bit pricey. Dr Georg seems to be the only brand around. It is good quality. They also sell Coconut Butter (aka Manna). Ask at your local Herbolario to order you some. Price about €25/litre

Beef Dripping, Suet, Lard is impossible to source here. I bring mine in from the UK.

Butter is expensive here and the cows are rarely grass-fed. However, there is a Basque company called Xanceda which sells Unpasteurized Butter, small scale from their own cows. Good stuff.
Ghee is available, but super pricey and doubtful quality. Better to make your own.
I can find raw milk, but raw heavy cream is impossible. There are some decent Spanish cheeses made from raw milk, especially goat and sheep milk, but you need to read the small print.
The concept of A2 dairy is unheard of so far in Spain.

Duck/Goose Fat is sometimes seen in upmarket stores and always imported from France. Again, I usually get mine from the UK.

Palm Oil can be found in the Indian/African stores, but I have not tried it.

Generally, apart from the quality Spanish Jamon, all meat in Spain is factory farmed and poor quality. Spain is the largest grower of GM crops for animal feed in Europe. So we can imagine that the factory farms will use the cheaper GM Soy & Corn feeds. Therefore meat, especially regular pork and all its derivatives are to be avoided. I bring all my pork and bacon from the UK, where we still have some small scale free-range, outdoor pig farmers - This does not exist in Spain. Some beef from Galicia, Asturias etc is mainly grass-fed, but often "finished" with grain feed. Not perfect, but I do eat it and use the bones for broth.
Lamb is only really available as suckling lamb, but the mother will be on grain feed not grass. Same goes for goat and suckling pig.
Chicken is all factory. Any decent poultry is almost always imported from France. I don't really eat it at all.

Brussel sprouts are not popular here (Spaniards do not eat real greens at all) and can be pricey. Lidl or Aldi do sell them cheaper though.

Seaweed (dried) is available, usually in Herbolarios and is domestically produced in Galicia.

Oysters can be found as cheaply as €1 each, but you need to buy direct from the supplier also in Galicia.

Brazil nuts (Coquitos) also tough to find, but El Corte Ingles sometimes carries them.
Macedamias also tricky and often fried. Eroski stocks them raw.

My two top picks from Spain are Boquerones (fresh anchovies) which are usually €5/kg from the fish market - I marinate the fillets in vinegar and use the heads in a MHS (thanks Inger) mixed with Gazpacho to smooth the taste - and Sardines, which I have found in glass jars (Ortiz brand) to avoid the plastic lined, hormone disrupting tins, which are cheap, convenient and delicious.

Finally, wine, plenty of good stuff in Spain obviously, but fluoride based pesticides are heavily used. Worth paying the extra couple of euros for organic, which is increasingly available.

Hope this helps.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-15T17:07:15+0200

Graeme thanks for all that info!helps a great deal,a trip to blighty is in order by the sounds of it!
I am lucky here in El bierzo that there is a growing movement of organic veg farmers so at least I can pick that up and my local butcher kills and sources good grass fed beef although as you well know it is not hung and for me lacks in flavour.I concentrate mainly on tinned sardines and a bit of beef and I have to say the odd fresh chorizo,those are available cured from game,javali(wild boar) and deer so worth looking out for.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-18T13:48:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-153242>

I had two great nights sleep one after the other,It was brilliant,more energised on rising.
Yesterday was interesting,I went for lunch with some friends,they live on the 9th floor looking across a beautiful park(it was most odd to be looking down on the trees and birds flying about!).There is an antenna about a mile away I would say,so I wasn't too happy about it all but had a great time never the less.
When I got back to my silent house my tinnitus was blaring away and its the first time i've noticed it in a while so loud,a result of the lunch situation??
I got in 3 15min face dunk sessions refreshing the water half way through to make sure it was really cold and 60mins of ice prior to going to bed(I think this is helping me sleep as I go to bed with my torso freezing and its maybe 12deg c in the room,drops to 7 by morning depending on outside ambient temp).I couldn't face any cold showering though and felt bad to be stood under the hot water and not having the will power to put it on cold!My excuse is the tapwater this time of year is maybe 5deg c so its quite brutal.I am hoping the CT bath will be better.I plan to slowly drop the water temp as i get used to it as i doubt I will be able to stand 10deg c water straight away.I have always found cold water very painful!I am thin and never had any real fat to insulate me and was always the first out of the pool while my slightly chubbier friends could stand it a lot longer.

A quick word about my father(something that occured to me the other day).He is 78 and in perfect health,no medication or medical conditions,good body composition and still has the stamina to drive over to Spain in 3 days from the UK!!!!
He has always for lunch eaten sardine or pilchards or cured herrings(he used to put them on my sandwiches for school and work,soggy fishy sandwiches!)he eats lots of watercress(it was a staple for him during the second world war!watercress sandwiches).Both he and my mother are good traditional meat and two veg cooks and have never seen a packet meal.My mother is also in good health,just starting with osteoperosis so I am trying to educate her bit by bit,but otherwise fit and healthy.
Just an interesting observation of the benefits of DHA and a moderate diet,a point to note is that neither of them apart from a good walk have ever been sports people.My father has periodically weight trained but nothing obsessive,in fact at 76 years old he thought he was suffering from angina,it turned out he had ripped his pectoral muscle from the bone,showing off in the gym to the "Kids".

I hope to get organised and start the third phase of CT next week.

Best wishes to all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-20T11:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-153414>

Managed to pick up a brand new bath yesterday for twenty euro from a reclamation yard!!So scrapped the swimming pool idea.I can sit the tub by my window and look out at the pine covered mountains(all white and snowy today again,still not got any red speedos Caroline,so no snow angels!)while doing CT.So just got to set up a bit of plumbing and its game on!!Quite nervous,the great unknown....but excited too as Its what I really need!
Doing well with most of the other fundamentals now,two meals a day is great,saves so much time!And sitting in the dark at night just feels natural now,either sitting staring at the fire or listening to an audiobook or podcast.Sleep comes easier,still wake up in the night but its getting better and feeling more rested when I awake in the mornings now.

I have rigged up a grounding cable and put it down my sock whenever i'm sat down or stood at the computer,but i've read somewhere that this may be a bad idea and actually acts as a antenna for EMF.Maybe I should put this question in another part of the forum?Any advise as to what we should be doing would be appreciated.

Best wishes

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-26T12:50:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-153882>

Scrapped the earthing rod idea,thanks for the advise Caroline and nicld.Much better to ground on the earth outside so shoes off when sun is out for a few minutes.
Loads of progress last week,my blue blockers and a pool thermometer arrived last week,I was so excited,made me laugh!
Got my ct bathtub up and running now after having a leak first time round!Did first ct session friday evening and the water comes out of the well at exactly 10 deg c (50f) I wasn't sure on how much water(didn't want to flood my room) so it ended up with my legs outstreched and immersed up to my belly buttonI did 20 odd minutes at a guess and it was tough going.
I did my second session last night and the water had warmed up to 12dec c and I filled up the bath so that i could lie down and be immersed up to my neck with just my knees unexposed and it was totally different,I felt calmer and wasn't fighting the cold so much,i set an alarm for 30 minutes and after about 20 started shivering but concentrated on my breathing and got through to the alarm and then 10minutes more!Woohoo!I was cold for a good half hour or so after but my feet and hands were warm and I fell asleep in the chair for a couple of minutes,so dashed off to bed and slept well.

It is great to be proactive and I look forward to the dark evenings and hearty breakfasts,surviving on two meals a day but need a big breakfast to see me through.
Will incorporate seaweeds as soon as I find a supplier who delivers to Spain(any ideas folks?).Going through all the podcasts a second time and pulling out new information and a better understanding.

Best wishes to all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-28T12:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154088>

Getting the hang of CT now(Thanks Kristi for your feedback)The well water is at 9 to 10 deg c so perfect and the last two evenings I've got the level sorted out and marked it on the bath so it is right up to my chin with just my knees uncovered,I read or heard Dr Kruse mentioning that it is best to have the femoral and carotid triangles covered for maximum effect and what Kristi said about the water being more comfortable at 10 deg seems to work as I guess you go a bit numb and its like a prickly sensation rather than cold.I am surprised that it does not hurt as I have always found cold water painful.
I have done 40 minutes the last two evenings,the last ten are tough but will get better I guess.It takes me an hour to rewarm after but I am considering just going to bed cold If I can cope with it to get a better effect,early days though so bit by bit i'l eat the elephant!Or bite by bite...

Focusing more on the ketogenic aspect now and iodine now I have the CT and blue light/circadian cycles set up and good water(leptin reset I perhaps should say).Going to set up a corner of the garden to catch the sunshine and do some grounding too and have even considered getting a couple of chickens for my own pastured eggs and they can do the weeding!!

There are some trees with buds on here and the migratory storks have arrived and a slight change in the air here,just a hint of spring I like to think.The native would say rubbish but compared to a British winter it is already like spring here.Helps to be positive about it all I think.

Best wishes to all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-29T19:40:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154213>

35 minutes of CT yesterday water temp not optimal(12deg c)forgot to buy some ice!
I have been exhausted every day this week,constantly fighting the urge to sit down,that strange ache in my quadriceps(Which seem to be the worst affected muscle group)so I'm a touch fed up!
Just going to face dunk today and see if I feel any better tomorrow.
I will also change my CT time to earlier in the day and see if that helps.Don't really understand what drives these "bad spells".Not sleeping through the night often so that must affect me.
Tomorrow is another day,all we have to do is keep fighting and never give up and we can't lose!!

Best wishes to all of you and thank you for all the support,it all helps

### caroline — 2015-01-31T10:46:37+0200

### Spanishjeff — 2015-01-31T11:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154348>

Hi Caroline,

yes you are right of course!I start the day with bacon and eggs or a variation round that theme sometimes a load of sardines and also a coffee with plenty of butter and usually whey protien,so breakfast is huge and fatty and protieny.(drink the coffee soon as I'm up and within a half hour of waking,just so I have consumed something as per the rules of leptin reset whilst breakfast is cooking).
No lunch

Dinner some vegetables(onion,garlic.cabbage,or cauliflower and brocolli,or whatever is local and in season)and either meat or fish and sometimes mussels.
I eat this at least 4 hours before going to bed.So I feel overall I am doing what I should and at the right times,oh I tend to eat a bit of 90%lindt chocolate after every meal too!

The only thing I can think is that I perhaps have caught a slight cold or virus and that is what makes me feel so tired,although I haven't really had a "proper"cold for a year or so.

Perhaps I was expecting to much initially from CT maybe I need another month or so to properly cold adapt.

I slept poorly again last night,I drop off ok about 10 to 10.30 pm and then wake up at 2am ish to go to the toilet and then struggle to get back to sleep,last night I was awake for a few hours!!!I do feel overall that I don't wake up as tired as before I started with JackKruse.com so that is something positive.

I did CT yesterday in the afternoon about 5pm and found it a touch easier to get into the water at least(It was 9deg c,fresh out of the well)and I managed 35 mins.I was shivering quite badly at the end and really had had enough!So I will do every day if I feel like it and if not every other day come what may and see how I feel.

One of my closest friends comes out with many good quotes and this one sums it up well"Tiredness makes cowards of us all!"

So be strong and fight on.Anything you can see I am missing or doing wrong food wise Caroline?

Best wishes

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-31T13:54:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154357>

> [Spanishjeff said:](/goto/post?id=154348)
>
> I did CT yesterday in the afternoon about 5pm and found it a touch easier to get into the water at least(It was 9deg c,fresh out of the well)and I managed 35 mins.I was shivering quite badly at the end and really had had enough!So I will do every day if I feel like it and if not every other day come what may and see how I feel.

Hi Jeff,

I've found my tolerance of CT varies ..... My own n=1 is that there is a sweet spot of when it seems that my muscles are just starting to vibrate, a sort of pre-shiver state .... It could be in 5 minutes or 35 minutes, 0.2C or 10C, its however long it takes for my muscles to change signal .....

I can tolerate colder temperatures in the morning, particularly if I've just eaten a BAB first. The bulletproof/optimal coffees do nothing for me, and my n=1 is that eating seafood isn't great for me before CT. I've learned what makes the best "CT fuel" for me to tolerate the lower temperatures. Yesterday I did CT without breakfast, then didn't have time to eat before going to work, worked in a cold environment and by lunchtime I felt yuk, I wasn't shivering but I hadn't warmed up properly, my muscles ached, and I was tired. With the right fuel, at the right time, the experience would have been different, a challenge instead of a stress. I think I do better with daily challenge rather than alternate day stress IYKWIM.

I find CT later in the day is much tougher, usually because its been too long since my last meal, (and because I'm not getting the same photons as in the morning because of where the bath is positioned). I don't think I could do 9C for 35 minutes at 5 p.m. if I hadn't eaten since the BAB, my metabolism isn't there yet ...... 9C for 35 minutes in the morning straight after a BAB - a different story.

Blessings, Jeff!

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-01T12:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154434>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=154357)
>
> Hi Jeff,
>
> I've found my tolerance of CT varies ..... My own n=1 is that there is a sweet spot of when it seems that my muscles are just starting to vibrate, a sort of pre-shiver state .... It could be in 5 minutes or 35 minutes, 0.2C or 10C, its however long it takes for my muscles to change signal .....
>
> I can tolerate colder temperatures in the morning, particularly if I've just eaten a BAB first. The bulletproof/optimal coffees do nothing for me, and my n=1 is that eating seafood isn't great for me before CT. I've learned what makes the best "CT fuel" for me to tolerate the lower temperatures. Yesterday I did CT without breakfast, then didn't have time to eat before going to work, worked in a cold environment and by lunchtime I felt yuk, I wasn't shivering but I hadn't warmed up properly, my muscles ached, and I was tired. With the right fuel, at the right time, the experience would have been different, a challenge instead of a stress. I think I do better with daily challenge rather than alternate day stress IYKWIM.
>
> I find CT later in the day is much tougher, usually because its been too long since my last meal, (and because I'm not getting the same photons as in the morning because of where the bath is positioned). I don't think I could do 9C for 35 minutes at 5 p.m. if I hadn't eaten since the BAB, my metabolism isn't there yet ...... 9C for 35 minutes in the morning straight after a BAB - a different story.

Thank you Sue,that is some stellar information!!Really insightful,I will experiment more with CT times and foods,I struggle with water in the mornings and would drown if I had a shower first thing!!But perhaps ct is different.I guess it has to fit in near to a meal which for me is either breakfast or dinner.Great info thanks again,time to experiment more

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-01T12:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154436>

Yesterday was a real rollercoaster day!
Did the usual domestic rubbish,shopping etc,then went off in search of spring water.It has been raining here for 3 days straight so my regular spring was a bit dirty.I took a drive into the hills and it was stunning,all the lichen glowed and the mosses on the boulders,the rivers were wild and the sky full of snow and sun and rain all at once.
I spent an hour cutting firewood and felt good to be outside.On returning to the house still feeling energised I decided to do a workout(I'd not done anything for 2 weeks apart from cutting firewood).I listened to a few "motivational videos/montages on you tube and ended up shedding a few tears whilst deadlifting(definitely a first!),tears of frustration,determination and sheer emotion and had the best workout ever,I am stronger than ever and more intense,quite perplexing as I've felt so weak lately.Still feeling motivated I ate two tins of sardines and got myself in the bathtub for some CT,the water warms up after its been in the bath for a day so it was 13deg c not ideal but I compensated my immersing myself right up to my chin and my arms up to my gloves(never done that before)whilst watching "The electric universe"(thunderbolt project,you tube)which helped distract me and I managed 30 mins by which time I was shivering big time and my back muscles were just starting to clench up uncontrollably and i'd had enough.I did it without a fire lit too for the first time so the ambient temp was 12 deg.
All in all an emotional day which left me shattered but feeling back in control and on the right path.

Sleep is still disturbed,hopefully another month and that will change.

It is wonderful to be part of this community and on every page I see people with astounding stories and situations that they overcome with grit and determination.You are all helping me so much,I feel surrounded by like minded people full of help and knowledge!My life seems to be different every day,only 3 months ago I felt so low and overwhelmed.Thank you from the heart x

### nonchalant — 2015-02-02T16:44:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154533>

Hi Jeff, interesting journal. Sounds like you are progressing nicely.
I just love splitting firewood with an axe. Almost as much as I like watching it burn in the stove.
But I like to do it barefoot, and that freaks DH out.  Guess I could give up some of my grounding time for safety...

### caroline — 2015-02-02T23:13:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154557>

Sorry Jeff and Kristi .... I just saw this. Whey protein really bothers me and quite a few others here as well.
Are you having ice water before doing C/T? High fat meal?

Jeff - I am wondering if you are reacting to something you are eating? Re - exhaustion? Broccoli and cauliflower really bother me ....maybe just eat very plainly for a bit and see how you feel?

Do you have a crock pot? You could throw a roast pork in before going to bed and wake up to breakfast? Maybe you are reacting to eggs? I can't eat many of those either....

I had a really emotional day like you described..... I felt like an emptying of all the old crap and then felt like I was ready to be filled up with all this wonderful new stuff.....I became much more aware of things and people and my surroundings and just felt very peaceful. That peace and serenity has remained - I see everything with new eyes.....I am much more open to living ......really living, really laughing, really seeing people, really listening......

Life just kind of washes over me now - like a constant cascade of light showers....everything has meaning and definition.

The crap is still there ....but it is only background noise.

Have a brilliant day .....and as kristi said - question everything....you owe yourself that!

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-03T13:53:06+0200

Caroline,thank you!
I'd not thought anything about cauliflower or broccoli and I have been eating a lot of them as they are kind of in season here and available organically.I did wonder if it was a food issue and will try changing diet for a week,the whey is not ideal,nor are the eggs.I can't get any good pork here,its all factory rubbish!perhaps a week of prawns and fish for breakfast.
I do still feel exhausted but more positive mentally(Thanks to you guys).I had a headache for nearly two days just like a hangover,food related or mold from food perhaps.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-03T14:13:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154608>

Ct,30 minutes yesterday(water 9deg c ).Just doing every other day at the moment,as so tired all day.I find watching a video helps rather than just listening to something as I find ct very intense,after 20 mins I start quivering then at 30 I am shivering so much the water is making waves and my muscles start clenching up quite uncontrollably so I have to get out or at least sit up and try for 5 or 10 mins more(Trying to catch you up Kristi!).

I need to do some grounding every day as I currently don't connect at all!And some sunbathing(when its not raining).
The journal is good for accountability and focus and all the help,pointers and support.

I wake twice a night to pee,which is frustrating.Dr K drinks 32 oz of ice water before going to bed!How does he do it?Maybe I wake up because my sleep is still poor?I will buy a magneto when funds allow.

I am now putting the diet under the microscope and will change it and see what results.And I am going to start measuring myself(thanks fitness@home on Kristi's thread post 196)as I would like to know what is happening with my muscle mass,I am very lean and since starting the leptin reset and ct am virtually fat free.The illness I have affects muscle mass so it will be good to monitor it,I can see I am stronger as my deadlift weight is increasing every month,which is good news for me but I am concerned with my quadriceps as they always feel odd,kind of achy and slight inclines are like a wall to me although i can do them and even steep climbs up hills,so i'd like to see some growth there too even if just to feel better mentally!

I was speaking with my sister today and she is getting interested in all this and EMF,so I'm really pleased,she has turned her wifi off and her mobile and is asking all about food and diet!

Best wishes to you all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-04T12:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154702>

I've been deep in thought after what has happened recently and I am now questioning what I am doing with my diet,or at least looking at what I have been eating and I am constructing a simple diet for the next few weeks based on the epi paleo rx and information from the forum.My diet whilst it seemed to make sense in some areas is I can see now not optimal and as my redox potential is poor and inflammation must be a problem based on the understandings of the AI and the odd feeling in my thighs.
Before finding JK I was doing a lot of veg smoothies and salads and was constantly suffering with "Bad guts"tender to the touch and diarrhea(spelt wrong i'm sure)and I am glad to say that all that has gone with the leptin reset and supplements(vit d3 and k2)as per epi paleo rx,so I have made some progress with leaky gut but now I need to look at it all again and see if it helps my energy levels.So some work to do!
I am not in the position at present to do any labs although i do plan to get some done as soon as I can.I am in a slightly different situation as I do have a diagnosed illness so I know I am unwell and as it is inflammation based I can assume certain basics but it would of course be a much better situation to know exactly what was going on.Healthcare is pretty basic here and although there are some brilliant doc's there are also many that just go through the motions and what they learned to qualify is all they know!So I can't expect much there and my current neuro is not in the former camp!Off to buy a tape measure and I will log all body measurements on here and repeat every month.

Just out of interest and for my education Caroline,brocolli and cauliflower what are the issues there?Mould?
And indeed if anyone would like to make any input into possible clean and simple meals in particular breakfast it would be greatly appreciated.

Namaste to all

Hi Jeff….I've been following along with this discussion recently and I would second all the wisdom you are getting here. The way I understand CT, is that it has the ability to make the energy you do have work more efficiently…and it can input energy if you do it outside with photons and grounding. But, if you do too much, you are risking running your battery down…as JK described to me, if you can't hold a charge at all boosting your battery isn't going to help. So, it's finding that sweet spot as Sue described it, what's the timing, duration, temperature, atmosphere and frequency that works best for you…..you will know because it will start to feel like an allover boost of wonderfulness. You've persevered through the hardest part which is actually starting! Now for the fun stuff where you can experiment and tweak exactly what you need for any day. All over muscle cramping and clenching is not, in my opinion, a good sign. What your redox potential is and how it is changing will determine a lot as you continue your discoveries….we are all so different and our trajectories are unique….

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-04T16:04:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154717>

Hi Jeff

If you get into the water and get out before you get into the pre-shiver state, you will still have lost heat to the water, helping bring inflammation down, and getting other CT benefits .....

If you have a health problem that involves muscle mitochondrial function, that may also be affecting your ability to tolerate CT. I also wondered about what the vibration from using a chainsaw was signalling to your muscles? Then a workout, and then CT? Maybe more than your mitochondria could handle at the moment? (Beware motivational videos - context!)

I've found CT to be most successful, and my energy generally better, when the basics of grounding, good drinking water and getting sunshine when I can are covered first. Start with the low hanging fruit .....

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-07T17:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154973>

Thank you all!!

Its been a bit of a rollercoaster week,not had one like that for a while!So damn tired all the time,grinds you down in the end.
Two days without eggs or cauliflower an broccoli.I've just been eating steak for breakfast with some good fatty Iberican bacon(pigs fed on acorns,perhaps thats not good after all!?).Definitely felt different,not so acidy.Dinner has been a salad of onion,fennel,ginger,garlic and apple or orange with mackerel and sardines.
Still eating 90% chocolate with most meals.
I've been so cold all week so haven't done ct for 3 days only face dunking,morning and evening.Just can't face the tub at the moment.I have been grounding and in the sun every gap in the clouds and the weather is set to improve a bit.It has been snowing most days and the wind from the north and my house is missing a window on the north side so when it blows it gets quite draughty,great for sleeping though!

Struggling to make much sense of the blogs,my brain is just not processing the stuff at the moment.I will up my membership when funds permit to watch the webinars.
Thank you all for helping me get back to the three legged stool this week.I can see I was sat on maybe one leg and occasionally rocking onto two!!
So concentrating on the three now,see if I can find some stability.

Had two goodish nights sleep(I moved my bedtime to 10.30 -11pm from 10pm and that seemed to help)Last night was not brilliant though.

I'd love to get my ct tub setup in the garden so I can ground and get some photon effect at the same time as ct,so that is the project for this month and then just do some shorter duration dips and listen to what my body says.

Best wishes to you all and thank you for all the support,ideas and advise

### Graeme — 2015-02-07T20:55:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-154981>

Hello Jeff,

I've been following your story and the great efforts you are making to turn things around, especially the CT.

You have high nnEMF, IBM and GERD to deal with and now a lack of energy. You are doing most things right, but I would suggest the following 3 things to help you improve things:

1\. Water: You said you drink 1 litre or more per day of ice cold water. You need more! Quantity trumps temperature here. Try less cold water and drink 2-3 litres per day. You can do it! I managed to get my 78 yr old father up from 1 glass to half a gallon a day.
Drink more, then when you get to 2-3 litres, go colder.

2\. Fat: High nnEMF means you need more saturated fats and more ketosis. Coconut oil and MCT oil are what you need. Eat tablespoonfulls with meals or do BPC (bulletproof coffee).

3\. Omega 6/Omega 3 ratio: Like most people, you will have mainly eaten O6 vs O3 and your tissues will reflect that. (GERD is in part an O6/O3 story). I suggest you go even more fish and reduce the meat. I would also hold off on the 90% Lindt for this reason. As you gradually replace O6 with O3 in your tissues to improve their ability to conduct electrons and light, you may experience some upset from the inflammatory compounds, which are released when O6 is broken down.

I hope this helps.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-02-12T13:28:59+0200

I got chil blains again all over my hands!!Its funny I ordered an infra red thermo and it never arrived,so I wrote it off,then the day after my chilblains it arrived!I took a reading off my hands(back of the palms)27deg c and then the fingers 10 deg c!!!my fingers are in ct!That kind of explains the chilblains.My house is cold in the morning(10deg c <>)till the fire is going.Wearing gloves now in the mornings!

I think I must be really broken just now,tired,weak,emotional and generally fed up.

The constant tiredness is the worst,just gnaws away,even going shopping is a big effort,just want to sit or lie down(I try not to however).Just try and hold it together be patient,do what I can,eat well,drink water and face dunk.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-13T12:00:33+0200

> [Spanishjeff said:](/goto/post?id=155523)

> So relax more do "the good stuff".Breathe,I know my breathing is terrible,very shallow,a subconcious habit I think from living in a very stressful domestic situation for a long time.Its just me now so I need to stop stressing myself out!!

Yeah, cortisol is probably the issue. Tensegrity #2 explains some of the mechanics -- jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/

Essentially, cortisol causes cell swelling, pushes things further apart from each other, and makes things less energy efficient.

For optimal function of the circulatory and contractive functions in the fingers, you need charge-separated water close to hydrophillic proteins. The problem is also that the collagen fibers in the peripheral tissues like the hands and feet are far apart, meaning water is further from a protein, and therefore you don't get as much charge-separation and basically have less motive force to aid with circulation.

Sidenote: Gerald Pollack posted about "Blood Flow Without a Heart" 2 years back -- [http://www.ebnerandsons.com/blogs/news/7936157-blood-flow-without-a-heart](http://www.ebnerandsons.com/blogs/news/7936157-blood-flow-without-a-heart) . His book 'The Fourth Phase of Water' basically expounded on how is it electromagnetic attractive forces that is the main driver of flow through our blood vessels. This requires the presence of charge-separated water.​

To solve the problem you need to tighten up the collagen structures of the hands, so that water in those tissues is as close to a hydrophillic protein as possible. You can do that through systemic addition of electrons to the entire system, or you can add energy directly to the problematic tissues through, therapies like Infrared lasers directly the hands.

Obviously those direct therapies aren't easy to get . I've personally been struggling with bad hand and foot collagen as well, and definitely see a correlation between any sort of stress and swelling of my hands or feet. Interestingly, the more weight that I lose, the better my symptoms are ....

....

I watched a video yesterday from the myositis organisation and they seem to be making some progress.The doctor is ironically from my home town of Manchester!And it was great to hear my native tongue.
I was surprised when he spoke about a subgroup and it nailed a couple of my symptoms,mechanics finger(a rough split patch of skin on the first finger)i'd always thought it was just dry skin and raynauds which explains the chilblains,he also talks about interstitial lung disease and when I had my initial attach of myositis in the UK It was always thought to be a problem of the lungs and the consultant commented that I had some scarring on the lungs.
here is the link to the video for anyone interested(it is a long one)!I'd really like someone to decipher it for me(Dr kruse ideally)as it is very technical.It does give some pointers to some labs I could do to help identify my subgroup and I have to consider returning to the UK as I have been waiting 6 months for a muscular biopsy here in Spain and I don't feel my neuro consultant is very enthusiastic in pursuing my diagnosis or treatment.

best wishes

In a nutshell;

\-Eat seafood... loads... and lots of fat.. and seaweeds! Read the Epi paleo Rx blog
\-Stay away from EMF as much as it gets
\-Spend as much time as you can out in the wild.... barefoot if you can. Earthing = body is healing
\-Look into the sky, often. Every morning as the sun rises 1 minute, and also other times as much as you remember
\-Keep it dark after sunset. No lights on. Only candles or red lamps. Use f.lux on the computer, turn down the brightness and put on blueblockers if you need to read on it after dark. But try not to. You will heal faster
\-Keep it cool/cold if you tolerate. Do as much CT as you feel comfortable with, increase slowly
\-Drink plenty of spring water
\-Think beautiful and positive thoughts, imagine you are healing... I thought about it in the woods today on my walk... I need only the wilderness/woods as my doctor  That and my fishheadsmoothies

> Did a bit of grounding this morning.Wait and see how I am once coconut oil arrives and a few other supplements in line with the epipaleo rx that I have not included so far.
> Mentally ok not miserable which is great in fact i feel really excited about all that i've learned about ketosis and how it links in with cancer and alzeimers and possibly helping those conditions as we probably all know of people with these illnesses.Mary Newport I think is worth looking up,she cured her husbands Alzeimers or certainly improved it!!
>
> Best wishes

Hi, Jeff  I also have a Ketonix Sport version that uses breath to test. Some people say results vary too much but I find that when I keep the breathing out consistent I get consistent readings (I compared them to the blood readings and they seem to match well). It doesn't give you a number, but general light colors. I think it's good enough to know that you are in ketosis anyway: [https://ketonix.com/](https://ketonix.com/)

Namaste

### Spanishjeff — 2015-03-11T12:48:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-158191>

"If you can’t control what you think, you will not be able to control what you do. Your thoughts control your feelings, and your feelings control your actions. All of this is controlled by electrons in your brain and none of you seem to know it."

I just stumbled across this from one of jack's comments in the Oysters vs scallops thread.
It sums up where I am!!
I went to the docs last week as I was spiralling down in a depression of mental and physical weakness.He proposed a basic blood analysis,looking at iron levels etc.My cholesterol came back as always "high".
HDL 76 mg/dl, LDL 171mg/dl, triglicerides90mg/dl. I accepted the statin rx and was then surprised when the doc grabbed a well thumbed paperback from his briefcase and showed me what it said about high cholesterol"lack of happiness"(this was a Spanish book so thats the rough translation),all in my head he said,including he said the inclusion body myositis,which was perhaps going a bit far BUT in many ways he was right,lack of happiness!!!And then to read Jack's comment and the latest blog,I have got back my perspective,Be Happy!!Easy right!

I am sleeping reliably well and have been out in sun and grounding every day,I am going to reintroduce CT and get back on plan,enough being miserable,its just not me!!

Again Jackkruse.com and all the participants have helped me see the light!!

Thank you

P.s ,anybody see me being miserable please give me a virtual slap!!

### Spanishjeff — 2015-03-22T12:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-159166>

It's been a while!Bought some oysters the other day.i had forgotten what a joy they are,with a drop of lemon juice I was in heaven,ate 6 one day and 6 the next.I will make it a regular things funds allowing.
Went to some hot springs yesterday it was perfect,sunshine,grounding and a natural cold ct tank as well as the lovely warm sulfurous springs,another one to repeat as regularly as possible,about 2 hours away so not once a month is possible.

I am having trouble with my legs,they seem so weak and I am concerned that they are degrading quickly,so I am thinking what is the best exercise for building muscle mass?Squats comes to mind,but all ideas welcome,just thinking of one quick session once or twice a week,enough to stimulate some growth without over training,less is more philosophy.

Grounding every day and sleep is getting better and better,yipee!!

A few ideas on ct after yesterdays experience outside at the hot springs.
I much preferred being outside,so I will consider using a nearby river what I would like to know is as I can't be in the water for long without getting really cold and perhaps over stressing my body,Is there more benefit to say a 5 min session in water lower than 50 deg f say closer to freezing or is the benefit from more time at a steady 50deg f,is there any information on this apart from my own n=1.
To summarise what I am saying,I struggled with normal ct at 50deg f for 20 mins,but I know it is more likely that I can do up to 5mins quick burst and if the effect is better with colder water then I can probably handle it better than trying to do a longer session in warmer water.Again any ideas and feedback would be appreciated.

Best wishes to all

### Sue-UK — 2015-03-23T16:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-159276>

> [Spanishjeff said:](/goto/post?id=159166)
>
> I much preferred being outside,so I will consider using a nearby river what I would like to know is as I can't be in the water for long without getting really cold and perhaps over stressing my body,Is there more benefit to say a 5 min session in water lower than 50 deg f say closer to freezing or is the benefit from more time at a steady 50deg f,is there any information on this apart from my own n=1.
> To summarise what I am saying,I struggled with normal ct at 50deg f for 20 mins,but I know it is more likely that I can do up to 5mins quick burst and if the effect is better with colder water then I can probably handle it better than trying to do a longer session in warmer water.Again any ideas and feedback would be appreciated.
>
> Best wishes to all

Hi Jeff

I think you need to be very careful about using water closer to freezing if you can't tolerate 20 minutes at 50F. If you can't tolerate 20 minutes at 50F, why not start with 5 minutes, or less, and gradually increase when it becomes easy? Or start with warmer water for 20 minutes and gradually decrease the temperature? Getting into near freezing water when you are unadapted is a whole different ballgame.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-01T19:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-160079>

A quick update.

Sleep has been great,slept right through the night on various occasions and actually awoke refreshed!
I've been grounding and in morning sun most days and I think in my case it is vital!
Its been really warm in the day so I'm getting the ct tub outside so I can get in to cool down a bit in the afternoon and combine it with grounding and the sunshine,I will just ease back into it and monitor how I feel.

I am back to just breakfast and dinner and don't miss lunch,I went through a phase when i was hungry all the time,I really think the sunshine and grounding are helping.

I have felt "better" overall whilst still feeling terrible every day(kind of like a permanent mild hangover) I am definitely noticing a change!!A combination of leptin reset kicking in with circadian cycle returning to normal.
I still feel generally weak but my strength is improving all the time,so a bit of a contradiction?

Tinnitus has been noisier the last week,which I can't explain?

I have been researching a little the blue blocker theme as again I think it is vital for me to protect my cicadian cycle,but there are occasions when its nice to go out and socialize and the uvex are rather ugly and people think you are madder than they already do!! so I found bolle do a "Dark brown gun"lens from their alpine range that blocks 99% blue light,I don't know if they are too dark to use at night or not,hope to go and test a pair but here is the catalogue page for those interested,there are others that come close but I don't know what % is acceptable?Perhaps someone else has already looked into this subject.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-05T12:22:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-160338>

Been feeling better this last week!I am cautiously optimistic because this seems to happen from time to time then I feel ill again,but yes feeling GOOD!Is it the grounding and the sunshine and the water!Is the three legged stool working its magic...?
I went to get spring water the other day and its been hot here,a truly fantastic March,it was mid afternoon and hot,I pass a river so I stopped on the way back to just see what the water was like and before I knew it I was in and cting!!felt great,I did some Inger inspired dips as it was very cold(its melt water from the mountains right now!!)did 4 dips and then sat on the rocks drying out and grounding into the bargain.
Went back yesterday feeling slightly nervous as this time it was on purpose,dipped again and swam,4 times and the 4th time I just stood up to my speedos(just for you Caroline!)and it didnt hurt in fact it felt good and I felt full of energy and then stood on the rocks watching all my muscles dancing away doing their little shiver boogie!!Really happy about getting back into ct and it is glorious by the river with the birds and butterflies,beats the bathtub any day,seems I am an outdoor cter!I will go as and when and just keep it spontaneous.Got some funny looks by dog walkersLove it!!Dare to be different.

Namaste

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-16T09:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-161385>

A quick update(hopefully).
I have been doing really well,ct in the river every other day(not got a water temp yet,but its melt water from about 10 miles upstream so its cold!!).I only do dips and then stand in it up to waist for as long as I can(5 min or so) and then finish off in sunshine sitting on a rock with feet in the water till I dry off and stop shivering.
I'm out grounding in the garden too every day weather permitting for at least half an hour and I wet the floor where I put my feet to improve conductivity.
I had been sleeping well then it just dropped off,I don't know why,currently there is a bird that starts the dawn chorus half hour before the rest of them,right on my roof,with an awful repetitive chirp!!!!Its driving me wild,I have to get up go out on the balcony and squirt water up on the roof to scare it off(If only the neighbours could see me!but they are all asleep,in the end I give up and get up and at least I get to enjoy the full blast of the morning chorus and see the day begin,which is a real pleasure,so maybe this ba\*\*\*\*d!!bird is really doing me a favor???!!!
Yesterday something odd happened.I have been feeling better and better of late,no miraculous cure or anything of that nature but when you've felt so ill for so long anything is an improvement.I've been working physically hard in the garden and been able to do it!Back to yesterday,I had to go to town to the mechanics and he said I could wait or leave the van there,I elected to wait and normally have a chat with the mechanics(they are keen mountain bikers and in their early twenties so always full of stories of crazy downhills etc),They were busy so I just stood at the door and watched the Spanish drivers trying to kill and maim each other negotiating the roundabout 30 feet away.I had felt good that morning and had jacked the van up and changed some steering components(quite physical as wheels are heavy etc)and was just waiting to have the steering tracked before going for the annual inspection.I had been stood maybe a half hour and kind of felt ill,my usual hangover/flu with a slight dizziness symptoms,so I walked just outside the workshop door to stretch my legs looked around and then up,i was right under some high tension cables,directly overhead and no more than 100 feet above(maybe less)Hmm I thought not ideal,but as I was close to being attended to I kept waiting,in all I was an hour I think.
So off for the inspection and a battle with unhelpful civil servants,the queue was huge and I was feeling worse by the minute,I returned to the office to ask if I could come back the next morning,"No" it was going to cost me a brand new inspection!!(all I needed was to check the new parts fitted and collect my new document)Grrrh!!Luckily the inspector who did the test the previous week saw me and we "Jumped the queue" and i was off home.I felt worse and worse,a headache aches in the joints.I was in bed by 11pm and I will do some grounding today and see how I feel.
once I feel better again,I will go on purpose under the same cables and see if it happens again as it may just be coincidence.
"Slap!!"Thats me giving myself a slap as it is not the most positive posting although for me perhaps it is as it may be a signpost on the optimal journey(maybe I am emf super sensitive?).Not really finished but posting now for fear of losing a long post.
Namaste

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-16T10:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-161388>

Phew it uploaded!!On a more positive note,I have been going to yoga for 4 weeks and it is really helping and I do seem to be benifiting from a ketogenic epipaleo diet,I seem to get stronger with very little effort.
I have made contact with my girlfriend and we have been "going out"and its fantastic,she is in 25 years of dating the person that I really feel sparks with,a very profound connection(she feels the same)so its like we were meant to meet.We dont know what will happen but at least we are enjoying each others company and I think she is helping me feel better(she's giving me her electrons!!).
Spring is here!!Got a friend coming to stay at the end of the month(not seen him for 2 years)and then my parents are coming over,can't wait,I miss them.Then its summer and lots of river time!

What a journey this all is(life,in particular optimal life,thanks Jack!!).We learn so much,I am trying to help people I know who are ill,some listen some don't,just keep giving the info,they will either wake up or not!

I bought a side of home grown Bacon (5 kilos) the other day,oooh its too nice and it is hard to find so has a kind of special value to it.

Bit of a long one today,just had to unload it all and here I feel truly at home with all my comrades!!Namaste to you all(made me laugh Kristi,the yoga instructor actually said it to us and I knew what it meant,thank you!!!for educating me!!).

### Linz — 2015-04-16T16:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-161421>

Hi Jeff,

I love the way that even your 'unhappy' posts sound cheerful. I think we learn so much from health crashes when we know the cause and what to do about it. I have IBD and used to relapse frequently no matter what I did (so I thought then!) and often needed steroids to get back on an even keel. I am still occasionally surprised by a relapse but usually there is an obvious cause although it sometimes takes longer than I expect to regain the lost ground. (no steroids for many years now).

'Some listen and some don't.' As my health improves I have found many people ask and some of my friends have started doing a few similar things, all too slowly although some do ask more if they have seen their own improvement. Before I had much knowledge about why real changes were happening to my health I realised my children had started to say they didn't want pasta/rice etc. Packets of biscuits were going soft in the cupboard, they rarely asked for sweets when we passed them in shops and they noticed that they didn't need sun cream any more. One day my doctor said, ' You don't come here often. What do you do that is different from my other patients?' Sadly he retired before I had an answer and I still hope I will meet him again because I think he would listen. Another time a nurse asked me why there was only one medication in my file when most people had a long list - another missed chance. I do find being able to show people that I don't burn in the sun and use no chemical protection makes a big impression on some, persuading them to download f.lux and to get rid of the inflammatory oils is not difficult . Cold baths are a different thing. I'm sure your gf will prefer the river!!

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-16T22:25:21+0300

After watching this video I became suspicious about my electricity meter it is a sagem cx 1000-3 it uses AMR through IEC 62056-31 bus output which appears to be a readless meter that transmits the reading by either radio signal or telephone line,so I think it is a smart meter!!If anyone can verify this I would really appreciate it and what to do about it!?
Felt awful all day from the power lines yesterday,really ill,just been to yoga and that was great certainly helps.
Namaste

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-17T18:15:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-161538>

I've just confirmed that I do indeed have a smart meter broadcasting for radio frequency!If I can get a doctors report stating that I am suffering from EMR sensitivity,then their might be a chance they can change it but I'm not holding my breath as it is law in Spain that by 2018 all meters will be smart!For now I'm going to put an aluminium roasting tray behind it to reduce the exposure levels.No wonder I am struggling to recover,being a good boy and not turning on mobile or lights and breaker off at night whilst I'm getting zapped 24/7 by the smart meterGot to laugh i guess else i'l cry!!Namaste

### Spanishjeff — 2015-04-20T12:03:22+0300

The sun is shining againJust sat out with my feet in the wet earth and had breakfast(sardines,goats cheese,avocado and coconut oil!!).I am almost back to normal now after my powerline experience!
It has been a positive thing in the end as it helped me identify the smart meter I didn't know about.Spains EMF limits are ridiculously high compared to the rest of europe,so I imagine that all their masts etc are giving out huge doses.I was relieved to see that there is at least awareness here and various groups with information for spanish speakers,so I've bombarded my friends with it all!!See if any of them wake up....
Just got a new supply of all the basic leptin reset supplements,I finished them a month ago after 3 months but noted some stomach soreness coming back so it will be interesting to see if that goes away over the next week or so.
I've slept well the last two nights,is it due to shielding the meter???I hope so.As an extra measure I am moving my bed and desk as far away as possible from the meter.Going to do battle with the doc,see if he will write me a report saying I have sensitivity to emf and then I can try and get the meter changed????
All steps forward and long term(well as soon as possible) I will move to a mountain location,free from phone signals and other houses,did a little tour yesterday and there are some lovely spots,so quiet,it instantly felt right.I will put up some pics when I get a moment.

### Spanishjeff — 2015-07-03T18:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-170554>

Not forgotten about the pics!!Will get some up.

cant believe it last post 14th may!
Overdue an update.Some good news,finally had a biopsy on leg muscle and its come back normal so no evidence of muscle wastage,doc's now going to repeat the electromiogram that showed up the initial problem 2 years ago,see how that works out.
I paid to see a private neurologist although he didn't have much to say as it would depend on the biopsy.
I have been feeling somewhat better and not as tired.Is this to do with Jacks protocols,i think so as I have been diligently living them,no blue light,and following cicadian cycles,early morning sun and eating within first half hr,really upped seafood intake last couple of months and I can be regularly seen clearing out all the tinned sardines in the supermarket,much to the interest of the locals!!I saw Jacks fish eye tip on twitter and once learned how to extract them in one piece find them easy to take once frozen.
Ct was going well till I discovered that it seems all the rivers here are frequented by snakes and some of them venomous although small,so i've not done as much as a bit of venom is probably not a great idea.Found a lovely lake although the water is a bit warm(will be great for winter).I sleep like a baby now and thats a biggie for me and from a macro standpoint blue light,dha and cicadian appear to top the to do list.

I'm considering the altitude too i'm at 1943 ft here and the land is all crushed and squashed up,often you see the layers of rock and slate going vertically up so I assume based on one of jacks comments that this reduces magnetism.So perhaps a move to the coast once the house is finished and sold.
Just had a weekend at seaside and it just makes me feel good and ct in the sea,love it!I also like sailing and windsurfing so its got to be a goal!

Thats it for now,best wishes to all

### Spanishjeff — 2015-09-13T13:28:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-177059>

Yet again time is flying by!!

lots of news!I have had the results from the electromiogram(Not sure if that is the correct term,they test muscles with a needle connected to an oscilloscope looking for correct nerve function).They have come back normal,2 years ago they showed a problem in the waist and shoulders which was initially diagnosed pending a biopsy to be a form of myositis,most likely due to the location and associated symptoms as being inclusion body myositis.

I had been feeling steadily better over the last couple of months,so it was all starting to look good,i have been following Jack's protocols laid out in his book and blog and I reset my sleep pattern and gut function.The Neurologist had signed me off till December for a review.

Then in the last two weeks all my symptoms are back just like that!I feel awful like the flu,bronchitis and a hangover,very tired and a general feeling of being unwell.So I went back to the neurologist and we are back to a full bloodtest and go from there.
My stomach has started to be tender and slightly sore again,which to me indicates that the microbe is not right!?
My diet is mainly tinned sardines,sheep's cheese and some leafy organic greens with mushrooms and onions and organic eggs,supplements are jack's list for immune conditions,coq10 and coconut oil.
What has changed?I have spent more time on the computer,mainly in the day and if at night with blue blockers(going to post this now before it gets lost in space).

### Spanishjeff — 2015-09-13T13:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-177060>

I am grounding every day and have managed to find a morning sun nude sunbathing spot and I have been most days for 15 mins in early am sunshine.CT has been 1 to 3 times a week in lakes and rivers temps from 16 to 20 deg c at a guess,so not ideal but I struggle with it perhaps due to my skinny body comp,trying to keep the momentum going to see if I can keep it up in winter.
I have bought some bolle contour esp for use on computer see spec in link

[http://www.bolle-safety.com/safety-spectacles/contour-contesp](http://www.bolle-safety.com/safety-spectacles/contour-contesp)

they block 57% blue light I put them on for computer work in the daytime and wear them for hours.Can these be affecting my eye clock!?
I am out first sun up for 15 mins with no glasses to syncronise my eye clock then I come in and can be on computer all morning with the bolle's on.Is this having an effect,it is the only change apart from more computer time in general that i can see?

I first became ill 2 years ago and it was 10th august so I also have this coincidence in time of year??And change of season.

Does anyone else suffer from this general feeling of a cold/flu/short of breath going up hill and general ill feeling!My lung function is perfect by the way and oxygen in the blood appears normal too!

Fed up but fighting!!Grrrrhhh!

Namaste to all

### Spanishjeff — 2016-01-05T13:05:35+0200

### Spanishjeff — 2016-01-05T13:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-185455>

I have neglected the journal over the last few months so here is an overdue update.
I am definitely better than 12 months ago,which was a very dark time,mentally and physically.This year I feel mentally very positive and optimistic and physically stronger,I can walk for a good hour at a quick pace and i couldn't do that last year.
I still run out of steam late afternoon/early evening but that's better than being wiped out all day.
Sleep is really good now,I do have the odd night when its not so but overall its great.
I have been doing cold showers every day for a couple of months,only for a couple of minutes but it seems to help,I can ct in a river but struggle with getting into a cold bath.I started the cold showers after studying Wim Hof and he had a "cold shower challenge"so that started it off.I really like his stuff and will buy his course when funds allow.It would be great for him and Dr Kruse to get together,at least in my opinion!
I visited some hot springs the other day and alternated between the river and the springs and it was glorious,I ended up bright pink all over.

The hospital has finished with me and suggest it may be a type of fibromyalgia.I think the electromyocardiogram they initially did that showed I had myositis was erroneous due to operator error(althought they wouldn't admit it)as when they repeated the test I didn't end up like a pincushion full of needle holes and the doctor seemed to have no problem finding the nerve signals unlike the first doctor who really struggled??Who knows but it seems to make sense to me why the repeat showed nothing!
Posting this as part one so I don't lose it!

### Spanishjeff — 2016-01-05T13:30:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-185457>

Part two;I don't really fit fibromyalgia as I dont have pain but perhaps chronic fatigue or ME is closer,even then I don't fit the clinical criteria exactly I can do a lot one day and the next I should suffer "Payback" but I can be fine the next day.
I have been following the leptin reset protocol since I joined the site and have for the last couple of months stopped all supplements and not noticed any ill effects.
I am a big fan of the frozen fish eye and whenever I buy fish freeze the eyes.I've also managed to make reasonable conserved raw fish swedish style and eat that most days(Inger would be proud!).
A move is on the cards as soon as I get the chance and it will be to the coast,fingers crossed this year if I can finish a house and sell it!
A few pics here(not brilliant but I know some of you like pics)of a river swim it was nov 1st so quite cold but it felt great,not repeated it unfortunately as the field is really private and now full of cows,so I have to trespass to get to the river.

### Spanishjeff — 2016-01-05T13:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-185458>

I have a couple of questions if anyone can help that would be most appreciated

I live in the northern latitudes and there is currently no UV so should I substitute with a "black light" and if so which bulb and what time of day!

IR,should I be using it as well at sunrise or is there a specific time and again what bulb(I saw one in Martin's story a rubylux,is that it?

If I have to use the computer in the morning should I use blue blockers(I have flux on max and also turned down all blue on monitor)Or indeed any time in the day?I did try wearing 60% blue blockers when using computer in the day but I think it affected my sleep somewhat,can that be right?At night I wear them religiously.

12months ago i pulled a muscle deadlifting and it keeps coming back,I think it is where the gluteous connects to the pelvis as it is so severe,what can I do?It stops most exercise/lifting,12 months on it should be better at least but it has been so bad this month just from pulling a few weeds!

Any help would be great.I am up to date on the latest blogs and for me they have been big eureka moments!It is just the real life application I am unsure of eg;UV and IR and blue blocking

well wishes and wellness to everyone

### Spanishjeff — 2016-12-14T18:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeffs-journal.12513/post-205266>

5th of January!How time flies.
Had a great summer,living the 3 legged stool,sunshine and swimming in a beautiful river most days.I've really crashed since the light has gone and the sunshine.The chronic fatigue has been a bit up and down over the 3 years I've been ill,perhaps if the worst was a 1 then I have been up to a 5 but currently back down at 3.at 5 I was putting on some muscle almost effortlessly and having the energy for 2 workouts of 15minutes a week.
I have been working hard on mental attitiude and that is a lot better up at an 8 right now its been as low as a -\*\*\*\*\*\*1000!Its steady now though,still end up crying on those days when you've been tired for a week and are so fed up of being tired(to those of us sufferers tired has a much deeper meaning,i'd love one day to just feel normally tired).
I've been cting hands and feet with freezing water and its really helped with poor circulation even did a 10 minute barefoot in the snow.
I keep of forum as it can be a rabbit hole and half a day is easily lost in its bowels!
Although I do miss the interaction with all you starfish out there!

Working on a relocation plan to all year sun as soon as possible,next year it should happen,before winter ideally,in the meantime shortest day here we come!I celebrate with a few friends and a bonfire pagan style as were all vikings from around here.

Every day I say this is it make the most of it and every night I find some point of progress however small it may be.

Positivity to all!

### Kevin W — 2015-01-03T20:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12514/post-151485>

Hello everyone!!!

Just joined. My wife has been listening to Dr. Kruse lately as have I.

We both are being treated by Dr. Kresser. My wife has a rare issue, all of her joints are literally stuck, she is basically stuck in a sitting position. It all started back in 1993 withe a water contamination in NYC (You can actually Google the incident). She fell ill, and then literally overnight her joints all locked. Of course she has many other issues, namely GUT issues.

We both have a variety of issues, namely working on getting our C4A numbers down from mold exposure. We both have the sicker quicker gene and the mold susceptibility gene.

We are really wanting to clean up the EMF, dirty electricity issues we have.

Look forward to poking around and learning more from everyone....
