### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-03T22:10:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12514/post-151498>

That issue is due to a change in the extracellular matrix proteins and their redox state. The MMP proteins are huge in joints. I am quite familiar with this problem since I am from NYC and I had three friends affected who have all recovered for the most part. Most of them sought my help long ago. I am afraid if this is ongoing with your wife this long she may be in deep because her joints could already be permanently altered. Hillview's water is chlorinated and direct summer sunlight breaks down chlorine into toxic chemicals. This incident occurred in the summer of 93 if memory serves me. The atmospheric action of Cl on Ozone has been talked about here........many times. The city and EPA blamed the event on seagull dung and a specific strain of E coli. they were diverting attention away from some recent repair work done on the tanks that had asbestos. City did not want that medical legal burden at all...........I believe the issue might have been a combo effect of all issues that were present. They need to cover those reservoirs and I dont know if they did. CT and DHA was the key to getting most better. One had to go deeper with the use of HBOT and TCM therapy.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-03T22:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12514/post-151501>

One has a sero conversion to SLE with AB but it later went away. The other was suspected for RA. Both were women.......not surprising. Both had to move outside the city. One still lives in 1 hour North of the city and can not return even to this day without muscle skin and joint pain returning. she also developed electrosensitivity and chemical sensitivity to perfumes and soaps.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-03T22:15:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12514/post-151502>

Chlorinated pesticides and crystalline silica are two suspects linked to SLE........and the area around the sites all had pesticide exposure as I recall.

### Kevin W — 2015-01-03T22:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12514/post-151509>

Thank you so much for your kind reply. It's thrilling to finally meet someone that knows about this incident (yes, 1993 and yes, blamed on seagulls) and can give us so much info about it. Disturbing and fascinating! Though we are not newbies to Paleo, been dong typical Paleo for 2 1/2 years, we are newbies to you and your site so we are in the shallow end of the pool as far as understanding your in depth knowledge and applying it. We have never given up hope of recovering my wife, disabled with all of her joints stuck. We are willing to do most anything within our power and are ready to dive in the deep end of the pool. Can you give us a recommendation on where to start and if you have any tips, we would be most grateful. I see you are recommending CT and DHA which we are learning about right now and will implement. (just ordering your book and woke to a sardine breakfast!) Is there anything else that she should do to start (like do we need to be doing HBOT and TCM therapy now also or does that come later? -or should she just focus on those first two aspects for now?

She was initially diagnosed with SLE. She has severe chemical sensitivities. I mean this neighborhood stinks, Mountain Fresh Tide, Bubble Gum Dryer sheets and fertilizers especially in the spring.

Thanks again for your help. Any info you provide is beyond a blessing. We may be newbies but are instant "Kruse" fans!

# Thread: Near a Cell Tower
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 27

### Kevin W — 2015-01-03T20:53:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/post-151491>

Hello again…

**Background:** We are working on and have done loads of changes within our home. Some examples would be we are already hard wired for internet. We do not use WIFI. I only turn on my cell phone to make a deposit 2 times a month. We will not be watching TV at night or using PC’s after dark soon. We want to go to lantern style light at night soon, etc. We will be cutting the power to our room at night soon as well. Our bed is all wood, etc. So we do have a ways (we have a list) to go but have made some nice changes.

I live in a typical neighborhood. If I did turn on my WIFI on my laptop, I can pull in easily 12 WIFI signals from neighbors (I never use WIFI). I know there is plenty I can do to protect my home from EMF. We are working towards much here.

I know everyone is technically EMF sensitive. However in my wife it shows quickly with migraines, etc.

**My question is:** We live approx .62 miles (exactly measured, point to point, not measured driving to it) from a cell tower. I know I need to protect my home no matter what. But even with EMF protecting our home with EMF paint, and more inside, is this just too close? Especially for a really sick wife. Of course we both have issues.

Blunt and quick answers are great!

Thanks in advance for any replies.

Kevin

### Lahelada — 2015-01-03T20:58:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/post-151492>

H.

Everyone will tell you that it is too close. I would have the EMF load measured or measure it yourself. My gut feeling is that you are under high exposure but you might have blocks in the way,large buildings,pine trees. The latter is my theory that you can be in the vicinity of a celltower and have lesser exposure than in places wit a more open infrastructure . Just my theory though,no proof. If you can , move . That is always the best way to go.

### Kevin W — 2015-01-03T21:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/post-151493>

No blocks at all, just homes, some 2 story homes and that is it. The trees are all very small here.
I am measuring in my home, using the Cornet Electrosmog Meter. I do get spikes, trying to determine where they are from, the best I ever do is .0007 mW/m2. However get many spikes to .5, 1.5 and so on.

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=151492)
>
> Everyone will tell you that it is too close. I would have the EMF load measured or measure it yourself. My gut feeling is that you are under high exposure but you might have blocks in the way,large buildings,pine trees. The latter is my theory that you can be in the vicinity of a celltower and have lesser exposure than in places with a more open infrastructure.

### Kevin W — 2015-01-03T22:11:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/post-151499>

In between, we used to live rural. This area was more rural, it is now a master planned community, I live in the block with the star. I live on the outer edge actually and they just so happened to be building hundreds of more homes behind me now. The red markers are cell towers.

Highway 380 is fairly busy, new Walmart went in 1.5 miles or so away from us on 380 and it is getting more busy and packed. North of here fairly rural though. Smaller town, etc. South of here larger communities with some rural and lakes. Sorry for the ling winded answer, was hard to answer yes or no to that. THX!!!

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-08T18:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-a-cell-tower.12515/post-247915>

I agree-- first you need to quantify your exposure to microwave RF radiation from towers in your vicinity before you reach any conclusions on potential health effects or the need to move out.

If you are in the market for a brand new RF meter, shop around for one that can measure the new **5G millimeter waves**. I don't know if any are already commercially available yet, and I heard that they will be pricey vs. existing RF meters.

Now then, let's look at base station (cell tower) configuration in your immediate area. .6 miles away is close, but maybe not too close for comfort. Remember in your physics class they told you that electromagnetic radiation power intensity drops off exponentially as you move away in distance from the source (inverse relationship). Again, you don't know squat about what's going on until you measure, measure, and measure.

Another factor to consider is topography between you and nearby base stations. If you are deep in a cozy valley and they are on hilltops (their favorite haunts) you may be getting even less exposure than you imagined. Bear in mind the cell tower antennas are positioned and aimed to point the focal point of their beam on the horizon.

Another thing to consider is what antenna hardware is on nearby towers. Are there dense clusters of antennas hanging form the tower like ripe fruit? Then chances are you are getting more of a wallop from a variety of RF waves.

Once you have mapped your nearby cell towers (bear in mind that wireless companies will also place them inconspicuously in harmless looking structures like church steeples, crosses, and fake trees-- so beware-- **_antennasearch.com_** can help you find the hidden ones) and have your RF measurements both inside your house and all around your neighborhood you have some invaluable data to consider your next step.

Consider also a viewpoint that if your area is mature and already densely built out with base stations then there is less chance of new ones springing up nearby to totally surprise you (**exception-- new 5G mini antennas!**). A move to a less densely populated area brings no guarantee that, as urbanization spreads, new antennas won't spring up overnight like mushrooms.

### Dennis Clark — 2015-03-16T20:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-west-howdy-from-arizona.12516/post-158682>

Dr. K ... just posted book review on Amazon.

Thank you all for the nice welcome.

Caroline...my expertise is in plant biochemistry, specializing in flavonoids. It has been entertaining over the years to see my lovely natural products achieve such an exalted status in human health. These so-called 'new phytochemicals' have been known to folks in my field for well over a century!

One of the favorite courses that I taught at Arizona State University (until retiring) was 'Integrative Medicine and Society', wherein I included a lot of research on plant chemistry and herbal medicine. The course included everything I could teach about human health outside the purview of the American Medical Association. I discovered the most significant aspects of health and biochemistry to be in energy medicine. Ultimately that is what biochemistry is anyway - energy.

Dr. K's explanations of human/animal metabolism based on biochemistry and quantum mechanics would have been a significant addition to my course if I hadn't retired. The importance of his work to me now is more personal, as a guide for me and my family to achieve optimal health.

I also appreciate all of the speculation about evolution. Some of my best friends are evolutionary biologists, and none has put together the big picture that Dr. K has, especially regarding epigenetics. My training and teaching is from the (now) old school of genetic determinism. I even wrote a textbook that presented genetics that way. Those poor students!

Time to stop babbling now.

### Dennis Clark — 2015-03-16T20:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-west-howdy-from-arizona.12516/post-158683>

Hi, Caroline...one more thing relative to flavonoids is that the 'new star' in my field is resveratrol, which is a sirtuin 1 activator in longevity studies.

[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0049761](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0049761)

...although my personal sirtuin activator (SIRT6) of choice is myristic acid in coconut oil:

[http://www.jbc.org/content/early/2013/09/18/jbc.C113.511261.full.pdf](http://www.jbc.org/content/early/2013/09/18/jbc.C113.511261.full.pdf)

I promise to stop babbling this time.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wim-hof-new-cold-record.12529/post-151770>

### Josh — 2015-01-06T02:52:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wim-hof-new-cold-record.12529/post-151776>

What would limit the maintenance of heart and neural function in the mitochondria during hypothermia? Would all of the ubiquinol be used up as an antioxidant leaving a deficit for starting up the electron transport chain? Would an ubiquinol deficit result in decreased uncoupling and IR production? Or would the generation of reactive Nitrogen and Oxygen species with the failure to recycle Glutathione result in a sudden increase in apoptosis as the ubiquinol antioxodant stores become exhausted?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Figure 2: Modulation of apoptosis by hypothermia. After a serious insult the cell can trigger apoptosis, a highly regulated cell death mechanism.Intrinsic Pathway. Hypothermia increases ATP stores and slows ion channels then maintaining the integrity of the membranes. Hypothermia applied together or immediately after injury decreases the production of ROS. These events limit the rupture of the outer mitochondrial membrane and the release of proapoptotic molecules like cytochrome c into the cytosol. The hypothermia-induced increase in nitric oxide also avoids cytochrome c release and it is even reported that early NO production can exert a negative feedback regulation of iNOS \[[34](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/#B91)\]. Moreover, iNOS transcription activated by NFB was diminished after hypothermia \[[35](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/#B88)\]. Since catalase is absent in mitochondria, maintaining GSH redox cycle is critical to avoid H2O2 accumulation. There is abundant evidence that hypothermia keeps GSH pool. Extrinsic Pathway. It was found that hypothermia decreases the affinity of the death ligands-death receptors, with the consequent inhibition of the initiator caspases like caspase-8 or the NFB-family molecules.

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/)

# Thread: Suddenly averse to wearing gloves and a scarf!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-06T00:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/post-151761>

My hands can be freezing cold to the touch (as they were this morning in the 10 degree F weather) but I cannot bring myself to wear gloves. I have also become resistant to wearing my winter scarf. My body has clearly learned that the cold receptors need to feel the cold in order to start the whole uncoupling process to produce the heat necessary for the conditions. It's some weird psychological compulsion, that's for sure. I find myself increasingly underdressing for the weather, and find it so stimulating and energizing!

### sjoshua — 2015-01-06T01:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/post-151765>

> [Hansen Kenimer said:](/goto/post?id=151761)
>
> I find myself increasingly underdressing for the weather, and find it so stimulating and energizing!

Cool... ha

You are merely adjusting your relativity; under vs. over dressing is just individual perspective

What if... we'd never been versed in the concepts of cold vs. hot, but instead accepted either of them as merely 'sensation' on a wider scale of compatibility with our fleshpods...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T01:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/post-151769>

[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-exposure-cold-reveals-formation-brown.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-exposure-cold-reveals-formation-brown.html)

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-06T17:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/post-151824>

> [sjoshua said:](/goto/post?id=151765)
>
> Cool... ha
>
> You are merely adjusting your relativity; under vs. over dressing is just individual perspective
>
> What if... we'd never been versed in the concepts of cold vs. hot, but instead accepted either of them as merely 'sensation' on a wider scale of compatibility with our fleshpods...

Well, I suddenly love the sensation! It was 23 degrees F this morning and I drove to work with my vents open. Breathing the cold air, freezing my hands off....it was GREAT. I'm high as a kite right now.

The interesting thing is I didn't flip this switch until I started sprinting again two days ago. Even with CT. It's like I burned off some metabolic sludge (I mean this literally, I feel it in my brain) and now I'm craving cold. And fat. My appetite for butter is insatiable. Along with ice cold water. And iced coffee.

I'm very curious to see what happens with HIIT aaaaaand CT aaaaand keto epi-paleo. I'm feeling absolutely superhuman right now, and I think I've just now opened the can of worms....or, the barrel of monkeys. ;-)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### digital — 2015-01-06T00:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-i-had-extra-oysters-tonight-and-made.12531/post-151764>

I propose a new thread be created "What I had for Dinner", and should contain a photo and a short description. Would be like the "What I saw in the news" thread.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=151763)
>
> Oyster artichoke soup........
>

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T01:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-i-had-extra-oysters-tonight-and-made.12531/post-151767>

Melt butter ( one stick) in a large saucepan over medium heat. Saute onion and celery (one pint diced) until tender I add one two two diced carrots too, about 10 minutes. I add lots of garlic but it is your choice how much you like. It is diced. I'd say I put in 1/4 cup in this one. Pour in the seafood or chicken stock (cup to pint), and reduce heat to low. Cook for about 15 minutes. Add the artichokes (2 cups diced) and oysters (two dozen), and simmer for 10 more minutes. Take half out and add one cup of heavy cream and put in vitamix and blend. I add Lemon juice of one lemon and its zest, tumeric, black pepper, garlic, oregano, rosemary, sage, more diced onion and celery with some reserved artichoke chunks with some shrooms. Finally, stir in the heavy cream (another cup if you need to make a few more servings) Salt to taste. To thicken you can use egg yolk or parmeasean. I used the cheese tonight cause I had some left over from NYE party. Add back the Vitamix stuff to the blend you just made so the soup has some chunks in it. Cook until heated through, but do not boil, about 15 minutes o cook down. Serve immediately. If you want to make it a sauce add more cheese or yolks to thicken and pout over anything cause it rocks.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T02:19:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-i-had-extra-oysters-tonight-and-made.12531/post-151771>

Pork loin wrapped in bacon stuffed with cream cheese and jalapeño

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T02:40:18+0200

I was in surgery today and really tired...........and I told my nurse and NP I was going home to jump in the pool tonight (33 degrees) and make something different with a pork loin and oysters......I was in for 15 min and got inspired........and man was this good. I am alive. I even opened up a good bottle of wine and some chocolate from Europe.

I am new to this forum. Have read EpiPaleo RX and have been 80% on a paleo diet for about 2 years. I try to read Jack's website articles but not being in the medical profession find it dense to read and understand what to do. Here are some recent labs:

Bun 21
Creatine 0.72
Bun/creatine ratio 29 (yikes!)
Sodium 143
Potassium 4.4
Calcium 9.6
Vit d 89 (I supplement)
B12 1395 (I supplement, was once at 50)
HDL 92
LDL 110
VLDL 19
Triglycerides 95
Glucose 87
A1C 5.7
AST 23
ALT 19
ALK Phos 78
TSH 1.22

Need suggestions on what to improve on and how to do it.

Thanks for your support.

### BTA — 2015-01-13T03:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-needing-help-in-interpreting-labs-particularly-bun-creatine.12543/post-152605>

Yes - it's the key to if you are able to hold water, use water and dispense water correctly. If it's tanked - you'll never be able to be hydrated  Check Blood & Urine osmolarity. Dr Shoemaker, MD

"Reduced MSH can decrease pituitary production of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) which can lead to thirst, frequent urination, neurally-mediated hypotension (NMH), low blood volume, and electric shocks from static electricity. While sex hormone production is often down-regulated the pituitary may upregulate the production of cortisol and ACTH in the early stages of illness, then drop to abnormally low, or low-normal ranges later."

"The blocked leptin receptor will no longer create the initiation of steps that lead to production of alpha melanocyte stimulating hormone (MSH)."

[http://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/the-biotoxin-pathway](http://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/the-biotoxin-pathway)

.
.

# Thread: Hello, everyone!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley — 2015-01-07T00:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151897>

Posted on the forum about a year ago but thought i'd go ahead and start fresh!

I'm 22 and been an investigator of my own health for about 6 years now. I'm not sure I consider myself a die hard paleo-er as I often question everything I read in every sphere-since I've (probably) tried it all. My journey truly started at 15 when an eating disorder came into the picture. My strive for optimal (especially on the outside) has never seemed as easy..constantly feeling as if I'm in a battle with my body despite the "cleanest", strictest diet and attempts to live a healthy "primal" life. I'm at the point where I have tried to adopt every "guru's" philosophy leaving me restricted and my body resistant. So many conflicting ideas leads me to believe there is much deeper science not yet understood in the paleo community which is why I have always been drawn to Jack's work. My biggest problem right now is just not having the educational background or time to truly understand the principals he is teaching. I am curious if there is any diet templates or a list of understandable recommendations so that I can get started. I'm afraid it may take a lifetime of reading these articles to understand one recommendation to start applying it!

So far I have found a few bits of info that I have started applying like:adding seafood (and not supplementing with omega 3), cut out all starch in winter (eliminated the 15g of squash i was eating) and letting my body get COLD!-I live in Colorado and have been restraining from heaters but haven't adopted ice baths yet

thank you all for any help!

### sjoshua — 2015-01-07T01:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151905>

Hello to you as well! Glad you made it back

You're right, while you were gone there were probably a few dozen new blog posts issued, and then webinars and Q&A's to boot. There is a ton of information available, but you don't have to understand it all to skim the ideas from it and take action where you can. Consider, it may take a theoretical 'lifetime' to get through it all, sure, but is it worth trying if it were to allow you to add an optimal lifetime onto your lifetime, or at minimum allow your lifetime to be filling with wellness instead of pain?

I think the sound bite from Jack that goes something like: 'Learn to be cold, dark and boring, and learn how to sleep well' is the easiest way to sum it all up simply.

My interpretation of it:
cold = take literally, which it sounds like you are already beginning to embrace
dark = follow the sun's pattern for determining if/when to be exposed to light - be it a bulb, a TV, or your phone's screen, etc.
boring = limit stressors, be it emotional/mental (i.e., work out your problems amicably) or physical (like, non-native EMF)
sleep well = take literally, but implementing the first three effectively and this will likely happen on its own

> [Haley said:](/goto/post?id=151897)
>
> eliminated the 15g of squash i was eating

Geez, 15 grams huh? Those are some tiny squashes... Don't overdo it

### Haley — 2015-01-07T02:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151918>

sjoshua-thanks for your reply!

15g of squash....my bad...meant 15g of carbs from winter squash.....that is "winter" squash-still not acceptable? lol

Do you think living in elevation and a cold environment might be enough without the use of ice baths?
I think I'm doing well with my sleep patterns-usually going to sleep between 9-10pm and waking 6-8am. However, since I have cut out that last bit of starchy carbs I am awake a lot during the night-or sort of in half asleep mode. Which ALWAYS happens when I cut carbs. As well as increased cravings and hunger. Never can seem to find a solution for this. Already very weary about ketosis as I have done it in the past with poor long term results and found much information on it not being a good idea. I saw a post with Jack's comments on the issue and again, could not understand what he was saying (not his fault, but my inability).

I do hope this is a happy Q&A environment as I do have many questions already!

thanks!

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151946>

Welcome  from another one with a eating disordered past....... You will heal for sure! This is the right place  Just never give up, and step by step it will get better. Remember healing is not linear.... it will be ups and downs.... but the ups will be bigger  I am not fully healed yet either... maybe we never are... we just improve all the way
The good thing is... Jacks protocols are very easy  and one can start right away.. and they does not cost a cent

I think it is a great move to do the ketosis with lost of seafood! Preferably raw

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-07T17:20:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151968>

### Haley — 2015-01-07T17:30:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151971>

Hi all!

I live in Durango, CO (also in the western slope!)..have the blessing of living in a A-frame out in the mountains..

Thank you for the encouragement everyone. Will posting in other parts of the forum help me get some questions answered?

### Martin — 2015-01-07T17:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151972>

Yes, there's a ton to read on the blogs, but you can get a flying start with Doc's book: The Epi-Paleo Rx. Gobs of data and easily understood in many ways. I'm constantly referencing it for many things, not to mention inspiring one to go cold, eat oysters, and get the hell away from technology whenever you can.

Listen to as many webinars as you can. So many complicated concepts are brought together when Doc goes all "freestyle" and links how things are connected. With those general concepts in mind, you can dive into the blogs for more.

This is the place, the Rosetta Stone, the Cutting Edge, for what you're looking for.

NSH

# Thread: Long arctic winter nights and circadian rythym
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rouanne — 2015-01-07T05:38:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151932>

Hello!

I am new here and I absolutely need some help because I am going crazy :-o I am in my 30s, I have fibromyalgia, and I have about 50 lbs to lose. I am following the leptin reset diet to a T, plus I watch my caloric intake and since I am so gorged on this diet, I only eat 2 meals a day. I am taking supplements too..but I am not progressing at all. If I had only 10 lbs to lose I would understand but I am still nearly obese. I go to bed at 10-10.30 pm at night however I live in the Arctic region and in the winter the sun sets by 3 pm. I obviously cannot go to bed that early  and cannot stay in bed till 10 am when sun comes up. I wonder if it affects my ability to lose weight? Of course I cannot lose in the summer either - when we have 24/7 daylight..
Is there a hope for me or is this perhaps a coincidence?
I am at the end of my ropes because I am not losing inches either. Cant afford to get labs done..so I am in the dark here, so to speak!
Thanks for looking and any insight!

### caroline — 2015-01-07T06:19:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151934>

Hi ...and welcome Rouanne. first thing - please put up a picture - Dr. K's forum rule.

Please start a journal in the journal section and give us some health history - Dr. kruse needs context to be able to point you in the right direction.

What about your non native EMF situation? home? work? where do you live? Do you have blue blockers?

do you drink bottled water? grounding? sunshine? C/T? How is your sleep?

you mentioned calories - the reset is not about calories. I am assuming you are female .... we \[women\] don't lose easily on the reset. It is all about resetting our circadian cycle - that is hugely important.

Also - Dr. K. believes we should get all our nutrients in their natural package ...food.
What supps are you taking?

Do you have Dr. K's book? It covers the first two years of the blog ... so easier to grasp.

Do you listen to his podcasts? they are a quick and easier way to get info.

Please tell us more....

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:29:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151944>

Welcome!
Use candle lights... or red lamps.. I use salt lamps for light... and i use blueblockers + f.lux and darken the screen on the computer in the night......
\+ take care to be outside when it does is light... i think this is really important too...... especially take care to get sunlight/daylight directly in your eyes (no glass in between) in the mornings!

# Thread: Greetings from a Long-Term Lurker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Graeme — 2015-01-07T18:03:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/post-151974>

Hello to everyone!

After several years lurking in the shadows of this blog, all the time basking in the warm glow created by Jack's astounding knowledge and the genuine warmth and excitement created by his forum and its members, and now that I have read every line of text on this site, I felt that it was about time I introduced myself.

Firstly, I would like to express my deep respect and gratitude to Jack for the incredible work he is doing here. Rarely have I felt so humbled, yet simultaneously challenged and inspired by the ideas presented and the complex connections made by one individual. Jack, you are a very special person indeed. I salute you.

Congratulations are also due to the forum and its members. This is the only forum to which I have ever felt the urge to contribute - polite and welcoming, combining practical, knowledgeable help with opportunity for sometimes mind-bending, profound discussion. Even the inevitable, occasional squabble is deftly handled by the moderators and other members - I wonder, is this the world's first meta-stable forum? It seems that the quantum entanglement of Jack and his forum members are more than a match for the frailty of the lower dimensional disorder that is the IT infrastructure of the modern world.

Regarding myself - I may post a journal at a later date, but for now - I am a 47 year old, normal(ish) English male who has lived in Spain for the last 20 years. I was fortunate enough to have had access to a good, old fashioned education. I enjoyed being a good at multiple disciplines, a generalist, but I was a physics major having rejected medicine at the time mainly due to a comment from a respected aunt that success in medicine was more about "bedside manner" rather than medical knowledge. I didn't fully understand the comment at the time, but it was enough of an offence to my scientific mind to make me think twice. I never thanked my aunt, but I would have made a lousy doctor. I eventually ended up with a "career" in finance (I know it's all a joke, but it is, what it is!), but I maintained a keen interest in all things health related especially as I approached middle age and became a father.

So, by way of William Davis and Wheat Belly, then via MDA, I arrived here in late 2011 and I have been here ever since. In my element.

I feel I should also point out that I am a Rush fan. This trivia seems to be of real statistical significance I have never come across another place with so many other Rush fans. We were always a slightly odd minority. I find it curious in the extreme that so many should be drawn to Jack's work.
Thanks to NeilBB and Shijin13 for musically reminding me of this connection recently.

I'll finish, if I may, with a minor criticism of sorts, not with Jack, but with some of the members on the forum. I often feel that the wide range of discussions on the forum, which (simplifying greatly) sometimes get bogged-down either in the practical application of the basic principles or in the highly entertaining discussions of finding the source of consciousness etc. I often think that we need to make sure we don't forget the task that Jack has set himself here. He is trying to revolutionize the world's understanding of health by teaching, using new concepts and connections which he has weaved into his quilt from his years of experience and research across many disparate fields of knowledge. This is a very rare and special thing.

I suppose what I am trying to convey is that we should always remember the fact that we are all privileged to be here. I believe we are witnessing history in the making and I look forward to contributing in some small way to this great effort in the future.

Many thanks for everything,

Graeme

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-27T14:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/post-153987>

You said,
I'll finish, if I may, with a minor criticism of sorts, not with Jack, but with some of the members on the forum. I often feel that the wide range of discussions on the forum, which (simplifying greatly) sometimes get bogged-down either in the practical application of the basic principles or in the highly entertaining discussions of finding the source of consciousness etc. I often think that we need to make sure we don't forget the task that Jack has set himself here. He is trying to revolutionize the world's understanding of health by teaching, using new concepts and connections which he has weaved into his quilt from his years of experience and research across many disparate fields of knowledge. This is a very rare and special thing.

I suppose what I am trying to convey is that we should always remember the fact that we are all privileged to be here. I believe we are witnessing history in the making and I look forward to contributing in some small way to this great effort in the future.

I'll say:
Leaders never cross the finish line first. They wait for others because they are patient. To follow the leader means the leader is bringing others to a destination. That destination is not a place where followers congratulates the leader, it's the place where the leader applauds those who followed and were able to cross the finish line first before themselves. It is not looks, not height, not fitness, not intelligence, not wealth, not clothes, not humor. It is patience that matters for an innovator intent on transformative change.

If you want to change things, then finish early start early. Innovators know that speed matters. If you teach to lead people, then they must always be your top priority. Visionary leadership is packaged with some degree of delusion that pushes an individual forward, but...contains the potential for his or her destruction. An innovator accepts this risk because mistakes often point to areas in which we lack self-awareness of a weakness or threat.

One can have too many exotic drawings of bar magnets etc............but I think it is important not to decide to rule out such discussions. The human mind naturally goes "deep" and 'movements' that rule out thinking or talking about such things usually constrain themselves in some way that shows up later as an important omission. Nothing should be 'taboo' and the way I see it at least so much 'thinking' by ruling out 'deep' questions (even if we easily don't find answers) short change themselves. Medicine itself is I think an example of that..............Dr K last winter talked a lot about how medicine just could not consider a lot of discoveries in physics and kind of put it all to the side and started filling out the crossword in a way that seemed "good enough" at the time but is now shown not to be good enough.....................

### caroline — 2015-01-28T00:40:31+0200

In my opinion - this keeps us all engaged. I have no science background what so ever ...but Jack has never made me feel excluded and that I couldn't contribute in other ways.

I live this simple lesson every single day now ..... I can smile at a complete stranger and touch them lightly on the arm and ask how they are ...and really listen to their answer - and I didn't need a degree in Chemistry!

and .... I am the one that is completely and utterly enriched....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T03:00:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-here-i-thought-all-this-sun-cycle-stuff-was-a-little-far-fetched-8.12554/post-152039>

Rob.........3 things is all you need to focus on.......I know it is hard to believe.......but this is what 10 yrs have taught me. light water and magnetism. That is it.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T05:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-here-i-thought-all-this-sun-cycle-stuff-was-a-little-far-fetched-8.12554/post-152057>

it is the simple math of erg's. We are beings of light and no one appreciates it yet. The sun is several thousand times less efficient than the human body at converting mass to energy, but it lights the spark of light in us and then a cell becomes a dissipative structure for sunlight the rest of its existence. One kilogram of the sun generates only 1/5000th of a watt of power on average, whereas your mitochondria typically generates usually more than 1 watt per kilogram. Now let us consider how light and electric current relate the sun and your mitochondria. When a nerve fires an impulse, a significant amount of electricity is produced. According to Oxford University geneticist and author Frances Ashcroft, the electric field through the ion channel is the equivalent of about 100,000 volts per centimeter. If all the electricity in a person’s body could be harnessed and converted to light by DHA, the human body would be sixty thousand times brighter than a comparable mass of the sun. Sounds incredible huh? Pound for pound, you could be brighter than the brightest star in the solar system. Most people will find this hard to swallow so let us do the math to prove to you this is not hyperbole. The sun’s volume is 1.4 x 1033 cubic centimeters. Each second, each centimeter of the sun emits 2.8 ergs (an erg is a unit of energy). So, the total luminosity of a cubic centimeter of sun is 2.8 ergs per second. The human body has a volume of about 75,000 cubic centimeters. Dividing human luminosity (1.3 x 1010 ergs/ sec) by volume gives you 170,000 ergs per second per cubic centimeter. There you have it. 2.8 erg's per second for he sun and 170,000 ergs per second for your cells. Life requires massive infusions of energy to attain its spark of life, but it needs the sun to ignite it.

### Lahelada — 2015-01-08T12:56:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-here-i-thought-all-this-sun-cycle-stuff-was-a-little-far-fetched-8.12554/post-152072>

Careless of me to decide to be born about a month after a well documented solar proton event. .. ( feb 5 1965)

### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152041>

My Journal

First of all I want to say thanks to Jack for having the insight to join together all these disparate silos of health & nutritional information and assemble a theory that ties it all together. Between you and the rest of the community here, and the shared experiences you have documented has allowed me to try and put together the missing pieces of the jigsaw.

I will try and keep this succinct as many on the forums have a lot of reading to do (myself included). I have read the book, all the RX’s and now starting with Brain-Gut and trying to work through chronologically.

I’m mid- thirties currently living in London UK; I am married with lovely wife and three lovely children (ages are 11, 7 &3). I work for a Technology service provider as a technical Director (IT plus Video/Audio ENG). I have a passion for many disciplines of movement and have studied various Martial Arts, but currently love Parkour, Climbing, Olympic lifting & Gymnastics (in rotation). I also love food and cooking which helps.

Hair: Brown

Eyes: Blue

Height: 5’9”

Weight: 62 kgs @ approx. 9-14 % body fat

The optimisation of my health has been a lifetime goal for me as I have always known bad health growing up; with many people believing I was a moaner / hypochondriac. I have found things in my life that have massively improved my health but it is very much up and down and a recent downturn led me to find this site.

**BREIF MEDICAL HISTORY**

Childhood conditions:

Fractured skull @ 18 months (opened a ‘childproof’ window lock)

Severe Eczema / Asthma / Heyfever

Various Aches Pains / Bodily inflexibility / Pain and tightness near Diaphragm that got worse with stress.

Backache from 12 yrs old

Mental Disturbances from 09 yrs. old – inability to handle stress, depression, low self-worth, suicidal tendencies, & bi- polar type symptoms. Sporadically reoccurs\*

Always had stomach / digestive complaints. Had Symptomatic H.Pylori for years finally diagnosed @ 21 was given a single antibiotic pill and the stomach ulcer hasn’t plagued me since.\*

\*GUT-Brain connection.

Parents:

Mum died 10 years ago of Metastatic Lung cancer that spread to her bones. I’m very much my Mother’s son in terms of bodily health; as she had anxiety issues and generally had a poor state of health.

Dad is prone to back pain and has high blood pressure, and anger outbursts.

### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152042>

When my Mum died I developed a hernia and struggled with the grief, and my digestion got even worse. This led me to find an amazing functional medicine practitioner here in London that blew away all the other complementary doctors I had ever seen before. She helped my modify my N=1 dietary experiments that I had been doing, gave me some supplements.

Then I had two massive breakthroughs

**1\.** About a year after seeing her she diagnosed me with Pyroluria and I tested high on the urine sample. I was given high dose Zinc (Zinc Picolinate, Zinc Citrate, Zinc sulphate) 26.5 mg x 3. P-5-P and a Methyl based B complex high in methylB12 and methyl folate. As well as decent multi mineral and Mag Malate/Glycinate combo. And of course an Omega 3 oil with Q10. This plus meditation massively helped my mood, stress handling, etc. **I was like a new person.**

**2\.** However some digestive issues still persisted and the Doctor recommended the work of Weston A Price (Traditional food diet, like paleo with fermented grains and dairy). I added more eggs and more animal fat + broths, plus more green veg to my diet and it got a lot better. (I now know I had improved my cholesterol levels and glutathione levels to fix up my leaky gut and start to remove inflammation from my gut). **I was now in better health than I could ever remember.** All veg fresh from farmers market and all meat from grass fed/high welfare sources.

Still a couple of things persisted though:

1\. On and off Back/Muscle pain.

2\. High levels of muscle tension all the time which led me to always stretch etc. I now believe this to be systemic inflammation as I have been able to remove these restrictions (just not consistently).

3\. I also have extreme lethargy in the summer esp. after spending a lot of time in the sun (inability to store the energy given by the sun?) I cannot wake up before 0800 in summer, but always wake around 0600 in winter. Vit D levels have been suboptimal but not out of clinical lab range (poor indicator) all three times I have been tested.

4\. Night time frequent urination, ache in lower back gets better upon drinking a glass of water.

5\. Dopamine seeking behaviours.

6\. Always eat loads and not put on weight.

7\. Extreme moods on Full Moon.

For 6 years or so I felt great, and when I stopped feeling good it was down to an external factor such as stress @ work / home, or because I had stopped taking my supplements and the fix was easy.

I was able to increase my activity levels and recover faster and actually enjoy life without worrying too much about my health; until Nov 2014 (prior to finding the Blogs)

### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:36:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152043>

**Symptoms Nov / Dec**

· Loss of vitality / energy swings.

· Constant Muscle twitching

· Headaches (front, back and top – just not at the same time). Grade 1 most of the time up to Grade 7.

· Strong aversion to Light.

· Personality change

· Loud sudden noises would anger me or get me on edge.

· Mood swings (hourly)

· Energy massive slump between 1430 and 1700.

· Tight Diaphragm.

· Pins and Needles in arms at night in bed.

· Loss of libido

· Vertigo & dizziness (Inc. a couple of severe episodes when I collapsed)s

· Bi Polar Esqe mania and depression at the same time.

· Confusion

· Memory loss (didn’t know where I was on a couple of occasions)

· Paranoia

· Waking middle of the night 0300-0500.

· Cold hands / feet

· Severe Reactive Hypoglycaemia when hungry.

· Wind

· Gut Pain.

· Nausea

· Weight Loss (went from 65kg to 60kg in 3 months)

· Crazy sugar Cravings.

· Raised glands in groin

· Dark and Puffy eyes.

In this time the supplements didn’t help, have seen a really good Homeopath who has helped with some of the acute symptoms.

I have also been in the care of the National Health Service (GP and Hospital) here in the UK and have some blood tests (although not the right ones IMHO).

I have also had recently:

1\. ECG

2\. Chest X-ray x 2

3\. MRI Brain and Upper spine.

4\. CSF – Lumbar Puncture

5\. Full neurology exam.

6\. Dr Shoemaker VCS test (self admin)

7\. I have a sleep deprived EEG tomorrow morning (which has allowed me to write this journal).

All came back clear, but tested positive for cytomegalovirus (which states in the book that is correlated with low glutathione).

Also had multi coloured mould issue in the bedroom at the time which is now resolved.

**EMF Exposure**

I purchased a Trifield 100xe based upon info on the site and found the following readings:

1\. Office desk = 1.5 - 3 milligauss

2\. Office Background = 1.5 - 4.5 milligrams

3\. Speaker system under my feet at Desk (100 milligrams) – now turned on only when used).

4\. Horrid Fluoro strip lights in office = 25-30 milligrams – not used

5\. Bedroom environment = 0.5 – 1.5 milligrams

6\. Bedroom clock near wife’s head = 20-30 milligrams

Wi-Fi

1\. 2 Wi-Fi signals at work, 1 I can switch off 1 I can’t.

2\. Bedroom 3 Wi-Fi SSIDs out of my control.

3\. Home living area 7 Wi-Fi SSIDs out of my control.

4\. Have always turned router off at Night at home.

Cell

1\. iPhone 6 – always tried to keep phones away from body/head.

2\. 2 x Cell base stations 150m from office (they don’t read very high on the Trifeild though).

**Changes Implemented since (from info in the book / functional medicine)**

· Fish / Seafood daily

· Once a day seaweed (mixture of Arame, Wakame, Kombu, Nori, Dulse, Hijiki)

· Beef bone broth every other day in soup/stew.

· Flaxseed tea x 1 day

· Flaxseed oil rubbed onto inner thigh.

· Pudding of Almonds/Pecans/Macadamias, with Flax, Sunflower seeds and oils, phosphatidyl choline and cultured coconut cream.

· Strictly low TDS mineral water (in plastic thoughL) 1.5 – 2.0 litres per day.

· Strict bedtimes / rising (when well rested will always awake between 0600-0630 – still dark outside)

· Blue blocking sunglasses after sundown.

· Light timing (trying to be outside at sunrise and sunset). This is difficult in the grey temperate maritime climate of the UK.

· Eating 4-5 hours before bed, bed latest 2300 so eating latest @ 1900.

· Adherence to lying meditation (sensation based like Vipassana) every day pref before lunch.

· BAB – have always eaten a big breakfasts.

· Increased Fat intake.

· CT – face dunking in Morning in 55 deg F water. 6-9 full breath holds. Face and top of head are numb for a while after. Just started Chest icing although takes 20mins to get skin to 55 deg F. haven’t been drinking ICE water though.

· Earthing – on iron scaffold structure in my garden and on grass in cotton socks. Only for about 5 mins in morning.

· I collected water from a deep spring aquifer and have stored in the fridge and started drinking it cold before bed.

· One thing that is hard to get is Vit D from the sun in this country.

· Got a Near IR lamp but haven’t used it yet.

· Turmeric mixed in Coconut Oil

**Current Sups**

Although would love to cut these once optimal and spend more money on food.

· Triple Zinc

· P-5-P

· Methyl B complex

· Liposomal Methyl folate + B12

· [Total Mineral](http://catalog.designsforhealth.com/Complete-Mineral-Complex-90)

· Choline + Inositol

· Mag Malate 1 G at night time.

**Current Symptoms That Persist (i.e. the above changes have helped massively)**

· Phased Headache no visible pattern on timing.

· Reactive Hypoglycaemia

· Varying energy levels (daily)

· Night time urination.

· Some days very low energy afternoon affects mood (induces anger).

· Back Pain

· Sleep disturbed badly after salty meal in evening.

· Sore toes (gout/chilblains)

· Piles

· The BAB (even with loads of meat/fish and Fat) @ approx. 0700 is not enough to keep me going I am starving by 1230.

· Dark amrks

**Labs (should I post these on labs forum)?**

I have attempted to compile a spread sheet for the blood test that I have had done through the Doctors before paying privately.

A few things stand out to me:

· Low blood Volume, RBC and MCV = dehydration due to EMF exposure/lack of Sunlight exposure.

· Low Neutrophils = ??

· Slightly raised HB1AC + mid fasted gluc levels = something to do with Reactive Hypoglycaemia.

· Low Magnesium / Vit D = Low cellular Redox Potential casing Leaky Gut

· Low LDL Cholesterol = Poor liver function?

My doctor has stated to me that all my labs are perfect and they have taken every test possible, so he believes that it is solely a Psychological matter and he will refer me to a Psychiatrist / Psychologist.

In a nutshell things are starting to get better, but I need to get them to beyond Optimal (aim High!), I’m thinking missing Sunlight is a massive thing for me should I get a Lumie or equivalent?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T16:04:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152112>

In the new video's I created for the 2015 optimal reset you will learn a lot about how things go on underneath your perception. You should sign up because it is free. This is what I posted after the video went live.......because it is complex science.

About 12 years ago, researchers at the University of Western Ontario began a battery of tests on the effects of very low magnetic fields in the brain. The data from the majority of these tests showed that very low magnetic fields had a consistent and sometimes profound effect on the areas of the brain that processed unconscious thoughts and senses. In one test, conducted in 2009, researchers looked at the effects of very low magnetic fields on thirty-one volunteers. This experiment was designed to find the exact areas and mechanisms in the brain that were affected by the low magnetic fields. To do that, researchers put each volunteer in an fMRI scanner and prodded him or her with a heated stick. Next, the researchers broke the volunteers into two groups. In the control group, they repeated the exact same experiment with no changes; in the other group, they exposed each volunteer to a magnetic field of no more than two hundred microteslas (a measurement of magnetic flux). Neither the control group nor the exposed group reported feeling a difference between the two tests in the level of pain inflicted by the heated rod. However, the scans of the exposed group showed significant changes in the areas of the brain associated with processing pain (anterior cingulate, insula, hippocampus). The brains of the group exposed to the low magnetic field processed fewer pain signals, although the members of this group hadn’t consciously known it. The results of the study suggest that the effects of low magnetic fields aren’t patent— that is, consciously sensed— but latent. In other words, they could affect and influence brain function without us realizing it. This is why you can't perceive the benefits of the magnetico pad, but if you bio hacked yourself with function MRI you'd see it works. I would remind you all that the Earth’s magnetic field ranges between twenty-five and sixty-five microteslas, about four times less than the field used in the experiment mentioned above. Whether the subtle magnetic field of the Earth would be strong enough to make the human brain sense direction, though, nobody knows. But my bet, is this is how we get our sense of direction and it is how we tell seasons in the hemisphere we live. This information is shared with the Fe/S cores in your cytochromes and has to match. If it does not, metabolic syndrome is the most common disease one would expect to manifest. This is why T2D is the fastest growing neolithic disease in the world today.

> 2\. Never ever turn on the Fluorescent tubes. Use red/orange led lamps.

Grounding mats can be a plus or minus to your particular condition. For my collagen issues in my feet, grounding mats made it worse (trying to put extra electrons into collagen that is already unwound and hydrophobic is basically over-loading the system).

Again, try it if you want to, but everything I said in my previous post about grounding mats holds true, and I personally do not think that it's worth the investment given your situation.

Never using the fluorescent tubes is of course a good idea . If you can get a hold of red lights, and actually swap out all of your lights, then that's great!

\---

The issue with the bed is troublesome though. If I'm understanding right, the measured magnetic field where you sleep is still 0.5-1.5mG despite no electronics being on (and even when the power is killed)?

Easy way to test is to remove the mattress and then just test the immediate area above the bed frame to see if the field is any different. If it is much lower, then you know it's the mattress that is the issue.

If the mattress is what is causing you grief, then the only way is to swap out a new mattress. That's gonna cost you some for sure , plus you need to get the wife on board.

Personally, I'd try sleeping on an airbed on the floor, and if you sleep improves as a result, you know something is screwy with your bed.

The next cheapest way I can think of is to swap out your mattress for a cheap futon that is thick enough to provide the feeling of a "real mattress", and then see how things go. Then if the outcome is positive, maybe get a "proper mattress" that has no metal at all when the wife is fully onboard.

....

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-08T22:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152152>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=152102)
>
> Yew - what was so different about Darwin (other than the population) that made that much difference?

I don't know, since I never got up to Darwin

It's even closer to the equator (at 12.5deg vs Cairns at 17.0deg). It has a smaller population, though I'd argue that density is greater in Darwin (130,000 people in Darwin's 112km^2 vs 150,000 people in Cairns' 488km^2).

Main issue for me was that access to the International Airports is much more difficult in Darwin, and it's generally further away from infrastructure. Example: postal routes don't really go through Darwin that well. It's also further away to most of the major Australian cities than Cairns is.

So since Cairns did it for me, I decided to stay here instead

(And the property prices in Darwin are crazy , while I can get a half-acre house here at AU$700k)

### Clayton — 2015-01-08T23:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152157>

Just to add to the grounding mat issue. Neil has mentioned on here that he uses an EKG sticker instead of the grounding mat, makes for much less of an antennae and also doesn't cost $200. You could probably get a grounding set up using this method for under $20. Having said that I am just experimenting with this strategy. I sleep in a faraday cage and have all breakers off at night and no smart meter....not sure I would risk it if I was surrounded by sources of EMF.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-08T23:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152159>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=152118)
>
> It also makes me think the trifeild is not sensitive enough.....
>
> Would you recommend the magnetio for everyone?

Yup, I personally think that measurements using any trimeter is not an accurate measure of

-   Firstly, the earth's magnetic field is pretty damn small.
-   Then there are other factors to the magnetic field, like it's inclination

The only way I think that we'll get an accurate measurement is to get a SQUID that measures the magnetic field the same way that our mitochondria measure the magnetic field. This is really (really!) expensive at present.

And even if we can do that, does it lead to better clinical outcomes? I don't know, and would like for SQUIDs to cost $200 so that we can try it in the field and see if better measurement ability actually help us.

\-----

Magnetico is always a plus. But it sure is expensive .

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=152098)
>
> One thing bothers me in this my work environment hasn't changed in 2 years and I moved to this rented house in dec 2013, so if this is purely EMF based then why did it kick in Oct/Nov 2014?

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=152100)
>
> And I have previous history of many of the same conditions prior to the levels of nnEMF we see today. And these have presented in both rural and small metro areas in the UK other than London. e.g. Oxfordshire & Bristol.

Based on what I said above about our sensitivity to small changes in the magnetic field, it's hard to say that the environment didn't change. Part of the reason why an apartment is troublesome is that what your neighbours do can affect you tremendously. Also, the wiring in an apartment can be troublesome. Or maybe just a new radio base station popped up recently .....

\---

I also want to say that one of the things I took away from the EMF4 blog post is that you can "cover up" symptoms by eating a high-carb (fast energy!!) diet. By switching to a more optimal diet, you can paradoxically make things worse before they get better.

...

I live in a smartmeter hellhole and was thinking about a low cost solution using this faraday tent from lessemf.com [http://lessemf.com/personal.html#313](http://lessemf.com/personal.html#313) but I seem to recall Dr K cautioning in a podcast about using a faraday if you are not getting Schumman resonance. I sleep on a futon in the living room. It is a single floor house in Florida. We have buried power with a junction box next to the house. Our porch is concrete slab with a layer of colored astroturf-like carpet. Do I need to get a Schumman generator if I am going to use a faraday cage? I was planning on sleeping on the futon with the faraday foil tent, but wondering if I should sleep on the floor or the porch floor. If I have to go outside and sleep on the grass I am also concerned about the buried cables causing me issues. I was also wondering if I could sleep on the futon in the tent if I hook up an ekg sticker to ground wire out the window to the ground (again, the buried cable concerns). I would be most grateful to know if there is any hope here. I am making a faraday cage for my smartmeter (after that maybe I can convince my immediate neighbors to allow me to make theirs). ### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-09T07:09:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152199>

> [[email redacted] said:](/goto/post?id=152192)
>
> Anyway my question is if you care to or have the time to answer what did you notice that improved. You have said more than once in Melbourne almost no matter what you did it was not enough (kind of how I feel about living in LA now) but what exactly or as close to 'exactly' was the difference. I pretty much have decided to move to Ireland but to make myself more solid and commited about my decision I want to know as much as possible and it seems you have experience of both and you have such a great brain I want to 'borrow' it for a minute if you can.............

Well, 4 months of great sleep and signs of disease reversal in Ireland is a good enough reason, isn't it

Sleep was not and still is not a big issue for me, so that has basically remained consistent. The big issue for me was gut health, and the first thing that I experienced was an immediate improvement to the bristol score and stomach bloating in general. "Immediate" meaning over the first 2-3 days. I'd still be vulnerable to having gut issues when I ate the wrong stuff, but slowly over the next 2 weeks I started to become more resilient.

I did not really feel that different otherwise. The gut issues usually come only when I have some dietary transgression, and I was staying pretty clean (though I did eat too much chocolate on 2 occasions during those first 2 weeks).

It took about 3 weeks to find a place next to the beach, after which I settled in and got to the beach every day. That's when the gut issues disappeared completely almost regardless of diet. Sometimes I'd get a transient bristol 7 due to eating too much nonsense, but within 24hrs its cleared up.

Then about the time I made the move to the beach, I experienced a sudden increase in motivation to sit down and do work, which meant writing more code, reading more stuff, and writing more on this forum. The mood centres of my brain obviously got a kick out of being on the beach.

Then slowly over the next 8 weeks, I saw improvements to hand and foot collagen, and I started to lose weight as well (despite keeping calories the same, and yes, I still track calories thanks to the good-old bodybuilding-obsessed days ).

\----

More generically, I think it's helpful to have anywhere from 2-3 important qualitative metrics of your disease state, and track those closely after you make a move.

For me, it was the bristol score, and general waist and weight measurements (the most accurate measure of "bloat" for myself). For @[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) , it's probably a good thing to track sleep, and perhaps stuff related to T2D like blood glucose (especially after-meal glucose readings).

And of course, trust your instinct to a certain degree. Implement the protocols, move to a good place, and track how you feel on a consistent basis.

...

> Energy massive slump between 1430 and 1700.----->> low cortisol,
> work on Circadian Rhythm, check pregnenolone, progesterone, DHEAs, cortisol,serum 8AM/12PM (also 4x saliva cortisol),
> likely all four hormones are low, plus probability of bad cortisol profile.
> If preg, prog & cortisol are all low (like I had), I used pregnenolone-MLM (Micronized Lipid Matrix) to raise all three.
> DHEAs should be raised first.
>
> Note, this is a way to let body figure out the real time needs for cortisol. It beats any hydrocortisone, prednisone or medrol supplementation.
> Also phospholipid supplementation may help (listed below)

> Overall less costly plus eliminates unnecessary supplementation.

### dantothep — 2015-05-04T12:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-163251>

Current Key indicators (things if significance) for my health based upon various JK blog posts & book.

Symptoms:

\-Wavering energy levels inter and intra daily wide variance.
\-Occasional brain fog (only once or twice a week now - 15 min nap can fix this)
\-Occasional adrenaline releases (without logical/visible external stimulus), couple of times a week.
\-Pain, locked muscles between shoulder blades pretty consistently. MRI shows some early degen of discs throughout spine.
\-Sleep only good if enough carbs eaten throughout the day.
\-No consistent Bristol type or time of delivery.

Key Indicators from Blood Tests:

\-Low Vit D (60)

\-High SHBG/low free test

\-Low quartile cortisol and DHEA (correct pattern though)

\-melatonin low slightly raised @ 1400 hrs,

\-low T4, T3 borderline low, Rt3 ratio 12.5

\-High Bun:Creat

\-LDL 140

My current thinking towards the cause of my health issues - Brain injury both physical and Environmental:

\-After reading more into TBi/CTE A lot of the symptoms I had experienced as a child were relevant. I have had quite a few head, neck and spine trauma's growing up including fractured skull (opened a child proof window and dropped out on my head @ 18 months old.) There is a lot of research into HPA function and a history of concussive episodes.

\-I took charge and fixed a portion of my health (diet and lifestyle), though religious use of low TDS spring water, and learning mindfulness was probably the biggest factor IMHO.
\-My previous office and home environment config. All the usual suspects here - blue light, nnemf. Have removed all removable sources of EMF (inc. speaker system under desk that used to rest my feet @ 100millgauss), no longer use any internally fixed lighting in office (all fluoro).

\-Exercise and eating late after 2100 - Did this for 4 days a week for 5+ years. Exercised to excess for the endorphin rush, felt crap/stressed/angry 24 hrs after it had worn off.

\-Lack of adherence to light cycles - always working on laptop till late, reading on a phone till late.

\-Pushing myself too hard in everything I did.

Thoughts:
ASI – when I had this done (01/04/15) I felt pretty good, my results showed I lacked enough cortisol and dhea. Hopefully if I can raise these levels endogenously or exogenously I should feel amazing!

Leptin Reset – I did this for 5 weeks and had all the signs of LS coming back, such as being able to go from 0800-1700 without needing food, feeling v warm at night, CT felt easier and easier. Since a mild concussion (12/04/15) I have lost a lot of this. My ft3/rt3 ratio was <20, but I have no visible signs of LR, could this mean I still have some LR in my liver? and If I’m to do the Leptin Reset again how can I hack the reset to affect the liver?

### dantothep — 2015-05-04T13:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-163255>

Brain or Gut – Which to heal first?
I probably need to read more blog posts to understand this correctly, any ideas from anyone are much appreciated. I find that selecting the correct protocol is probably the hardest thing to gauge.

Future – I feel I will try DHEA at 50mg day titrated up without changing anything else, and then retest the ASI. See what that says but maybe it’s just a case of carrying on with the protocols and letting nature take its course; these things will take a few years to reverse IMHO.

### dantothep — 2015-05-04T14:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-163258>

Jan I really appreciate your offering advice throughout my journal I really do, and Jack says that you sure know your stuff.

I have to laugh at your term get serious as I take this stuff pretty seriously, maybe the point of it being counter productive in terms of management of stress levels, acceptance is a virtue that I have to live by when less than ideal circumstances present themselves. Essentially my current wage just about covers my rent, bills and feeding a family of 5 (some months I wonder how I am going to feed them when we don't get paid on time, or the car breaks etc) with no money left for holidays or rainy day saving, which is why it took me 3 months to save for an ASI test. This is also the reason I haven't taken many of the supps you recommended to me before.

The current rental market in the south of England has risen by 25% in the last 18 months (my wage hasn't), and I am somewhat trapped in my current environment. My whole life situation is pretty stressful and stressing about moving home (and the cost associated) becomes a negative feedback loop in itself and further worsens my condition. I know very little about you or your situation but if you have ever experienced the above you will understand.

Considering my current situation all I can do is my best for my health, and my family and to not beat myself up too much that things are less than optimal, both financially and medically. So I will continue to push ahead and try to win more business at work to better my financial situation, and do what I can to mitigate the environment I find myself in - even though the two goals are conflicting at times. e.g. Taking in more sun/grounding & not working in the evening vs. doing the number of hours a day needed to get the work done. I will do my best to improve my situation, If this ends up with disease and death then so be it, hopefully I will have passed on enough information to my children to help them and their children.

So I will try and follow the protocols as closely as I can on a limited budget, probably take DHEA for 4 weeks then retest ASI (without Testosterone or Melatonin, because its cheaper).

IS THIS SERIOUS ENOUGH?

Much love for any guidance.

p.s. Before I move I need to buy a meter than can test RF EMF (1MHZ+) so that I can test current home and ensure new home has less nnEMF from cell phone masts.

MY BIGGEST BUGBEAR AND CONTINUED FRUSTRATION OF THE BLOGS

Is the ever changing advice as new things come to light, and not removing of old blogs if the advice is not pertinent or correct.

E.g. Cortisol, DHEA, Adrenal fatigue

Previous blogs that have similar Rx's

[http://www.jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/](http://www.jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/](http://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cpc-1-dhea/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

Then JK posts this on another post:

Taking supplements is akin to losing negative feedback control in your coupled systems. My 2015 April webinar hits that out of the park. Predator and prey used as an example. When you affect one aspect of their ecosystem enough both become extinct. When you take a supplement you affect the balance nature created so then you ruin coupling and signaling gets worse........not better.

SO WHAT TO BELIEVE? PARALYSIS BY ANALYSIS.

My take is that I supplements are to be taken as a short term intervention, taking the load of certain systems so that the body can catch up. Somewhat like CT slows down cellular time to allow for changes to happen without inflammation getting in the way.

Dr. K was leading us into another way of thinking. If he would have given his suggestions for our health, without leading us through the science, most of us would be long gone. We lost enough people when he brought up EMF as a problem. Sometimes he would put up a brilliant blog post, only to be met with a thunderous silence. If we were in a classroom, I imagine he would have looked out and saw deadfishonthebeach-looks in our eyes. So he would have to regroup, and approach these new ideas in a more piecemeal fashion.

When we would ask him questions on the forum, he wouldn't answer if we weren't ready to go down that path. That often made us upset, so then he started answering the questions with partial answers. Or textbook-like answers, which sufficed until we were ready to consider something different.

Sometimes I was aware of this process, other times I was just pissed as hell! ;-)
I can see where reading old blogs could get really confusing. But I'm not sure there is an easy way to digest all this information. Perhaps the webinars.

### JanSz — 2015-05-05T21:29:40+0300

dantothep,

The ASI test was the best you did for your self.
It was about worst test that I have seen.
Now you know where you stand.
You can only go up from where you are now. Take it as a good news.

Reminds me of IvanDrago.
We have not heard from him in the while.
I recall wondering that for him his home environment was so toxic that working on a hot sunny days installing roofs could not overcome that.

Medical student going roofer then fisherman hand on a boat.
\------------------------
Your cortisol is flat-lined and all the time on the bottom.
Hardly any DHEA.

\---------
In the blood, when healthy person tests major steroid hormones by weight and take that as 100%,
his DHEAs level fraction is 95%
his morning total cortisol fraction is 4.25%
the rest is 0.75%
and
you hardly have any DHEA and cortisol.
(I am using 300mg/day DHEA, without testing you likely may try up to 200mg/day)
\===========================================
Dr Kruse tells us that when all is considered food comes as #3 in its importance.
#1 light (form of EMF)
#2 EMF
#3 Food

Food cost $$. Sardines, butter, eggs, chicken livers (and hearts) are very good food. Tap water.
As for #1 & #2
I got idea:
Now is May. You have 4-5 months until it gets cold.
Get a tent and porta potti, find a place (forest, park, lake, gardener, farmer), spread out your tent there.
Sleep there, spend most of your free time there.
Visit your family (if only to change clothes take a shower and empty porta-potti).
\-------------------------------------
Do not worry about anything, how you eat, what supplements you take, minerals, anything.

Just work on getting your cortisol and DHEAs as best as you can.
All else is also important, but it is second after these two.
\-------------------------------------

Hey Jan,
