Were they not living in elevation? I even remember Kruse commenting that this is why people living in CO are so healthy (because of the cold and other reasons). Also, I will add that CO has always been my favorite place to visit. Growing up I had a lot of depression and every time I came to CO I just felt good and felt more alive. Then I moved here 7 months ago and immediately my inflammation started leaving my body and I got huge amounts of energy. And when I went home to TX for 2 weeks over Christmas I immediately felt TERRIBLE in every regard. My digestion, depression, inflammation all came back hard…and this was before I knew location made a difference to health..but intuitively I KNEW the change geographically is what set me off. I then got back to CO and within ONE day my symptoms were being relieved. So, it is hard for me to hear you say that. And when I went home to TX for 2 weeks over Christmas I immediately felt TERRIBLE in every regard. My digestion, depression, inflammation all came back hard…and this was before I knew location made a difference to health..but intuitively I KNEW the change geographically is what set me off. I then got back to CO and within ONE day my symptoms were being relieved. So, it is hard for me to hear you say that. I don’t want to critically argue with your points but rather have a discussion about it because my experience doesn’t seem to be congruent with these findings.

Got the message and read it

The answer to the seeming contradiction with Colorado lies in EMF4, which I will admit is a very long and complicated read -- [http://jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/](http://jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/)

At altitude, you are definitely going to get more solar and cosmic radiation, which will cause all effects that Dr Kruse has been talking about regarding calcium efflux, increased glucose utilisation, and a general perceived need for carbs (fast energy).

Also at altitude, the SCN and PVN have to run faster relative to the rest of the body's circadian clocks. This was covered in the January 2015 Webinar, showing how orbital GPS clocks require to calibrate themselves to run faster to offset relativistic effects.

If there is a mismatch between the SCN/PVN and other organ clocks, then my personal opinion is that some degree of electromagnetic communication breakdown occurs, and the body needs to produce more signalling molecules like ROS/RNS, which do incur an energetic cost.

Also, for the SCN and PVN to measure environmental factors given less time, they will need to maintain a higher redox. Most people today attempt to solve that problem by adding more dietary carbohydrates into the diet, which as EMF4 points out, is a strategy that has biologic costs in the long run.

Also at altitude, there will be reduction in gravity (which is a derived force of the electromagnetic force). I personally think that lower gravity provides less "signalling spectrum" for a cell to read the environment with.

Also at altitude, there will generally be lower air pressure. The February 2015 webinar got into this topic, but basically, higher pressures (including surrounding air pressures) will allow for molecules in the body to be closer together, and therefore allow for more quantum mechanical interactions to happen. Generally, you only start to see quantum effects below the distance of 100nm.

I personally believe that humans are designed to function best at sea level, along the coast, eating the diet that gave us our huge brains (DHA) and genetic spare parts (viral DNA through our leaky gut).

\----

Dr Kruse has alluded to the fact that Colorado has an unusually high suicide rate, which I would guess is due to neuro-degenerative issues that stem from the stressors I listed above.

Eating a high carb diet and living the modern techno-phillic lifestyle (both of which most people in America do) can definitely portray an outward image of positivity, creativity, and liveliness, but it also tends to hide potential neurological energy losses that lead to the diseases that Colorado sees.

\----

The Sherpa people were chronically exposed to cold at altitude, which itself is a way to increase magnetic effects within us, which is basically the end point of increased gravitational or pressure effects.

Their diet tends to be carbohydrate rich, though they are known to alternate between fat and carb sources.

Traditionally, the Sherpas were also very active, which if your body is capable of handling, can lead to lots of cell component recycling (autophagy), which does not occur the expensive cellular costs of replacement. This depends on the cell having a high enough redox potential to deal with all the ROS/RNS signalling produced through this sort of activity, and then to respond with the appropriate autophagic response. A sick cell is unable to do this, hence the recommendation for people to avoid exercise when in a Leptin Resistant state.

It is known that the Sherpas have a very good VO2 max capacity, and are great examples of how cold can enhance a whole bunch of performance related factors. Despite all of these performance enhancements, and the likely possibility that the Sherpas live a happy life, it is not known how their longevity and other parameters of what we would deem "desirable" would fare if they had a different environment.

Also, there may be genetic factors at work here. While epigenetics is strong, trans-generational genetic changes can still have a major impact, that is completely unknown right now. ie: can someone like me, who comes from a warm-humid-climate sea-dwelling ancestry adapt to altitude in my lifetime? No clue.

Sidenote: Peter from Hyperlipid has talked about a gene mutation in the Canadian Inuit that increases likelihood of high FFA serum concentrations, and which makes carb consumption as a child essentially fatal. Anyone with this mutation is more inclined to live with an uncoupled metabolism, which has profound implications for dealing with large proton loads (would we call excess protons "inflammation") -- [http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2014/11/coconuts-and-cornstarch-in-arctic.html](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2014/11/coconuts-and-cornstarch-in-arctic.html)​

\----

The more important question: is it possible for you to reverse your particular disease at the place that you're currently at?

I don't know , and the only way to figure out is to live in different environments. What I've laid out above are just some evidence-backed points regarding extra stressors that you will face at your current position vs at a lower altitude.

Of course, altitude isn't the only factor, and your home in Texas could have even worse stressors, like a nearby cell tower or power line. All we can do is try to get as many generalisable facts correct, and then plan and run our own experiments.

Hope that helps!

....

My digestion, depression, inflammation all came back hard…and this was before I knew location made a difference to health..but intuitively I KNEW the change geographically is what set me off. I then got back to CO and within ONE day my symptoms were being relieved. So, it is hard for me to hear you say that. I don’t want to critically argue with your points but rather have a discussion about it because my experience doesn’t seem to be congruent with these findings.

Haley, I am with you a bit on this one. I feel GREAT when I go to CO and am NOT in Denver (EMF HELL!!!!). Something about being surrounded by mountains is just so calming for me. I have never had any issues with altitude when I have been out there. My sister, DH and DD all have got headaches and felt a bit sick. It may not be the best place but there are places that are WAY worse (NY or LA).

Im sure you and your references have some very great ideas and I am very open to hearing them. Right now I'm going to focus on what i can do. And in the meantime be at peace with where i live, as i have never been happier with where i call home..mountain air..trees..rivers..wildlife...sun almost every day of the year...yes, **nicld**..i could be worse off!
>
> I am wondering if anyone has any tips for keeping grocery costs down? I have been trying to supplement with canned foods, buy the wild fish on sale, some frozen....but i just can't seem to keep enough fish around the house without going way over my budget.
>
> Also wondering if all of my meats must be seafood? At least during the winter? I have only been eating seafood for the last few weeks, except for occasional meals out that may have chicken or bacon..and i had beef once..also tried liver again and just cannot get it down.

Yup, hope you can make it work where you are

You want as much DHA as possible. Do not worry about getting wild fish

The tradeoff with DHA favours quantity over quality. Eating more lower quality seafood is going to be better than getting less higher quality seafood.

The only tip is to try and get canned herring or mackerel first and foremost, because they have the highest DHA concentrations of the common canned fish varieties. Here's a thread on that topic -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pdf-nutritional-data-on-high-dha-seafood-sources.11276/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pdf-nutritional-data-on-high-dha-seafood-sources.11276/)

Do not stress over cooked vs raw either. Again quantity trumps quality -- eating 16oz of cooked fish will get you more DHA than 8oz of raw fish. See this forum post for details regarding cooked vs raw and how that affects DHA amounts -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/herring.11931/#post-144804](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/herring.11931/#post-144804)

Of course you can eat other meats. But make sure you hit your seafood quota first

### Haley — 2015-01-30T18:49:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-154281>

Oh, I wouldn't have expected you to advocate farmed fish. Would the omega ratio not matter? Or the fact that they are grain fed? I have to say wild taste soooo much better. But again, cost is important here.

Is it a waste to be eating a wild cod as a sort of filler-you know-along with the salmon, sardines, oysters? Its usually on sale so I have maybe 8-16oz/day.

Typically what my day has looked like: 1/2lb salmon, 1lb cod, 1-2 cans of sardines or oysters...along with leafy greens and EVOO or CO. Would you say that is efficient?

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-30T20:56:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-154286>

> [Haley said:](/goto/post?id=154281)
>
> Oh, I wouldn't have expected you to advocate farmed fish. Would the omega ratio not matter? Or the fact that they are grain fed? I have to say wild taste soooo much better. But again, cost is important here.
>
> Is it a waste to be eating a wild cod as a sort of filler-you know-along with the salmon, sardines, oysters? Its usually on sale so I have maybe 8-16oz/day.
>
> Typically what my day has looked like: 1/2lb salmon, 1lb cod, 1-2 cans of sardines or oysters...along with leafy greens and EVOO or CO. Would you say that is efficient?
>
> Very interesting about the raw vs.cooked...I have to say seared with raw inside is my favorite, too! and i never thought i would be able to eat raw..

I think your daily template is efficient enough, and you're probably getting a good 2+g of DHA a day (which is a lot )

"Cod" here I'm assuming is NOT referring to cold water cold (like from Norway). Warm water cods generally do not have that much DHA. Cold water cod is great, and is probably cheap enough if you live in the countries next the seas where these fish are native, but otherwise it's probably not very budget efficient.

"Is it a waste"???!!  No seafood is a waste .... especially not for you, who will need as many food electrons as you can get

\----

Ultimately, the question to ask is what you do have to do to **maximise positive outcomes** given your current constraints.

More genereally, I'm saying to just eat as much DHA as possible, and to get it in it's natural form as much as possible

"Natural form" mainly means:

-   In the sn-2 position on the glycerol backbone. We don't know why this is necessary, nor how it works in detail, but we know that being in the sn-2 position is critical in getting DHA specifically into the brain.
-   With the fatty acid surrounded by water and proteins and natural ionic cosmotropes like iodine, selenium, magnesium, etc ....

Which is to basically say to eat it in fish meat.

You're not likely to get this in fish oil supplements. You will find some of my and Jack's opinions about this topic in this Ask Jack post -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fish-oil-supplements-vs-fish-whole-food.12133/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fish-oil-supplements-vs-fish-whole-food.12133/)

Based on the evidence linked-to in that thread, it is still my opinion that any supplement, regardless of how well it is "cold processed" or whatever, is not going to have a large percentage of DHA in the sn-2 position. The paper I linked to in the Ask Jack question showed that 75% of DHA in the best-thing-we-can-currently-come-up-with LysoPC form still experience sn-2 to sn-1 migration within 20min in 37C (body temp) conditions.

In my opinion, it's going to take more than 20min for DHA that is consumed via fish oil pills to reach the blood, and by that time most of it is sn-1 DHA. Given that convention fish oil pills probably have a lower percentage of sn-2 DHA to begin with, you can see why I'm not a big fan of fish oil pills, and would rather recommend the worst grain-fed farmed fish

\----

The canned fish that I encourage the most due to having very high DHA concentration (herring and mackerel) are generally not farmed. The canning and cooking process obviously loses you a lot of DHA, but still, you're probably going from 5g of DHA in 16oz of raw mackerel to 2.5g in a raw-weight-matched 16oz can of mackerel (50% loss in DHA).

Sidenote: sardines are 3rd on my list on canned fish to buy (DHA concentration about 600-700mg per 100g raw serving), so keep eating those sardines

Any mackerel is good, with the exception of Spanish Mackerel, which is a large predator fish (generally we'll advocate for smaller prey fish) which both has a lower DHA concentration and a poor methyl-mercury to selenium ratio (too much mercury).

Canned oysters in general I personally think are a little "meh". Many of the benefits come from the raw oyster -- CoEnQ10 is lost when not raw, so do the prebiotic glycans. The DHA concentration of oysters aren't that high too (about the same as sardines, but cost 5 times as much). Pickled and jarred oysters are a different story, since they aren't heat processed, and you probably don't lose too much of the CoQ10 and pre-biotic glycans as a result. They're still not cheap though ......​

\----

I'm sure that when we eventually get to measure the biophoton profile of wild salmon meat vs canned salmon meat, we will find a difference.

\----

Again, we're trying to maximise positive outcomes given contextual constraints.

Some people like to argue all day about is raw vs cooked better, canned vs fresh, farmed vs wild .... plus a discussion about ethics . Team People dammit! (hat-tip to Joe Rogan ), .....

Enough evidence has shown that it really is just DHA that you should focus on, and by getting in the natural package described above, you're probably going to get a whole bunch of other good dietary compounds regardless of the supposed quality of the source.

Lesson learnt from Nassim Nicholas Taleb again: I'm here to win. I'm not here to win arguments.

Eat your canned fish and get healthy

...

I have personal experience with canned tuna that I might as well share. I even think solid white albacore that's canned while caught is a good choice. I've seen results in myself and others just by two cans a day. The fat from can to can varies but two cans of good stuff brings you to about 4 grams of fish fat. And when I say I've seen results I've seen kids who thought they needed to take roids eat two cans of tuna a day with rice post workout and blow up. No jokes or bs. Adults and kids who were literally plateau on chicken and rice I suggest two cans of solid white albacore and they start getting stronger and bigger at an exponential rate. This is no study just personal experience with my own two eyes.

### Haley — 2015-02-18T17:58:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-155965>

Update:

Since decreasing my protein to about a lb/day of seafood and increasing fats (avocado, coconut oil, duck fat, tallow and coconut) and adding in a few more non-starchy veggies (about 25g/day): I feel much much better! My cravings have diminished considerably, my mood is more stable and sleep is solid.

The downside being that, since I've added more avocado and coconut I'm wondering if these are the culprits behind my bloating that has come back some-since they are higher FODMAP and I had been avoiding those in large amounts before. I've been eating a combo of coconut oil, coconut flour and carob at night as a kind of dessert.  Im a little bit torn because these foods seem to improve my energy and cravings.

\--

My next step is determining whether or not I am LR (still) and whether or not i should continue the Reset. I feel pretty good overall and feel in control of my appetite for the most part. My concerns are: my eating disorder history, i eat a lower calorie diet because of my slowed BMR and other "experts" talking about low carb producing the lowest leptin levels. So I wonder if it will take longer than the 6 weeks I have been on the Leptin Reset. But based off my symptoms, I feel I may need to move on to the next step. Im hesitant to do this since it is a dramatic shift to switch high protein in AM to PM.

\--

What is behind the claim that low carb diets cause the lowest leptin levels? How does that fit in Dr.Kruse's protocols?

Any other tips to help me determine whether or not I am LS yet?

My menstrual cycle seemed to be extremely light this month (only lasted about a day). My assumption is that it is either low calorie+low carb/ketosis or the ketosis itself. Does anyone have any insight on this?

thank you all!

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-03T12:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-157261>

Sorry for the delay is response. I was travelling for the last 8 days

> [Haley said:](/goto/post?id=155965)
>
> The downside being that, since I've added more avocado and coconut I'm wondering if these are the culprits behind my bloating that has come back some-since they are higher FODMAP and I had been avoiding those in large amounts before. I've been eating a combo of coconut oil, coconut flour and carob at night as a kind of dessert.  Im a little bit torn because these foods seem to improve my energy and cravings.

I think you should keep these foods in the diet for now.

The only potentially problematic thing is the coconut flour, which can be potentially be oxidised. I don't think that it's an issue though, but if you're really concerned, then use a more coarsely-shredded dessicated coconut. The sulfur, which is used to preserve the coconut, is also likely beneficial.

> My next step is determining whether or not I am LR (still) and whether or not i should continue the Reset. I feel pretty good overall and feel in control of my appetite for the most part. My concerns are: my eating disorder history, i eat a lower calorie diet because of my slowed BMR and other "experts" talking about low carb producing the lowest leptin levels. So I wonder if it will take longer than the 6 weeks I have been on the Leptin Reset. But based off my symptoms, I feel I may need to move on to the next step. Im hesitant to do this since it is a dramatic shift to switch high protein in AM to PM.

You don't need a lot of protein in the afternoon to take advantage of the naturally high rate of protein synthesis. 25g of protein is enough, which is only about 4oz of meat (weighed raw).

So I'd personally think you can do both a 25g dose of protein in the morning a la the Leptin Reset, and then do a 25g dose of protein in the last 2 hours before sunset, to get the benefits of both.

> What is behind the claim that low carb diets cause the lowest leptin levels? How does that fit in Dr.Kruse's protocols?
>
> Any other tips to help me determine whether or not I am LS yet?

I think it's more a case that high carb intake increases Leptin **Acutely (in the short term, approx 48 hrs)** -- [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/...ez.Pubmed.Pubmed\_ResultsPanel.Pubmed\_RVDocSum](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10946866?ordinalpos=45&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum)

This effect is not seen when eating fat.

Then again, what we really want is Leptin Signalling, which at present, is not possible to practically measure using quantitative methods. Serum leptin still needs to get into the brain to do it's signalling, and how much leptin is required is going to depend on Leptin Sensitivity.

The Leptin Rx FAQ basically laid out a couple of parameters -- [http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/](http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/) , but it's basically asking you to look out for the standard parameters of health as stated in the Redox Rx -- [http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx)

Standard advice is to just focus on feeling and getting better, while maintaining the dietary pattern that is recommended above.

> My menstrual cycle seemed to be extremely light this month (only lasted about a day). My assumption is that it is either low calorie+low carb/ketosis or the ketosis itself. Does anyone have any insight on this?

This is an area which I can't really help ATM

....

### Haley — 2015-03-04T06:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-157380>

No worries at all, I hope you enjoyed your travels!

I added back in avocado and coconut butter after cutting them for a few days. And then I stopped the coconut flour and carob a couple days ago. I feel like this has helped to a degree. Im not sure yet. After some googling and reflecting I realized those two foods, being in a more unnatural form were more likely an issue. I also realized all of these foods have decent carb content and now that i am not subtracting fiber grams..i realize they can add up so I'm watching my intake..keeping it just under 50g/day of total carb.
I think i am continuing to put too much emphasis on macros but sometimes it feels like its the one thing i can relatively control.
\----
I started taking some Betaine HCL and molybdenum this weekend with each meal to help my symptoms. I can't say i've noticed anything yet. My thought is it would be nice to alleviate some of my symptoms while allowing the true healing to take place over time. But i don't want to interfere with that healing. I was wondering if taking something such as HCL would reduce my body's attempt at producing it on its own thus perpetuating the problem?
\----
In this post (was written in 2011):
**[http://jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next-2/](http://jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next-2/)**
Jack mentions eating more protein at night 25-75g), that is why I stated what I did above. I have to say I am surprised you recommend only 50g total..8 oz seems very low for total protein. Especially since Jack seems to err more on the higher side in his version of ketosis.
\-
**[http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)**
This is the first time I have seen this blog and I found several things interesting.

**"How do I test or measure my redox potential to learn if it’s good or bad?**
1\. **Loss of libido, erectile power, lubrication or small ejaculate size** are all signs of a lowered redox potential. Sex life signals of low redox potential.
_\-especially considering i am 22, yes, this describes me._

2\. **The quality of your hair, nails and skin** is a big clue where you stand on a redox potential. If they are bad, you can expect to have a [leaky gut](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-7-intro-to-your-gut-microbiome/) and “Leaky airway” leading to respiratory issues when the redox potential dips lower.
\-_i have hair that gets split ends notoriously, brittle nails i pick at, and my skin is often discolored especially on my arms (like red splotches)._

3\. [**Poor sleep**](http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-9-quantum-sleep/)
\-_this has improved dramatically! but it is still very sensitive to my diet and i have nights where i wake 2-4am._

5\. **Dreaming is usually a sign of a good redox potential**.
\- _i have been dreaming every night_

6\. **Your BUN/creatine ratios are critical to follow in these battery of tests.**
_my blood test from a year ago had 'high BUN/Creatine Ratio of 39. And when i had this test done I was in a carb-binge phase of a couple months after eating 'clean' for years. some correlation there._

7\. **Potassium levels tell you about the relationships to cellular ATP. You must pay attention to your Potassium (K+) levels**.
_how can one make sure they are getting enough potassium? specifically on the eli paleo diet?_

8\. **When LDL cholesterol goes below 200, your redox potential usually is low.**
a _year ago my LDL cholesterol was 162 which was said to be 'high'._

9\. **Chronic altered hormone panels** are another insight into your redox potential, particularly your Vitamin D status, DHEA and sex steroid levels.
\-_i know a year ago the naturopath diagnosed me with low Vitamin D.
_
10\. **Your cortisol/melatonin saliva tests are keys to assessing the redox potential.** When redox potential is altered, you can bet your Vitamin A and D cycles are off in the brain. When vitamin D is low in the gut, it opens the tight junctions in between gut enterocytes. Iodine assists Vitamin D in this action in low light seasons. **This is where leaky gut begins for most**. The tight junction between gut cells are broken easily when vitamin D levels dip below 30 ng/dl. Anyone who is struggling to get their vitamin D levels higher likely has a co-morbid iodine and magnesium deficiency due to a lack of water. Magnesium is hydrophilic, and without water, we lose magnesium.
\-_so is the indication that i should drink more water and get more sun? is there iodine and mg in water?_
\----

The reason I bring up leptin within the low carb debate is because I would like some more assurance ketosis will be the best option in the long-term. Considering it has already seemed to affect my cycle.

Stephani Ruper writes a lot about women's health, carbs and **_leptin._**

-   Maybe someone can show me what this means in context of what Jack is saying about leptin and if in fact low leptin levels are good:

"Glucose elicits an insulin response, which in turn **spikes leptin** levels in the blood. This is a short-term spike, so eating carbohydrates should not be used as a replacement for body fat, which is the primary long-term secretor of leptin. However, moderate, regular consumption of carbohydrate spikes leptin frequently enough to help signal to the hypothalamus that the body is being fed. Recall that leptin is absolutely crucial for reproductive function. Without leptin, the hypothalamus does not tell the pituitary to produce sex hormones. At all."
**\-http://paleoforwomen.com/carbohydrates-for-fertility-and-health/**

"Another role that carbohydrates play is spiking leptin levels. Whenever insulin spikes in response to blood glucose, leptin levels rise, too. This means that carbohdyrates help signal to the hypothalamus that the woman is fed. However, this is a short-term elevation. It only spikes in bursts and with meals, so it cannot be used as a long-term solution to health. **It is important to note, however, that a high fat, low carbohydrate diet is consistently associated with the lowest leptin levels possible."
\-http://paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/**

### Haley — 2015-07-10T06:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-171533>

Hey guys!

I wanted to give an update since its been a while and I have some new findings.

I decided to go ahead and meet with someone who specializes in digestive issues and get some labs done. I did the Diagnostech's stool test and the results showed that I have Aeromonas Veronii (in the same family as salmonella), virtually no good bacteria in my gut, blood in my stool and a highly inflamed gut. Candida/hpylori etc was all negative. I believe these findings explain why I have diarrhea every morning, bloating issues, weight loss resistance, fatigue etc. I also think it explains why I am not able to tolerate foods other than meat avocados and coconuts. I have no good guys in there! I have of course learned much about environment and its impact on gut health from this blog and forum and believe I have excelled in great lengths at altering my environment for the better. I'm excited to have found what is going on with my gut so I can stop self diagnosing, doing the wrong protocols and beating myself up for not losing more weight or improving other symptoms. I have been prescribed the following protocol:

FC Cidal & Dysbiocide (for 30 days to wipe out the pathogenic bacteria)
MegasporeBiotic (to reinoculate the good guys)
Endozin (zinc supplement with lglutamine)

I was happy to see her not recommend a crazy amount of supplements and be so in-tune with environmental factors as well as ketogenic diets. Hoping these additions along with continued sunlight, DHA and avoidance of EMFs will make a difference in my health!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-10T13:44:36+0300

### Haley — 2015-09-07T03:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-176646>

I haven't included any of those for a very long time. They all seem to contribute to my bloating (as does almost everything else) and are controversial for SIBO (which i suspect but don't know if i have). I am wondering though if i should push though the discomfort since i am already experiencing a lot of bloating lately.

It truly is a struggle for me to know what to eat with my dysbiosis situation. First and foremost I am focusing on my environment and I do get A LOT of sun. I really always have. As you could tell from my very tan skin. So my thought is it isn't a lack of sunlight but something that is keeping me from absorbing its benefits. My gut issues started after I developed an eating disorder 8 years ago. I began extremely restrictive diets with bouts of binging and I strongly feel this was a huge contributor-not disregarding my environment but the correlation here is strong.

The last three months have been tough for me. I have continues taking the Megaspore and finished the herbs. The shifts in my gut (i think) urged me to crave other foods and I fell off the wagon a bit and have eaten things I know I shouldn't (nuts, chocolate, sugar, chips, etc). If I stay away from these kinds of foods for a couple days my cravings with generally go away but it has been hard for me lately. Whereas before this gut protocol I had no cravings. Either way I am bloated and not loosing any weight. Otherwise I feel great. But these symptoms are enough to affect my wellbeing 100% of the time.

Although I understand the environment is core-I would like some help with my diet considering all of you still pay special attention to it. These days my diet looks like:

8oz fish, kale, coconut oil, avocado, mixed greens, pink salt
6oz salmon, avocado
coconut butter
8oz fish or oysters (1x/wk), kale, coconut oil, avocado, mixed greens, pink salt
coconut butter
(and here is when I may give in to a spoonful of peanut butter or blue corn chips-lately)

Thank you guys. I have really been trying to learn other common "to-do's" on the book page but need a little personal help

### Haley — 2015-10-21T17:02:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-180073>

Hi guys, I didn't receive any feedback from my last post but I'm going to go ahead and keep updating my journal. Maybe some advice will come my way at the right time.

I started the protocols learned here last January and at times I feel better but in some regards I have had some set backs. When I began I cut out all sweet potatoes and rice and kale and avocados became my carbs. My diet became very limited and I think this contributed to the dysbiosis because I was taking in virtually no good bacteria AND I didn't adopt the advice of getting in the sun. It was winter time here and with work I found that difficult. But Since about May I have been in the sun hours every day. I work in a restaurant so i move all day long, however, i have mornings and 3 days off a week where i spend almost every minute outside. I have a lot of NEAT activity, HITT 4x a week (10 minutes each) and I do not eat too much so i know my extra weight is not a calorie in/out issue-as we all know. But it is powerful to truly believe that.

What my day looks like:
wake up around 7am (gets light then)
walk outside barefoot on wet grass and look up at the sky for a minute
BAB: teaspoon colostrum, 8oz fish, kale, coconut oil, 1/2 avocado, spring mix, fresh aloe vera
wear blue blockers inside for about an hour
once sun hits my backyard I go outside for about 1-3 hrs and journal etc
10 minute weights (4x/week)
shower-recently been taking cool showers
WORK OR IF IM OFF-ACTIVITIES OUTSIDE (horse, hike, dog, online school etc)
1pm (ish): eat about 4 oz protein and some fat like avocado or coconut butter
7pm- start wearing blue blockers & using flux on computer
dinner: 8oz fish/oysters (1x/wk), a bit of cooked cabbage, dab coconut oil, spring mix,tablespoon fermented cabbage, 1-2 japanese sweet potatoes \*
then maybe a T or 2 of peanut butter if i feel i didn't eat enough calories
\* Within the last couple weeks I added in more veggies like cabbage/broccoli and some sweet potatoes because i realized I was still eating mostly fats through the summer, but i do realize now that its fall i should start cutting some, unsure how much and how to do this???
10-11pm-take MG citrate and malate, Megaspore
fall asleep with all devices wifi turned off

my biggest struggle: i feel inflamed, i look inflamed. my bloating has subsided on most days but I feel the weight is inflammation. I wonder if part of it is because my gut is getting used to new foods, etc...

I think I am doing everything I know to do except hard core CT. Im wondering if letting myself get cold more like i did last winter will help again. my inflammation went way down last winter. i am trying to take cold showers but i psychologically am having a very difficult time with submerging myself in ice cold baths.....

### Haley — 2015-10-22T18:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haleys-journal.12626/post-180151>

OK-after reading CT 7 I am thinking that perhaps I am doing many of the right things, just not at the right time. I have formed a couple questions, however.

I feel I may be the one of the 'young paleo' types he mentions in his blog who is suffering with these aging issues early on. In the blog Jack says **" The normal large circadian prolactin surge we should see at around midnight, after leptin enters the brain, does not happen if the patient has leptin resistance, sleep apnea, or has eaten food too close (within 3-4 hours) to bedtime."** I have a terrible habit of eating before I go to sleep. Mostly it is a comfort habit I developed long ago when I first developed an eating disorder. I have recently adopted eating a sweet potato or two as well which is even worse.

1) So, say I try and fall asleep at 10, when should be the last time I eat anything? 6? (just making sure he isn't referring to midnight when other hormonal functions are taking place)
2)I am not sure if I am cold or warm adapted, just coming out of summer and now nights are getting in the 30's here (no heat turned on), are the recommendations vastly different? Is it possible to become cold adapted without soaking in cold baths if you live in a cold environment?
3) Adding back in a sweet potato or two has drastically helped me to control my appetite and eat less. I would like to begin to enter back into ketosis for fall and winter, should I do this if i _am_ warm adapted? Can I keep a sweet potato as a once a day thing? When would be the best time to eat it or other carbs since night time is not recommended?
4) I tend to go look up at the sun right when I wake up but then go back an hour or two later when the sun hits my backyard to get my real dose for a couple hours if i can. Is this inefficient?
5) Could timing be my issue here? Any other ideas you have for me, that I could change to find optimal?

Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T21:48:26+0300

Doing the right things at the wrong time = doing shit wrong.

# Thread: Google glasses contact lenses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-glasses-contact-lenses.12627/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-16T02:25:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-glasses-contact-lenses.12627/post-152936>

[http://googleblog.blogspot.com/2014/01/introducing-our-smart-contact-lens.html](http://googleblog.blogspot.com/2014/01/introducing-our-smart-contact-lens.html)

Here is my comment buried in the 4 thousand others.

Horrible idea. As a neurosurgeon who knows a lot about non native EMF exposure in and around the eye and retina you need this info: you need to Read Cross Currents by Becker. Pay attn to page 191-193 when he talks about what the military found with 27 MHz rdio signals and cataracts. This is way below the microwave range that these lenses with use. You might be shocked. The results were published in Nature by JH Heller A. A. Teixeira-Pinto of the New Englnad Research Institute. Ten yrs later Dr. David E Janes and his collegues at the FDA repeated the experiments and got the same results at non thermal levels. Dr. Robert Birge was the first to show that low level EMF's directly alter the molecular structure of the photochemicals of the retina.......there is no way there wont be serious damage to people who wear these lenses. The data has been done and buried........but Becker published it all.

Moreover.....read Chapter 14 of Andrew Marino's book named Going Somewhere. The chapter is named Zaret pgs 160-169. Milton Zaret was the opthamologist the Navy's Paul Tyler hired to check the cataract effects and they ruined his life. Books have been written about it. There is a review in the NY Times about it. DOD and CIA buried his work. He lived in Scarsdale, NY and was an EMF expert on the eyes. These lenses are a bad idea.

Technology is a good servant but a horrible master. Because of technology, people feel distracted – they can’t focus, that they can’t pay attention to what’s in front of them because their minds keep jumping around. They aren’t getting their work done; they’re not paying attention to their kids and life in general. They can’t escape from the office, they have a "cubicle in their pocket" that demands their attention 24/7. We can’t relax; we can’t disconnect without feeling anxious. Ten years ago, co-workers didn’t call me on the weekends, so why are we emailing back and forth at 10:00 pm on Saturday nights now? Today, realize the Internet is both a lifeline and the plastic bag over our heads. We all need to change that in our lives.

Today, realize the Internet is both a lifeline and the plastic bag over our heads. We all need to change that in our lives.

^^^^^^^THAT

There are layers of meaning in this video...........opening the door to one of the more complex part of the human species in how they "communicate" across species to get circadian signaling correct without being "dazed and confused".

Scientific assumptions are the termites of symbiotic relationships. In fact, every human creature is constituted to be a profound secret to one another and mystery to every other species they communicate with because that mode of cross talk is layered. The single biggest problem in biologic communication is the quantum illusion that it has taken place and you might decipher it without pondering how it occurs. When the coherence account is high in tissues, communication is easy, instant, and effective. This implies the whole war in neolithic diseases is because we can't talk to each other properly. Sometimes the most important thing in communication is visualizing what isn't being signaled. The art of reading between the lines of 'light' is a life long quest of the wise, coded for in the wisdom of nature.

The earth speaks, clearly, distinctly, and, in many quantum layers of nature, loudly, to mankind, but _he fails to hear a single sound_. This is his communication breakdown.

this blog definitely broke into the million pieces everything I ever learnt studying biology on University. The very stable basic pilars. ### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-16T22:38:59+0200

A thought that came to mind while I'm reading OSF1

People afflicted with Candida tend to do better with a calorie-restricted high-fat diet (both factors boosts FIAF expression), and from my reading, don't do well with pro-biotics. I suspect this is because candida destroys the ability to produce Butyrate in the gut.

This was the "more conventional" explanation for the damage mechanism of Candida from Jack given in October 2014 (4 months ago) -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/candida-fest-help.11560/#post-145587](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/candida-fest-help.11560/#post-145587)

Candida is a Eukaryote, and I think the fundamental damage mechanism of candida is that it absorbs the light that our GALT cells are supposed to absorb. The two most important wavelengths should be the 380nm UV band, as well as specific bands in IR light (I'm thinking 800nm and 970nm).

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-16T23:18:30+0200

From most of the studies it appears the 3rd trimester might be "the time"...

Again, quick thoughts before I head to the beach (7+am here, need sunshine to fully wake up ), so probably not 100% coherent.

Quote: **The only two things that can contain light is strong electric and magnetic fields.** So I think that fundamentally the way to recover the gut is actually to first focus on fixing the redox potential of the GALT tissues, thereby giving them the ability to generate a stronger magnetic field in order to "out-compete" any other entities like Candida in terms of light absorption.

Using the example of Candida again, once it cannot compete with the GALT, it will die out since it cannot get energy.

I suspect that specific dietary mechanisms directly targeted through the GI tract can help improve the redox potential of the GALT.

-   Obviously, you don't need to be told that DHA consumption is important , and I suspect that the GALT is more capable of absorbed consumed DHA than Candida is (though GALT absorption is probably decreased)
-   I think the C8 and C6 MCTs could potentially help address the GALT directly

I think that it is possible to get some of the benefits of intermittent fasting without fasting for too long, but this generally means eating very little protein and carbs, and basically doing only fat.

Example: 12hour eating window from 7am to 7pm, first meal is almost only fat, second meal is at 12pm, and contains only a little bit of protein (like 20-25g) and minimal carbs, and then last meal is where most of the protein and carb load will go. (**NOTE**: this assumes that you're not Leptin Resistant)

That should create a temporary "fasting signal" in which fatty acid utilisation is very high at least for most of the morning hours.

The GALT can of course be healed indirectly through systemic energy addition to the body. Any means of adding energy works here -- grounding, sunshine, red lasers, Myer's Cocktails, etc ....

No idea how your current stage of pregnancy changes all of this. I need to sort out my thoughts about what sort of sensing and information transfer between mother and fetus is most important and get back to you.

It makes it again difficult to hope we will understand life in it's complexity anytime soon...we are yet to understand the very basics of life and it requires to start from scratch, to turn the bilogy almost into tabula rasa. I am indefinitely glad to have the opportunity to live through this very exciting times! There may be very different medicine waiting for the generation of our children already.
> I am particulary intrigued by the extraordinary capacity of specific wave lenghts to produce very different effects in life depending on context. However I struggle to absorb enough physics and matematics knowledge to understand it fully, it is a "scandalous concept" considering how much we manipulate light in our lifes. The small comment on cancer and it's relation to lower cholesterol levels is something one could be put in jail for ;-) I wonder how individual genetics plays a role in this mechanism considering individual/familiar genetic background predisposing to naturally lower cholesterol levels despite of good vit.D, vit.A and enough available sulphur in the system.

I think this whole frequency/biology area is really exciting. This sort of biotech was weaponised long ago but the prospect of using it to our benefit in terms of treatment and prevention instead is great.

I'm imagining using interference patterns to create localised standing waves at specific frequencies within a body to treat with light/vibration etc. - without needing, or lessening the need for, open surgery or pharmaceuticals.

Maybe such equipment as used in MRI for diagnosis can also be used for treatment (thinking of the case Dr. K. mentioned using magnetism for encephalisation) in new and innovative ways.

The mentioned effects of carcinogens changing light frequencies could possibly open up a field of technology for converting harmful radiation into beneficial forms.

For wireless communications technology - perhaps a change to Schumann harmonic frequencies or other beneficial frequencies and amplitude or other forms of modulation rather than frequency modulation could turn the tide.

In terms of food and water . . . . maybe it would be possible with technologies to match the energies of food electrons to enhance coherence per person and location rather than causing de-coherence.

I'm not suggesting we replace nature's ways with technology - rather that we enhance and amplify it where we can make adjustment to current technology and keep biocompatibility in mind with future tech.

How about a mono-polar magnetico? Or an electromagnetic mattress with flux created in pulsatile manner at biologically beneficial frequencies? Or a home that uses electricity at beneficial frequencies - a converter that takes the incoming power and changes the frequency to a beneficial one followed by gadgets that will run at that Hz and hence turn all your house electrics into a Schumann resonator?. All just following on from @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) standing on a magnet.

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=153096)

Or a home that uses electricity at beneficial frequencies - a converter that takes the incoming power and changes the frequency to a beneficial one followed by gadgets that will run at that Hz and hence turn all your house electrics into a Schumann resonator?. All just following on from @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) standing on a magnet.

I'm going to just post some speculative bullshit now

\----

I personally don't think that wireless communication can use the Schumann ranges that well without disrupting biology.

Firstly, I wonder how it would affect biology if organisms had to compete with interference from transmitting devices.

It also works the other way round I'd imagine -- that emittance from biological and geological sources with cause horrible signal-to-noise ratios for our technology.

\----

I think that we can make our receiving devices better at receiving weak EMF signals, thereby allowing us to use less power and have less nn-EMF pollution.

This is a limited area of optimisation in my mind, since signal amplification doesn't work at the receiving end to improve signal-to-noise ratio. (you amplify the noise as much as the signal).

Antenna directionality works to pick up signal from "quiet zones" .... if such zones exist .... and I believe we're going to see more wide-area-blast-and-conquer methodologies when it comes to feasibly getting internet connectivity to the world (works best for getting connectivity to a remote area).

In cities though, there is the possibility of having many low-powered "base stations". One of my personal software aspirations is to work on routing technologies that enable content-centric distributed designs that allow for what Pieter Hintjens calls the "EdgeNet", whereby a "base station" capable of routing internet traffic could be your very computer itself. Every internet router could become a base station.

In that model, it is not exactly known if many small transmitting devices is more or less harmful than fewer large cell towers. I think the level of harm will depend on the mitigation strategies that one uses.

Personally, I want the "many small base stations" model, because then I can put my own base station outside somewhere in the garden, and then shield the crap out of it. That is as opposed to some crazy big tower 1km away from me inundating the entire area with lots of EMF.

\----

I think the main issue is always the intensity of transmitting sources, and given the wide-area nature of todays transmitting devices, I only see more power (frequency) increase until a "beam-forming" transmitter comes into play.

If we have to use nn-EMF, then directionality of the transmitted EMF wave is always the goal -- you get it to only the places that you want it to go. The more coherent (as opposed to diffuse) we can make a beam of information, the more "EMF quiet zones" we can enforce. The technology required to do this in an air medium is basically science fiction for now , and I do not have expertise in that field to say how far we're off.

\---

Shielding technology is where I'm mostly hopeful for. You can take an Amethyst crystal and easily shield yourself from EMF today.

I think one day it will be possible to produce materials that have the property of absorbing incoming radiation and dropping the power (to something like IR) or mechanical (vibrational) energy. I think it is likely that we can make those shields entirely out of carbon, which will be cheap enough to afford comprehensive shielding without disrupting sensing of the earth's natural magnetic signals.

\----

Finally, I want a k-laser now!  [http://www.k-laserusa.com/](http://www.k-laserusa.com/)

....

When we get to these kinds of equations just about ANYTHING can be 'proven' (or disproven). Heidegger a German philosopher writing around the same time as Heisenberg, Schrodinger etc said the only important philosophical question was "Why is there something rather than nothing" I found it hard to agree with that if only because the way the question is framed makes it impossible to answer. The way I think of it is it is impossible to really 'think' about these kinds of issues. Even the attempt to think about it shows and assumes there is something there. "Equations" make these kinds of speculations 'respectable' but it is still just speculations and the equations seem more like window dressing. For that reason I think a lot of physicists are really 'philosophers' with equations.................

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-17T11:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153122>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=153109)
>
> how large does the amethyst need to be?...this is my interest too...

I actually don't know, but I suspect that a thin and wide layer is what we want.

I'm not 100% certain of the mechanism, but I think that certain crystals can respond to irradiation of nn-EMF by generating their own "repellent magnetic field", making them effectively diamagnetic.

Sidenote: I personally think that diamagnetic charge-separated water can repel nn-EMF as well, literally forming a water-shell around mitochondria and other important structures.​

Of the crystals, the pattern that I found is that the crystal:

-   Needs to have a Trigonal Crystal System -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trigonal\_crystal\_system](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trigonal_crystal_system)
-   Needs to be doped with Fe3+

The crystals that fulfil these criteria are:

-   Amethyst (which is basically iron-containing Quartz) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amethyst](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amethyst)

-   Iron-containing Tourmaline (usually black and brown) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourmaline](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourmaline)
-   I've seen references to Onyx (but not 100% sure) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onyx](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onyx)

If what I said above is true, then the crystal should emit a "protective magnetic bubble" in all directions that repels nn-EMF within that bubble.

I think the following will increase the radius of the extended magnetic bubble:

-   More Iron and/or Manganese in the crystal
-   Stronger native magnetic field => I think the crystal can absorb and amplify magnetoelastic waves from the earth (this is apparently part of the mechanism behind how the Greenfield encoder I have on my wrist works). An experiment will be to see if the buble is extended by having a Schumann generator nearby.

This can easily be tested, by having:

-   a transmitting WiFi router
-   a variety of Amethyst Crystals with varying levels of iron => in general, if you control for EMF irradiation, and get all the crystals from the same source, the Iron content is generally proportional to how dark the Purple Hue of the crystal is.
-   a radio frequency Trimeter
-   a Schumann generator

Put the WiFi router and Crystal some distance apart, then just modify the variables I talked about above, and measure the EMF field strength while standing next to the Amethyst, while progressively walking closer to the WiFi router and checking the field strength as you get closer and closer.

Then of course, you can just do the simple test of the difference in EMF field strength between putting an Amethyst Crystal between you and your WiFi router vs it not being there.

Unfortunately, I left my small Amethyst Crystal in Melbourne , but this sounds like a cool experiment to do once I manage to control for some of those variables. Now that you mention it, I'll probably go and pick up another amethyst crystal and check for any effects.

Ha, when I make enough cash. The damn thing costs something like $20k . But it's the most direct way to add energy to my poor collagen tissues, and red lasers are one of those top 5 biohacking gadgets that I'm going to buy.

### Josh — 2015-01-17T18:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153155>

[https://microbewiki.kenyon.edu/index.php/Bioluminescent\_Bacteria\_in\_Human\_Settings](https://microbewiki.kenyon.edu/index.php/Bioluminescent_Bacteria_in_Human_Settings)

> Bioluminescence is the production of visible light through a biochemically-mediated reaction, most commonly occurring in the marine organisms. Organisms use this visible light as an effective way of communication with other organisms (4). Light production has co-evolved with a wide range of organisms, especially those in bottom dwelling habitats, and it’s very important for their functioning, including defense against predators, hunting, and attracting a mate (4) (2). Bioluminescent bacteria are all [Gram-negative](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gram_negative), non-sporeforming and motile (6). One example of the benefits of producing light is found in the [zooplankton](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zooplankton) by researchers of the Hebrew University of Jerusalem; Zooplankton is attracted toward the luminous marine bacteria and ingests the bacteria, but it can’t digest the bacteria, so the bacteria stay in the zooplankton’s gut and produce light. This visible light also helps the zooplankton to attract their own predators (fish) to ingest them. Zooplankton can access to the nutrients in the fish gut and it’s very important for their survival in the deep and dark ocean (2). Based on the Annual Review of Marine, this phenomenon has evolved at least 40 times among existing organisms. In bacteria it has been suggested that the light-emitting reaction came from as a detoxification mechanism to remove the oxygen and also other possibility is that luciferase function like a “secondary respiratory chain” (6). By now scientists classified eleven species of bioluminescent bacterial into four genuses: Vibrio, Photobacterium, Alteromonas, and Photorhabdus (5).

[http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783)

> symbiont colonizes the tissues, how is it maintained in balance throughout the life of the host? Two early studies demonstrated that the squid-vibrio system is on a daily rhythm. The per-cell luminescence of the bacteria is brightest in the evening, when the squid is out foraging[\[36\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Boettcher1), and ~90% of the symbiont population is expelled from the light organ each day at dawn[\[37\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Graf1). When the technology became available, we explored the host and symbiont transcriptome over the day-night cycle to discover the dialogue between the partners underlying these daily rhythms [\[38\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Wier2). The resulting data led to the finding that the host crypt epithelium undergoes dramatic remodeling over the day-night cycle. These diel changes in host tissues appear to reflect variations in the food source provided to the bacterial symbionts. In response, bacterial gene expression vacillates from anaerobic respiration to anaerobic fermentation, a switch that would dramatically alter the pH environment of the crypts. These data demonstrate that maintenance of the squid-vibrio symbiosis is a dynamic process with a profound daily rhythm.
>
> [\[39\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-HeathHeckman1). We identified two different Cry isoforms in the host. The gene encoding one cycles in the head with a pattern similar to that described in other animals, where the response is to diel environmental light cues; in contrast, the other _cry_gene has higher expression in the light organ and responds to the cycling of symbiont luminescence. The cycling of the light organ _cry_ gene requires the presence of the symbiont, and the symbiont must be luminous, i.e., Δlux strains do not induce cycling of the light-organ _cry_gene. Further, similar to development, to obtain normal _cry_ cycling in the light organ requires not only the presence of the bacteria, but also their presentation of MAMPs, which appear to synergize with symbiont luminescence.

### Josh — 2015-01-17T18:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153156>

[http://www.photobiology.info/Lin.html](http://www.photobiology.info/Lin.html)

**"Figure 23.** The confined environment, in which the produced autoinducers can accumulate, is the key to the activation of the quorum sensing mechanism.
It is known that marine luminescent bacteria that live free in the ocean do not emit light, **_while bacteria living as symbionts in marine organisms and/or in a localized, confined environment emit a high level of light._** Therefore, in order for the light emission to occur, luminous bacteria have to grow in a confined, nutrition-rich environment, in which the autoinducer can be accumulated. When the concentration of the autoinducer increases to a certain level in the extracellular environment, the autoinducers will activate the luminous system of luminous bacteria. This process has been called "quorum sensing", as the bacteria literally sense when a sufficient concentration of bacteria has been reached based on the accumulation of autoinducer (Figure 23). "

### Josh — 2015-01-17T23:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153192>

I am thinking that the significance of HSP's and their binding to TLR4 have a place here?

> **Levee 27:**
>
> _**Heat shock proteins**_ (HSP). HSPs are proteins that are only expressed when the cell is faced with higher temperatures or other cellular stress. It appears that heat shock proteins’ origins come from direct transcriptional processes once a stressor is encountered. Most heat shock proteins are named by their weight, but the more important ones have names. One such example is ubiquitin, which is a HSP that marks proteins for degradation. Most HSP can be thought of as cellular pathway chaperones that help the cell do the business of homeostasis more effectively. They are very active in infection, trauma, burns, inflammation, exercise, hypoxia, starvation, nitrogen depletion, alcohol use, UV radiation, and heavy metal poisoning.
> The interesting thing about these proteins is that they seem to affect tertiary and quaternary structural folding of proteins in our bodies. This effect seems to be lost as we age so we see a defect in protein folding in aging related diseases. They also are critical in transporting proteins across cell membranes in all tissues. HSP are also vital in maintenance of steroid receptor function. Folding issues are thought to play major roles in Mad Cow disease, Alzheimer’s disease and Parkinson’s disease.

[http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/#HeatShock](http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/#HeatShock)

Toll-like receptors in Multiple Sclerosis. Ligation of TLR2 and TLR4 induces the production of IL1, IL6 and IL12, which induce the differentiation of naïve T cells into Th1 and Th17 cells. Th17 and Th1 cells secrete IL17 and INFγ respectively.**...

**

> TLR4 is expressed on monocytes and macrophages, myeloid DC and T and B lymphocytes, as well as intestinal epithelium. It can bind LPS from Gram-negative bacteria, bacterial and endogenous HSP, as well as the endogenous ligands HMGB1, fibrinogen, heparan sulphate and hyaluronic acid.
>
> TLR4 expression has been identified in cerebral endothelial cells \[[28](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b28)\] and microglia by RT-PCR \[[25](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b25)\]. Both TLR4 and its endogenous ligand HMGB1 are increased in expression in the CSF mononuclear cells of MS patients compared to healthy controls \[[33](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b33)\]. Association studies of functional (missense) mutations in _TLR4_(Asp299Gly and Thr399Ile) failed to identify any association with MS \[[42](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b42),[43](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b43)\]. A subsequent study of nine_TLR4_ single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNP) tested for association with MS in 362 MS patients and 467 healthy controls also failed to identify any significantly associated loci \[[44](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b44)\].

**

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/)**

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-18T00:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153198>

> [David Limacher said:](/goto/post?id=153178)
>
> Could it be possibly to take crushed amethyst crytal, add it to paint and paint windowless rooms with the amethyst infused paint?

My hunch is that this will work , so long as the paint medium doesn't disrupt the function of the amethyst.

I suspect that if you have a paint medium which can convert incoming energy (EMF) into mechanical energy (like vibration), then you could even take advantage of the natural piezoelectric nature of amethyst to generate a larger repellent magnetic field. I have no idea if the EMF->Vibration conversion is possible BTW.

Chalcedony ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chalcedony](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chalcedony)) and Jasper ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasper](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasper)) are basically rocks that are riddled with micro amethyst crystals. "micro" here is used literally, in that the embedded amethyst crystals are measured on a micro-meter scale.

The historical traditions value those rocks as having "unique energetic properties" as well, and probably they should be tested to see if the effect is the same as using a pure Amethyst.

### PaulG — 2015-02-07T03:20:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-154948>

**Are dark photons behind biophotons? by Matti Pitkanen**
[http://journals.sfu.ca/jnonlocality/public/journals/1/PREPRINTS/PitkanenII-105122013.pdf](http://journals.sfu.ca/jnonlocality/public/journals/1/PREPRINTS/PitkanenII-105122013.pdf)

> One can raise a long list of questions about biophotons.
> 1\. **What is the actual nature of biophotons?** If biophotons are not primary entities (contrary to what is usually believed), what is behind biophotons? Could biophotons be decay products of something more fundamental and perhaps new from the perspective of recent day physics? If biophotons are fundamental entities responsible for control and communication, the extreme weakness of their intensity becomes a problem. The idea about biophotons as leakage of more fundamental entitities could allow even metabolic role for the these entities.
> 2\. **How biophotons are produced?** The proposed mechanism based on oxidative stress does not explain coherence nor the reported communication and control function.
> 3\. **How do biophotons or more fundamental entities relate to biology** in general and specifically to metabolism, to neuroscience, to certain findings of Gariaev’s group \[I10\], to Becker’s DC currents \[J5\] and the related TGD inspired model discussed in \[L1\]? **Could biophotons relate to vision and imagination**? Bischof \[J6\] and Bokkon et al \[J27, J13, J8, J14, J7\] have made several proposals in this respect. **What is behind the correlation between EEG and biophotons** (for which multiple sources of evidence exist - \[J4, J19, J16\] - or that between the changes in biophoton emission in meditative \[J9\] and qigong practices \[J12, J21, J23, J22\]? What about remote mental interactions, which are natural and ubiquitous if TGD-inspired biology is accepted: **could biophotons or more fundamental entities provide a control and communication tool**?

Of course, the sleeping underwater thing was said tongue-in-cheek, but I do think it is plausible in the future to alternate being "as asleep as possible" and "as awake as possible". Being submerged in temperature-controlled water, surrounded by weak IR lasers, and a strong magnetic field, is probably a good combination to get as good a sleep state as possible. 6 hours of that a night would be incredibly restorative.

Sidenote: I'm not looking at particularly cold water per se. Cooling of the body occurs on the order of 1-2 degrees Celsius during sleep, and major cooling will definitely disrupt functions. I do however think that it would be possible to say safely drop body temp by up to 4-5C across a night of sleep, while using strong magnetic fields to induce a deep sleep state, and come out of the process with much better energetic and signalling function.

We're talking about sleeping in 27-30C water here, not some cold tub. Cooling down of core temperature will have to happen mostly via internal mechanisms, and not some external cold stimulus. ie: Melatonin leading to massive uncoupling. Being surrounded with water is still valuable for doing all sorts of things though -- temperature regulation, solutes like epsom salts or H2O2, etc ....

I did some small subjective experiments and it feels like if I carry them together with switched on cell phone it makes me nauseous ;-(

I haven't had much time to test

But one thing I can say for sure is that Amethyst in particular is likely diamagnetic in some form, and excludes EMF away from itself.

This is a problem, because the way that the nn-EMF waves are going to be distorted by the crystal will depend on very many factors which I have no clue about how to control right now. With my relatively cylindrical crystal, it seems to increase the power per unit area of EMF waves in a direct straight line from the crystal to the emission source.

I can't make a good recommendation, except to actually take a meter and test the effects. This is hard to do, so personally I would recommend that if people want to try out the addition of Amethyst crystals, they should create a context whereby they can determine the effects of before-and-after use of the crystal.

You do this like kovita does, by having a qualitative measure of redox potential to guage the effects of amethyst crystal introduction. In kovita's case, it's pretty obvious that having amethyst near her did not do her any good at all. Discontinue use if this is the case

I have no clue if these effects generalise to black tourmaline. Need to get a big crystal and test ....

....

Yew, excellent scientifical work as always ;-) I can definitely confirm that all attempts I made to use amethyst did fail for me. I have no meter, but my personal built-in meter in my head told me it hurts, not protect. The effect of the water brought to my mind a memory of a documentary movie abput the best czech gymnasts of all times, Vera Caslavska. She was the female hero of the 1968 Mexico OLympic Games. She is still very fit and preserves remarkably sharp brain. She said in the documentary her secret recipe is to fill her bathtub woth warm water each evening at 8pm, get in and fall asleep. She than wakes up after midnight in a room temperature water and moves to her bed. I always wondered what is the "magic" of this recipe, but after the last blogs I started to suspect the hydrostatic pressure and now I see there is another point to consider.
It makes me thing as well about the fetus enclosed inside of the maternal body surrounded by wateralike liquid. Should be very reasonable emf protection, but than there is growing evidence that the fetus could be badly affected by the high nnEMF. So is it the maternal tissue somehow translating the high nnEMF into damage or is the fetus so sensitive that even such a powerfull filter as combination of water and flesh is not enough and the damaging effect happens directly? If the effect should be direct than it is reasonable to think the evolution did develope protection which is not reasonable for nowadays environment.

ugh
